korean: 지금 일이 너무 많아서 정신이 없어
english: I'm completely swamped right now.
alt: I'm buried in work at the moment.
note: swamped/buried 둘 다 busy보다 강한 표현

korean: 갑자기 회의라니 미리 좀 말해줬어야지
english: You should've given me a heads up about the meeting.
alt: A little warning would've been nice.
note: heads up = 사전 경고/알림

korean: 회의 몇 시더라? 캘린더에 안 보이는데
english: When's the meeting? I don't see it on my calendar.
alt: What time is the meeting? I can't find it on my schedule.
note: schedule과 calendar 둘 다 자연스러움

korean: 그 보고서 내가 처리할게 걱정 마
english: Don't worry about the report. I'll take care of it.
alt: I'm on it. Leave the report to me.
note: I'm on it = 내가 하고 있어/할게 (더 캐주얼)

korean: 어제 보낸 이메일 확인했어요?
english: Did you get my email about the budget from yesterday?
alt: Have you had a chance to look at my email?
note: Have you had a chance to~? = 혹시 ~할 시간 있었어?

korean: 오늘 재택이에요 9시부터 온라인이요
english: I'm working from home today. I'll be online by 9.
alt: I'm WFH today. I'll be reachable from 9.
note: WFH = Work From Home. 메신저에서 많이 씀

korean: 오늘은 이만 퇴근하겠습니다 내일 봬요
english: I'm heading out for the day. See you tomorrow.
alt: I'm gonna call it a day. See you in the morning.
note: call it a day = 오늘은 여기까지

korean: 그건 제 담당이 아니라서 마케팅팀에 확인해보세요
english: That's not really my area. You should check with the marketing team.
alt: I'm not the right person for that. Try reaching out to marketing.
note: reach out to = ~에게 연락하다

korean: 다음 주에 미팅 하나 잡자
english: Let's schedule something for next week.
alt: Can we set up a meeting sometime next week?
note: set up a meeting / schedule a call

korean: 지금 통화 괜찮아? 잠깐 얘기하고 싶은 게 있어
english: Is this a good time to talk? I need to run something by you.
alt: Got a minute? I want to bounce something off you.
note: run something by you / bounce off you = 의견 구하다

korean: 이거 급한 건가요 아니면 월요일까지 기다려도 돼요?
english: Is this time-sensitive or can it wait until Monday?
alt: How urgent is this? Can it hold till Monday?
note: time-sensitive = 시간이 중요한. hold = 기다리다

korean: 프로젝트 진행 어떻게 되어가고 있어요?
english: How's the project coming along?
alt: What's the latest on the project?
note: What's the latest? = 최신 상황이 뭐야?

korean: 전부 다 읽진 못했는데 대충 핵심은 파악했어
english: I didn't read the whole thing but I get the gist of it.
alt: I skimmed through it. I got the main idea.
note: gist = 요지 / skim = 훑어보다

korean: 잠깐 나갔다 올게요 전화 받아야 해서
english: Do you mind if I step out for a sec? I need to take this call.
alt: Sorry let me grab this real quick. I'll be right back.
note: step out = 잠깐 나가다

korean: 몸이 안 좋아서 오늘 하루 쉬려고요
english: I'm not feeling well so I'm gonna take the day off.
alt: I'm feeling sick. I'm going to call in today.
note: call in sick = 아프다고 전화해서 쉬다

korean: 그래서 결론이 뭔데? 본론으로 들어가자
english: So what's the bottom line? Let's cut to the chase.
alt: Can you get to the point? What's the takeaway?
note: cut to the chase = 본론으로 들어가다

korean: 몸이 안 좋아서 오늘 좀 일찍 가도 될까요?
english: I'm not feeling well. Would it be okay if I left early?
alt: I'm feeling under the weather. Mind if I duck out early?
note: duck out = 슬쩍 빠지다

korean: 내일 연차 쓸 거예요 월요일에 복귀합니다
english: I'm taking PTO tomorrow. I'll be back Monday.
alt: I'm off tomorrow. See you Monday.
note: PTO = Paid Time Off / day off도 가능

korean: 오늘 야근해야 할 것 같아 먼저 먹고 있어
english: I think I'm gonna have to work late tonight. Go ahead and eat without me.
alt: I'm stuck at the office tonight. Don't wait up for me.
note: don't wait up = 기다리지 마 (보통 밤에)

korean: 이거 마감이 언제야? 급한 거야?
english: When's the deadline for this? Is it urgent?
alt: When is this due? How soon do you need it?
note: due = 마감인 / How soon do you need it? = 얼마나 빨리?

korean: 그거 내가 먼저 하고 있었는데 한번 맞춰보자
english: I was actually working on that first. Can we sync up?
alt: I already started on that. Let's coordinate so we don't overlap.
note: sync up / coordinate = 조율하다 / overlap = 겹치다

korean: 급한 일이 생겨서 회의 3시로 미루자
english: Something came up. Let's push the meeting back to 3.
alt: I have to deal with something. Can we move the meeting to 3?
note: push back = 뒤로 미루다 / move up = 앞당기다

korean: 데이터 보여줄게 화면 공유할게요
english: Let me share my screen so you can see the data.
alt: I'll pull up the data. Give me a sec to share my screen.
note: pull up = 화면에 띄우다

korean: 마이크 꺼져 있어요 우리한테 안 들려
english: You're on mute. We can't hear you.
alt: Your mic is off. Can you unmute?
note: You're on mute = 마이크 꺼져있어요 (재택 필수 표현)

korean: 인터넷 끊겼다가 다시 들어왔어 미안
english: Sorry I dropped off. My connection was unstable.
alt: I got disconnected for a sec. Bad Wi-Fi. I'm back now.
note: drop off / get disconnected = 연결 끊기다

korean: 그거 이메일로 보내줘 기록으로 남겨야 하니까
english: Can you send that over email? I want to have it in writing.
alt: Shoot me an email with that so we have a paper trail.
note: paper trail = 문서 기록 / in writing = 서면으로

korean: 그 이메일에 나도 참조 넣어줘 상황 파악하게
english: CC me on that email so I can stay in the loop.
alt: Loop me in on that thread.
note: in the loop = 상황 파악하고 있는 / loop me in = 나도 끼워줘

korean: 잠깐 실례할게요 급한 전화가 와서
english: If you'll excuse me, I need to take an urgent call.
alt: Sorry I have to step away. Important call.
note: step away = 잠시 자리를 비우다

korean: 하나만 말할게 우리 너무 복잡하게 생각하고 있어
english: Can I just say something? I think we're overcomplicating this.
alt: Hold on. We're making this way harder than it needs to be.
note: overcomplicate = 너무 복잡하게 만들다

korean: 그건 지금 논점이 아니야 주제에 집중하자
english: That's beside the point. Let's stay focused.
alt: That's a separate issue. Can we stay on track?
note: stay on track = 주제에서 벗어나지 않다

korean: 무슨 말인지 알겠는데 좀 다르게 생각해봐
english: I see what you're saying, but think about it from a different angle.
alt: I get your point but look at it this way.
note: see what you're saying = 무슨 말인지 알겠어

korean: 나 그거 완전 찬성이야 빨리 하자
english: I'm totally on board with that. Let's do it.
alt: I'm all for it. Let's get started.
note: on board = 찬성하는/동의하는 / all for it = 완전 찬성

korean: 솔직히 그건 좀 리스크가 크지 않아?
english: Honestly don't you think that's a bit risky?
alt: I'm a little worried about the risk on this one.
note: I'm a little worried about = 좀 걱정되는

korean: 회의 시간에 좋은 아이디어가 갑자기 떠올랐어
english: A great idea just popped into my head during the meeting.
alt: I had a lightbulb moment in the middle of the meeting.
note: lightbulb moment = 번뜩이는 순간

korean: 이거 예산 초과될 것 같은데 어떻게 하지?
english: I think this is going to go over budget. What should we do?
alt: We might blow the budget on this. Any ideas?
note: go over budget / blow the budget = 예산 초과

korean: 그건 아직 확정된 게 아니야 변경될 수 있어
english: That's not set in stone yet. It could still change.
alt: Nothing's finalized. Things might shift.
note: set in stone = 확정된/바꿀 수 없는

korean: 오늘 발표 잘했어 긴장한 티 하나도 안 났어
english: Great presentation today. You didn't look nervous at all.
alt: You nailed that presentation. Couldn't even tell you were stressed.
note: nail it = 완벽하게 해내다

korean: 실수한 거 알겠는데 다음부터 잘하면 돼
english: I know you messed up but just learn from it and move on.
alt: Everyone makes mistakes. Don't beat yourself up over it.
note: beat yourself up = 자책하다

korean: 이거 좀 급한데 빨리 처리해줄 수 있어?
english: This is kind of urgent. Can you handle it ASAP?
alt: I need this done pretty quickly. Can you prioritize it?
note: ASAP = As Soon As Possible / prioritize = 우선순위에 두다

korean: 그거 확인해보고 다시 연락할게
english: Let me look into it and get back to you.
alt: I'll check on that and circle back.
note: circle back = 다시 연락하다 (비즈니스 필수 표현)

korean: 좋은 기회인 건 알겠는데 타이밍이 안 맞아
english: I know it's a great opportunity, but the timing just isn't right.
alt: It's tempting but I can't commit right now. Bad timing.
note: the timing isn't right = 타이밍이 안 맞다

korean: 나 이따가 좀 빠져야 할 것 같아 먼저 양해 구할게
english: I might have to slip out early. Just wanted to give you a heads up.
alt: I may need to leave a bit early. Hope that's okay.
note: slip out = 슬쩍 빠지다

korean: 이번에 진짜 제대로 해보자 대충 하지 말고
english: Let's do it right this time. No cutting corners.
alt: Let's go all in. No half-measures.
note: cut corners = 대충하다/편법 쓰다 / half-measures = 미봉책

korean: 그래서 결론이 뭔데? 핵심만 말해
english: So what's the bottom line? Just give me the gist.
alt: Cut to the chase. What's the point?
note: bottom line = 결론 / gist = 요지 / cut to the chase = 본론으로

korean: 나 어제 면접 봤는데 망한 것 같아
english: I had a job interview yesterday and I think I totally bombed it.
alt: I went to an interview yesterday and it did not go well at all.
note: bomb = 망하다 (시험/면접에서)

korean: 오늘 야근이야 먼저 가 나 늦을 것 같아
english: I have to work overtime today. Go ahead without me.
alt: I'm stuck at the office tonight. Don't wait up for me.
note: work overtime = 야근하다 / don't wait up = 기다리지 마

korean: 하루종일 회의만 했더니 머리가 터질 것 같아
english: I've been in meetings all day and my head is about to explode.
alt: Back-to-back meetings all day. My brain is completely fried.
note: back-to-back = 연달아 / brain is fried = 머리 터짐

korean: 지금 퇴근하고 있어 30분 뒤에 도착해
english: I just left work. I'll be there in about thirty minutes.
alt: I'm heading home now. Give me half an hour.
note: heading home = 집으로 가는 중

korean: 무슨 말인지 알겠는데 동의는 안 해
english: I see your point but I don't agree.
alt: I hear what you're saying but I'm not on board.
note: I see your point = 무슨 말인지 알겠어 / not on board = 동의 안 해

korean: 프레젠테이션 준비 다 했어? 내일 발표잖아
english: Is your presentation ready? You're presenting tomorrow.
alt: All set for your presentation tomorrow?
note: all set = 다 준비된

korean: 이거 계약서 내용 좀 봐줘 뭔가 이상한 것 같아
english: Can you look over this contract for me? Something seems off.
alt: Would you mind reviewing this agreement? I think there might be an issue.
note: look over = 검토하다 / something seems off = 뭔가 이상해

korean: 너 이번에 진급했다며? 축하해!
english: I heard you got promoted! Congratulations!
alt: So you moved up! That's awesome. Well deserved!
note: got promoted = 진급했다 / well deserved = 당연한 결과

korean: 프리랜서로 전환할까 고민 중이야
english: I'm thinking about going freelance.
alt: I've been considering making the switch to freelancing.
note: go freelance = 프리랜서로 전환하다

korean: 이번 주 금요일까지 보고서 내야 하는데 아직 시작도 못 했어
english: I have a report due Friday and I haven't even started.
alt: My report is due end of week and I'm nowhere near done.
note: due = 마감인 / nowhere near done = 완성까지 멀었다

korean: 요즘 재택근무해서 출퇴근 안 하니까 너무 좋아
english: I've been working from home lately and not having a commute is the best.
alt: Remote work is amazing. No commute is a game changer.
note: work from home = 재택근무 / commute = 통근

korean: 나 이번에 자격증 시험 붙었어!
english: I passed my certification exam!
alt: I got my certification! Finally!
note: pass = 합격하다 / certification = 자격증

korean: 지금 자리 비워도 돼? 잠깐 볼일 좀 보고 올게
english: Mind if I step out for a bit? I need to take care of something.
alt: Is it okay if I duck out for a minute? I'll be right back.
note: step out = 잠깐 나가다 / duck out = 슬쩍 빠지다

korean: 너무 시끄러워서 집중이 안 돼 조용한 데로 옮기자
english: It's too noisy. I can't concentrate. Let's find somewhere quieter.
alt: I can't focus with all this noise. Can we move somewhere else?
note: concentrate/focus = 집중하다

korean: 그래서 우리 앞으로 어떻게 할 거야? 계획이 있어?
english: So what's our game plan going forward? Any ideas?
alt: What's the plan from here? How do we move forward?
note: game plan = 전략/계획

korean: 이 프로젝트 마감이 다가오는데 아직 갈 길이 멀어
english: The deadline for this project is approaching and we still have a long way to go.
alt: We're running out of time on this project and there's still so much to do.
note: approaching = 다가오는 / a long way to go = 갈 길이 먼

korean: 솔직히 이건 좀 아닌 것 같아 다시 생각해보자
english: Honestly this doesn't feel right. Let's reconsider.
alt: I don't think this is the way to go. Let's take a step back.
note: reconsider = 재고하다 / take a step back = 한 발 물러서서 생각하다

korean: 내일 면접이라 오늘 일찍 자려고 해
english: I have an interview tomorrow so I'm trying to turn in early tonight.
alt: I need to get an early night. Big interview in the morning.
note: turn in early = 일찍 자다 / get an early night = 일찍 잠자리에 들다

korean: 이번 프로젝트 리드가 너라서 마음이 좀 놓여
english: I feel a lot better knowing you're leading this project.
alt: It puts my mind at ease that you're the one in charge of this.
note: puts my mind at ease = 마음이 놓이다

korean: 이메일 확인 좀 해줘 아까 보냈거든
english: Can you check your email? I sent you something earlier.
alt: I emailed you a bit ago. Did you get a chance to look at it?
note: get a chance to = ~할 기회가 있다

korean: 나 이 일 적성에 안 맞는 것 같아 진지하게 이직 고민 중이야
english: I don't think this job is for me. I'm seriously considering a career change.
alt: This just isn't my calling. I'm thinking about switching careers for real.
note: career change = 이직 / calling = 천직

korean: 오늘 출근하기 싫다 누가 좀 대신 가줘
english: I so don't want to go to work today. Someone take my place.
alt: I would pay someone to go to work for me right now.
note: take my place = 내 대신 가다

korean: 이 프로젝트 누가 담당이야? 책임자가 누구야?
english: Who's in charge of this project? Who's the point person?
alt: Who's leading this? I need to know who to reach out to.
note: in charge = 담당인 / point person = 담당자

korean: 이번 출장 일정이 빡빡해서 관광할 시간이 없어
english: My business trip schedule is so tight there's no time for sightseeing.
alt: This work trip is jam-packed. Zero free time to explore.
note: jam-packed = 빡빡한 / sightseeing = 관광

korean: 그 사람 연락처 혹시 알아? 급하게 연락할 일이 생겨서
english: Do you happen to have their contact info? I urgently need to reach them.
alt: I need to get in touch with them ASAP. Do you have their number?
note: ASAP = 최대한 빨리 / get in touch = 연락하다

korean: 이 회사 복지가 좋은 편이야 특히 연차가 넉넉해
english: This company has pretty good benefits especially the generous PTO.
alt: The perks here are solid. We get a lot of paid time off.
note: benefits/perks = 복지 / PTO = 유급휴가

korean: 이 회의 언제 끝나? 다른 회의가 연달아 있거든
english: When does this meeting end? I have back-to-back meetings after this.
alt: I need to hop to my next call. How much longer do we have?
note: back-to-back = 연달아 / hop to = 빨리 넘어가다

korean: 네 의견도 일리가 있어 좀 더 생각해볼게
english: You make a fair point. Let me think about it some more.
alt: That's a valid argument. Give me some time to mull it over.
note: fair point / valid argument = 일리 있는 주장 / mull over = 곰곰이 생각하다

korean: 그건 좀 무리인 것 같아 현실적으로 생각하자
english: I think that's a bit unrealistic. Let's be practical.
alt: That's a stretch. Let's keep our feet on the ground.
note: a stretch = 무리인 / keep feet on the ground = 현실적이다

korean: 나 이번 주에 연차 써서 좀 쉬려고
english: I'm taking some PTO this week to recharge.
alt: I put in for a few days off this week. I desperately need a break.
note: PTO = paid time off / recharge = 충전하다

korean: 이 서류 언제까지 제출해야 해? 기한이 있어?
english: When is this paperwork due? Is there a deadline?
alt: What's the submission deadline for these documents?
note: due = 마감인 / submission deadline = 제출 기한

korean: 제가 그 부분 수정해서 다시 보내드릴게요
english: I'll revise that part and send it back to you.
alt: Let me make the changes and get the updated version to you.
note: revise = 수정하다 / updated version = 수정본

korean: 한 2~3일 정도 걸릴 것 같은데 괜찮아?
english: It'll probably take about two to three days. Is that okay?
alt: I'm looking at a turnaround of a couple days. That work for you?
note: turnaround = 소요 시간 / work for you = 괜찮아?

korean: 오늘 회식인데 안 가면 안 돼? 진짜 가기 싫어
english: We have a team dinner tonight. Can I skip it? I really don't want to go.
alt: Do I really have to go to the work dinner? I'd rather not.
note: team dinner = 회식 / skip = 빠지다

korean: 너 이메일 안 봤어? 아까 보냈는데
english: Did you not see my email? I sent it a while ago.
alt: I emailed you earlier. Did you get it or did it go to spam?
note: go to spam = 스팸으로 가다

korean: 나 이 프로그램 쓸 줄 몰라 좀 알려줘
english: I don't know how to use this program. Can you show me?
alt: I'm clueless about this software. Could you walk me through it?
note: walk me through = 단계별로 알려주다

korean: 그래서 네가 하고 싶은 게 정확히 뭔데?
english: So what exactly do you want to do?
alt: What's your actual goal here? I want to make sure I understand.
note: actual goal = 실제 목표

korean: 내일 휴일인 거 알지? 출근 안 해도 돼
english: Tomorrow is a holiday, you know? You don't have to go to work.
alt: Heads up tomorrow is a day off. Don't set your alarm.
note: day off = 쉬는 날 / heads up = 참고로

korean: 혹시 영수증 주실 수 있나요? 경비 처리해야 해서
english: Could I get a receipt please? I need it for expense reporting.
alt: I need a receipt for reimbursement purposes.
note: receipt = 영수증 / reimbursement = 환급

korean: 다음 주까지 결정하면 돼? 좀 더 알아보고 싶어서
english: Can I decide by next week? I want to look into it a bit more.
alt: Mind giving me until next week? I'd like to do some more research.
note: look into = 알아보다 / giving me until = ~까지 시간을 주다

korean: 너 연봉 올랐어? 요즘 물가 때문에 동결이면 사실상 삭감이야
english: Did you get a raise? With inflation a pay freeze is basically a pay cut.
alt: Any salary bump this year? If not you're essentially earning less.
note: raise = 인상 / pay freeze = 동결 / pay cut = 삭감

korean: 일이 너무 많아서 점심도 건너뛸 것 같아
english: I'm so swamped I'll probably skip lunch today.
alt: Work is so crazy right now I might not even eat lunch.
note: swamped = 일에 파묻힌 / skip = 건너뛰다

korean: 이 회의 너무 길어 핵심만 빠르게 정리하자
english: This meeting is dragging on. Let's wrap it up with the key points.
alt: Can we cut this short and just cover the essentials?
note: dragging on = 길어지는 / wrap it up = 마무리하다 / essentials = 핵심

korean: 나 내일 출장이라 오늘 짐 싸야 해
english: I have a business trip tomorrow so I need to pack tonight.
alt: I'm traveling for work tomorrow. Gotta get my bag ready.
note: business trip = 출장 / pack = 짐 싸다

korean: 그래서 다음 단계가 뭐야? 액션 아이템 정리해줘
english: So what's the next step? Can you list out the action items?
alt: Let's get the takeaways organized. What needs to happen next?
note: action items = 액션 아이템 / takeaways = 핵심 사항

korean: 나 이 일 더 이상 못 하겠어 한계야
english: I can't do this anymore. I've reached my limit.
alt: I'm at my breaking point with this. Something has to change.
note: reached my limit / breaking point = 한계에 도달한

korean: 그 사람 진짜 일 잘해 효율적이고 꼼꼼해
english: They're really good at their job. Efficient and detail-oriented.
alt: They're a top performer. Super thorough and never miss a beat.
note: efficient = 효율적인 / detail-oriented = 꼼꼼한 / thorough = 철저한

korean: 나 회의 중이라 나중에 전화할게 문자로 보내줘
english: I'm in a meeting right now. I'll call you back later. Just text me.
alt: Can't talk now I'm in a meeting. Drop me a text instead.
note: drop me a text = 문자 보내줘

korean: 이거 나 혼자 하기에는 좀 벅차 도와줄 사람 있어?
english: This is a bit too much for me to handle alone. Anyone available to help?
alt: I'm in over my head with this. I could really use an extra pair of hands.
note: in over my head = 감당이 안 되는 / extra pair of hands = 도움의 손길

korean: 제 메일 확인 부탁드립니다 급한 건이라서요
english: Please check your email at your earliest convenience. It's urgent.
alt: I sent an urgent email. Could you take a look when you get a chance?
note: at your earliest convenience = 가능한 빨리 / urgent = 급한

korean: 이제 그만 일하고 쉬자 내일 또 해야 하잖아
english: Let's call it a day. We've got more to do tomorrow anyway.
alt: That's enough for today. We'll pick it back up tomorrow.
note: call it a day = 오늘은 여기까지 / pick it back up = 다시 시작하다

korean: 그 회의 내용 회의록 좀 공유해줘
english: Can you share the meeting minutes with me?
alt: Send me the notes from that meeting when you get a chance.
note: meeting minutes = 회의록 / notes = 메모

korean: 나 이 일 때려치우고 싶어 진짜
english: I want to quit this job so badly.
alt: I'm this close to handing in my resignation.
note: quit = 그만두다 / handing in my resignation = 사직서 제출

korean: 보고서 초안 다 썼어? 검토 좀 해줄게
english: Did you finish the first draft of the report? I'll review it for you.
alt: Is the rough draft done? Let me take a look and give you feedback.
note: first draft / rough draft = 초안 / review = 검토

korean: 너 발표 잘했어 떨리는 거 하나도 안 보였어
english: You did great in the presentation. You didn't look nervous at all.
alt: Your presentation was solid. I couldn't tell you were anxious.
note: solid = 훌륭한 / couldn't tell = 티가 안 났다

korean: 내일 모레가 기한인데 아직 반도 못 했어
english: The deadline is the day after tomorrow and I'm not even halfway done.
alt: It's due in two days and I've barely started.
note: deadline = 마감 / halfway done = 반 완료

korean: 지금 시작하면 저녁까지 끝낼 수 있을 것 같아
english: If we start now we can probably finish by evening.
alt: We should be able to wrap this up by dinnertime if we get going.
note: wrap up = 마무리하다 / get going = 시작하다

korean: 이 회사 워라밸 어때? 야근 많아?
english: How's the work-life balance at this company? Is there a lot of overtime?
alt: Do people work late a lot? I want to know about the work-life balance.
note: work-life balance = 워라밸 / overtime = 야근

korean: 이번에 새로 온 팀원 적응 잘 하고 있어?
english: Is the new team member settling in okay?
alt: How's the new hire adjusting? Are they fitting in with the team?
note: settling in = 적응하는 / fitting in = 어울리는

korean: 오늘 미팅에서 좋은 인상 남겨야 하는데 긴장돼
english: I need to make a good impression at today's meeting and I'm so nervous.
alt: I've got a big meeting today. I really need to nail it.
note: make a good impression = 좋은 인상 남기다 / nail it = 성공하다

korean: 그 사람 진짜 일만 해 워커홀릭이야
english: All they do is work. They're a total workaholic.
alt: They live and breathe their job. Textbook workaholic.
note: workaholic = 일 중독자 / live and breathe = ~만 하다

korean: 이번 달 목표 달성했어? 실적이 어때?
english: Did you hit your targets this month? How are your numbers?
alt: Are we on track to meet our monthly goals?
note: hit targets = 목표 달성 / on track = 순조로운

korean: 회사에서 회식 안 가면 눈치 보여? 그냥 안 가고 싶은데
english: Is it looked down upon to skip company dinners? I'd rather not go.
alt: Do people judge you for skipping work socials? I just want to go home.
note: looked down upon = 안 좋게 보는 / work socials = 회사 행사

korean: 지금 연봉 협상 중인데 얼마나 요구해야 할지 모르겠어
english: I'm in salary negotiations right now and I'm not sure how much to ask for.
alt: I'm trying to figure out a reasonable number for my raise.
note: salary negotiations = 연봉 협상 / raise = 인상

korean: 그래 오늘은 여기서 끝내자 다음에 이어서 하자
english: Let's stop here for today. We'll pick up where we left off next time.
alt: That's a wrap for today. We'll continue this next session.
note: pick up where we left off = 이어서 하다 / that's a wrap = 오늘은 끝

korean: 알바 구하는 중인데 괜찮은 데 없어?
english: I'm looking for a part-time job. Know any good places?
alt: I'm job hunting for something part-time. Any leads?
note: part-time job = 아르바이트 / leads = 단서/정보

korean: 요즘 경제가 안 좋아서 취업이 쉽지 않아
english: The economy is rough right now so finding a job isn't easy.
alt: The job market is tough these days with the economic downturn.
note: economy is rough = 경제가 안 좋은 / job market = 취업 시장

korean: 회의 시작하기 전에 커피 한 잔 사올게 뭐 마실래?
english: I'm going to grab a coffee before the meeting. Want anything?
alt: I'm doing a coffee run. What can I get you?
note: coffee run = 커피 사러 가기 / grab = 사오다

korean: 이 프로젝트 담당자 바뀌었어? 누구한테 보고해야 해?
english: Did the project manager change? Who should I report to?
alt: I heard there was a personnel change. Who's my new point of contact?
note: project manager = 프로젝트 담당자 / point of contact = 연락 담당

korean: 이 프로그램 단축키 좀 알려줘 마우스 쓰기 귀찮아
english: Can you teach me the keyboard shortcuts for this program? Using the mouse is tedious.
alt: What are the hotkeys? I want to speed up my workflow.
note: keyboard shortcuts / hotkeys = 단축키 / workflow = 작업 흐름

korean: 이 메일 제목을 좀 더 구체적으로 바꿔줄래?
english: Can you make the subject line more specific?
alt: Could you tweak the email subject to be clearer?
note: subject line = 이메일 제목

korean: 참조에 팀장님 넣어서 보내줘
english: Make sure to CC the team lead on that email.
alt: Loop in the team lead when you send it.
note: CC = Carbon Copy. loop in = 포함시키다

korean: 답장 좀 빨리 해주면 좋겠어 클라이언트가 기다리고 있거든
english: It'd be great if you could reply sooner. The client's waiting.
alt: Can you get back to them ASAP? The client's on hold.
note: ASAP = As Soon As Possible

korean: 이 메일 누구한테 포워딩하면 돼?
english: Who should I forward this email to?
alt: Who do I need to loop in on this?
note: forward = 전달하다. loop in = 공유 대상에 포함

korean: 메일 보내기 전에 한번 검토 좀 해줄래?
english: Could you review this before I send it out?
alt: Mind giving this a once-over before I hit send?
note: once-over = 빠른 검토

korean: 그 건에 대해서 이메일로 따로 정리해서 보낼게요
english: I'll send a separate email summarizing that.
alt: Let me put together an email on that and send it over.
note: put together = 정리하다/구성하다

korean: 메일 확인하면 답장 좀 부탁드려요
english: Please reply once you've had a chance to review the email.
alt: Let me know your thoughts when you get to the email.
note: get to = ~에 손이 닿다/확인하다

korean: 이메일 톤이 좀 딱딱한데 부드럽게 고쳐볼까?
english: The tone feels a bit stiff. Should we soften it up?
alt: This reads a bit formal. Want to make it friendlier?
note: stiff = 딱딱한. soften = 부드럽게 하다

korean: 전체 답장 말고 보낸 사람한테만 답장해
english: Reply just to the sender, not reply-all.
alt: Don't hit reply-all. Just respond to the sender.
note: reply-all = 전체 답장. 실수 방지 표현

korean: 그 메일 아직 안 왔는데 다시 보내줄 수 있어?
english: I haven't received that email yet. Can you resend it?
alt: The email didn't come through. Could you send it again?
note: come through = 도착하다

korean: 메신저로 간단히 물어봐도 될까요?
english: Can I just ping you on messenger real quick?
alt: Mind if I shoot you a quick message?
note: ping = 메시지 보내다. shoot a message = 메시지 날리다

korean: 그 파일 메일에 첨부해서 보내줘
english: Attach the file to the email and send it over.
alt: Can you include the file as an attachment?
note: attachment = 첨부파일

korean: 지난번 메일에 이어서 팔로업 드립니다
english: I'm following up on my previous email.
alt: Just circling back on my last email.
note: follow up / circle back 둘 다 후속 연락 의미

korean: 메일에 오타가 있었는데 수정해서 다시 보낼까?
english: There was a typo in the email. Should I resend with corrections?
alt: I noticed a typo. Want me to send a corrected version?
note: typo = 오타. corrected version = 수정본

korean: 이 메일 긴급이니까 제목에 URGENT 붙여서 보내
english: This is urgent so mark it as urgent in the subject line.
alt: Flag it as urgent in the subject.
note: flag = 표시하다. urgent = 긴급

korean: 수신 확인 좀 해주세요
english: Could you confirm that you received the email?
alt: Can you acknowledge receipt?
note: acknowledge receipt = 수신 확인하다

korean: 메일 하단에 서명이 빠져있어
english: Your email signature is missing at the bottom.
alt: You forgot to include your signature.
note: email signature = 이메일 서명

korean: 메일을 잘못 보냈어 회수할 수 있나?
english: I sent the email to the wrong person. Can I recall it?
alt: I accidentally sent it to the wrong address. Can I undo it?
note: recall = 회수하다. undo = 되돌리다

korean: 이메일 스레드가 너무 길어져서 새로 시작하자
english: This email thread is getting too long. Let's start a new one.
alt: The chain is getting messy. Let's start fresh.
note: thread/chain = 이메일 대화 묶음

korean: 그 건은 슬랙으로 공유해줄래? 이메일보다 빠르니까
english: Can you share that on Slack? It's faster than email.
alt: Post it on Slack instead, it'll be quicker.
note: post = 게시하다. 메신저 소통 표현

korean: 전체 공지 메일 보내야 하는데 검토 좀 해줘
english: I need to send a company-wide email. Can you review it?
alt: I'm drafting an all-hands email. Mind taking a look?
note: company-wide / all-hands = 전사적

korean: 이 메일 문구가 좀 애매한데 다시 써줄래?
english: The wording in this email is a bit vague. Can you rewrite it?
alt: This phrasing is unclear. Could you rephrase it?
note: vague = 애매한. rephrase = 다시 표현하다

korean: 답장이 늦어서 죄송합니다
english: Sorry for the late reply.
alt: Apologies for the delayed response.
note: delayed response = 늦은 답변 (포멀)

korean: 메일 내용 길게 쓰지 말고 요약해서 보내
english: Keep the email short and to the point.
alt: Don't write a novel. Just summarize the key points.
note: to the point = 핵심만. novel = 장문 비유

korean: 읽었는데 답장할 내용이 없어서 안 했어
english: I read it but didn't reply since there was nothing to add.
alt: I saw it, just didn't have anything to respond with.
note: nothing to add = 추가할 내용 없음

korean: 이 건으로 별도 메일 보내줄래? 섞이면 헷갈려
english: Can you send a separate email for this? It'll get confusing otherwise.
alt: Let's keep this in a separate thread, or it'll get mixed up.
note: separate thread = 별도 스레드

korean: 자동 응답 설정해놨어 휴가 중이라서
english: I set up an out-of-office reply since I'm on leave.
alt: I've got my auto-reply on because I'm on vacation.
note: out-of-office reply = 부재중 자동 응답

korean: 메일 보내기 전에 맞춤법 한번만 확인해
english: Double-check the spelling before you send it.
alt: Run a quick spell check before hitting send.
note: spell check = 맞춤법 검사

korean: 그 메일 BCC로 나한테도 보내줘
english: BCC me on that email.
alt: Send me a blind copy of that.
note: BCC = Blind Carbon Copy = 숨은 참조

korean: 메일 드래프트 저장해놨으니까 확인하고 보내줘
english: I saved a draft. Check it and send it when you're ready.
alt: The draft is ready. Give it a look and fire it off.
note: draft = 초안. fire off = 보내다 (캐주얼)

korean: 슬랙 채널에 공유해뒀으니까 확인해봐
english: I posted it on the Slack channel. Take a look.
alt: Check the Slack channel. I dropped it in there.
note: drop = 올리다/공유하다 (캐주얼)

korean: 이메일 말고 전화로 얘기하는 게 빠를 것 같아
english: I think it'd be faster to just hop on a call.
alt: Let's skip the email and just talk on the phone.
note: hop on a call = 전화하다 (캐주얼)

korean: 이 메일 내일 아침에 예약 발송해줘
english: Schedule this email to go out tomorrow morning.
alt: Set it to send first thing tomorrow morning.
note: schedule to send = 예약 발송하다

korean: 참조에 들어가 있는 사람이 너무 많아 정리하자
english: There are too many people CC'd. Let's trim the list.
alt: Way too many people on this thread. Let's narrow it down.
note: trim the list = 목록 줄이다

korean: 메일 제목만 보고 넘겼는데 내용이 중요했네
english: I skimmed past it based on the subject line but it was actually important.
alt: I almost missed it. The subject didn't look urgent.
note: skim past = 훑고 넘기다

korean: 그 메일에 답장할 때 나도 같이 넣어줘
english: When you reply to that email include me too.
alt: Loop me in when you respond to that.
note: loop me in = 나를 포함시켜줘

korean: 이 이메일 영어로 써야 하는데 자연스러운지 봐줘
english: I need to write this in English. Can you check if it sounds natural?
alt: Does this email read okay in English? Can you proofread it?
note: proofread = 교정하다

korean: 메일함이 꽉 차서 정리 좀 해야겠다
english: My inbox is overflowing. I need to clean it up.
alt: I gotta clear out my inbox. It's a mess.
note: inbox = 수신함. clean out = 정리하다

korean: 그 건 메일로 문서화해놓자 나중에 근거 자료로 쓸 수 있으니까
english: Let's document that over email so we have a paper trail.
alt: Put it in writing, just in case we need to reference it later.
note: paper trail = 문서 기록. in writing = 서면으로

korean: 회의 시간 변경됐어 오후 2시로 옮겨졌대
english: The meeting got moved to 2 PM.
alt: Heads up — the meeting's been pushed to 2.
note: push to = ~로 미루다/옮기다

korean: 오늘 회의 안건이 뭐예요?
english: What's on the agenda for today's meeting?
alt: What are we covering in today's meeting?
note: agenda = 안건/의제

korean: 회의록 누가 작성하기로 했어?
english: Who's taking the meeting minutes?
alt: Who volunteered to take notes?
note: meeting minutes = 회의록. take notes = 기록하다

korean: 이 안건은 다음 회의로 넘기죠
english: Let's table this for the next meeting.
alt: Let's push this to the next session.
note: table = 보류하다 (미국식)

korean: 한 사람씩 돌아가면서 의견 말해볼까요?
english: Let's go around the room and hear from everyone.
alt: Why don't we do a round-robin and get everyone's input?
note: round-robin = 돌아가면서 하는 것

korean: 말씀하신 부분에 동의합니다 추가하자면요
english: I agree with what you said. I'd just like to add that...
alt: Good point. And on top of that, I'd say...
note: on top of that = 거기에 더해서

korean: 화상회의 링크 좀 보내줄래?
english: Can you send me the video call link?
alt: Could you share the meeting link?
note: video call / virtual meeting link

korean: 소리가 안 들려요 마이크 확인해주세요
english: I can't hear you. Can you check your mic?
alt: You're on mute or your mic's not working.
note: mute = 음소거. mic = 마이크

korean: 화면 공유 좀 해주실래요?
english: Could you share your screen?
alt: Mind pulling up your screen?
note: share your screen = 화면 공유하다

korean: 회의 길어지고 있는데 핵심만 정리하고 마무리하죠
english: The meeting's running long. Let's wrap up with the key takeaways.
alt: We're going over time. Let's summarize and wrap it up.
note: wrap up = 마무리하다. takeaway = 핵심 요점

korean: 오프라인으로 따로 얘기하죠
english: Let's take this offline.
alt: Let's discuss this separately after the meeting.
note: take this offline = 회의 밖에서 따로 논의

korean: 다들 이 방향으로 동의하시는 거죠?
english: So we're all on the same page with this direction?
alt: Are we aligned on this approach?
note: on the same page = 같은 이해. aligned = 동의한

korean: 이 건은 결정권자가 누구예요?
english: Who has the final say on this?
alt: Who's the decision-maker for this?
note: final say = 최종 결정권

korean: 회의 시작 전에 지난번 액션 아이템 확인하고 가죠
english: Before we start let's review the action items from last time.
alt: Let's go over last meeting's to-dos before we dive in.
note: action items = 실행 과제. dive in = 시작하다

korean: 다른 의견 있으신 분?
english: Does anyone have a different perspective?
alt: Any opposing views or concerns?
note: opposing views = 반대 의견

korean: 발언 중에 끊어서 죄송한데 한 가지만 짚고 넘어갈게요
english: Sorry to interrupt, but I'd like to address one thing.
alt: Pardon the interruption, but can I jump in real quick?
note: jump in = 끼어들다. address = 다루다

korean: 이 건은 투표로 결정하죠
english: Let's put it to a vote.
alt: Why don't we vote on it?
note: put it to a vote = 투표에 부치다

korean: 브레인스토밍 시간 좀 가지죠
english: Let's take some time to brainstorm.
alt: Let's throw around some ideas, shall we?
note: brainstorm = 아이디어를 모으다

korean: 회의 끝나고 회의록 공유해줄게
english: I'll share the meeting notes after the call.
alt: I'll send around a recap once we're done.
note: recap = 요약. send around = 돌리다

korean: 줌 연결이 자꾸 끊겨서 다시 들어갈게
english: My Zoom keeps dropping. Let me rejoin.
alt: The connection's unstable. I'll hop back in.
note: drop = 끊기다. hop back in = 다시 접속

korean: 회의 녹화해도 괜찮을까요?
english: Do you mind if I record this meeting?
alt: Is it okay if I hit record?
note: record = 녹화하다

korean: 카메라 켜주실 수 있나요?
english: Could you turn on your camera?
alt: Mind switching on your video?
note: turn on/switch on your camera/video

korean: 회의 참석 못 할 것 같은데 대신 참석해줄 수 있어?
english: I can't make the meeting. Can you attend on my behalf?
alt: I won't be able to join. Could you cover for me?
note: on my behalf = 대신. cover for = 대리하다

korean: 스탠드업 미팅 5분이면 끝나니까 다들 준비해주세요
english: Stand-up will be quick, five minutes tops. Everyone be ready.
alt: Let's keep the stand-up tight. Five minutes max.
note: tops / max = 최대. tight = 간결하게

korean: 회의 때 나온 피드백 반영해서 수정할게요
english: I'll incorporate the feedback from the meeting into the revisions.
alt: Let me update it based on what came up in the meeting.
note: incorporate = 반영하다. come up = 나오다

korean: 이 안건은 데이터가 더 필요하니까 보류합시다
english: Let's hold off on this until we have more data.
alt: We need more data before we decide, so let's park this.
note: hold off = 보류하다. park = 미뤄두다

korean: 회의 자료 미리 공유해주시면 좋겠어요
english: It'd be great if you could share the meeting materials in advance.
alt: Could you send over the deck before the meeting?
note: deck = 발표 자료. in advance = 미리

korean: 누가 타임키퍼 할 거예요?
english: Who's going to keep time?
alt: Who's the timekeeper for today?
note: timekeeper = 시간 관리자

korean: 이 건은 별도 회의 잡아서 논의하죠
english: Let's set up a separate meeting to discuss this.
alt: This deserves its own meeting. Let's schedule one.
note: deserves its own meeting = 별도 회의가 필요하다

korean: 데드라인이 언제예요?
english: When's the deadline?
alt: What's the due date on this?
note: deadline / due date = 마감일

korean: 이 작업 우선순위가 어떻게 돼?
english: What's the priority on this task?
alt: Where does this fall on the priority list?
note: priority = 우선순위. fall on = 위치하다

korean: 일정이 너무 빡빡해서 좀 조정해야 할 것 같아
english: The timeline is too tight. I think we need to adjust it.
alt: The schedule is pretty aggressive. We might need more time.
note: tight / aggressive = 빡빡한 (일정)

korean: 이슈가 하나 생겼는데 공유드릴게요
english: An issue came up that I need to flag for you.
alt: There's a problem I need to bring to your attention.
note: flag = 알리다. bring to attention = 주의를 끌다

korean: 진행 상황 보고서 이번 주까지 제출해주세요
english: Please submit the progress report by the end of this week.
alt: I'll need the status update by Friday.
note: progress report / status update = 진행 보고

korean: 이 일은 내가 담당할 테니까 다른 거 먼저 해
english: I'll own this one. You focus on the other stuff.
alt: I'll take the lead on this. Prioritize the rest.
note: own = 담당하다. take the lead = 주도하다

korean: 병목이 어디서 생기고 있는 거야?
english: Where's the bottleneck?
alt: What's causing the holdup?
note: bottleneck = 병목. holdup = 지연

korean: 리스크 요인 미리 파악해놓자
english: Let's identify potential risks ahead of time.
alt: We should flag any risks early on.
note: identify risks = 리스크 파악. flag = 표시하다

korean: 이 작업 예상보다 오래 걸릴 것 같아
english: This is going to take longer than expected.
alt: I don't think we'll finish this as fast as planned.
note: take longer than expected = 예상보다 오래 걸리다

korean: 마일스톤 달성했어 다음 단계로 넘어가자
english: We hit the milestone. Let's move on to the next phase.
alt: Milestone reached. Time to move to the next stage.
note: hit a milestone = 마일스톤 달성하다

korean: 역할 분담 다시 정리하자
english: Let's reassign the roles and responsibilities.
alt: We need to redistribute the workload.
note: reassign = 재배정. redistribute = 재분배

korean: 이 프로젝트 스코프가 자꾸 커지고 있어
english: The scope of this project keeps expanding.
alt: We're dealing with serious scope creep here.
note: scope creep = 범위가 계속 늘어나는 현상

korean: 백로그 좀 정리하자 쌓인 게 너무 많아
english: Let's groom the backlog. It's piling up.
alt: We need to clean up the backlog. It's gotten out of hand.
note: groom = 정리하다. pile up = 쌓이다

korean: 이 작업 의존성이 있어서 먼저 끝내야 돼
english: This task has a dependency, so it needs to be done first.
alt: We can't start that until this is finished. It's a blocker.
note: dependency = 의존성. blocker = 차단 요소

korean: 중간 점검 미팅 하나 잡을까?
english: Should we schedule a check-in meeting?
alt: Want to do a midpoint review?
note: check-in / midpoint review = 중간 점검

korean: 클라이언트한테 진행 상황 업데이트해야 해
english: We need to update the client on the progress.
alt: Time to give the client a status report.
note: status report = 상태 보고

korean: 이 작업 다른 사람한테 넘겨도 될까?
english: Can I hand this task off to someone else?
alt: Would it be okay to delegate this to another team member?
note: hand off = 넘기다. delegate = 위임하다

korean: 프로젝트 회고 미팅 하자
english: Let's do a project retrospective.
alt: Let's schedule a post-mortem.
note: retrospective / post-mortem = 회고/사후 분석

korean: 일정 지연 사유서 작성해야 해
english: I need to write up a delay justification.
alt: We need to document why the timeline slipped.
note: justification = 사유서. slip = 지연되다

korean: 이번 스프린트 목표가 뭐야?
english: What's the goal for this sprint?
alt: What are we targeting this sprint?
note: sprint = 짧은 개발 주기 (애자일)

korean: 이 이슈 핫픽스로 바로 처리해야 돼
english: This needs a hotfix right away.
alt: We need to patch this ASAP.
note: hotfix = 긴급 수정. patch = 패치하다

korean: 팀장님 이거 보고드려도 될까요?
english: Do you have a moment? I'd like to give you an update.
alt: Could I run something by you real quick?
note: run something by = 보고/의견 구하기

korean: 피드백 감사합니다 반영해서 수정하겠습니다
english: Thank you for the feedback. I'll make the revisions accordingly.
alt: Appreciate the input. I'll update it based on your suggestions.
note: accordingly = 그에 맞게. input = 의견

korean: 혹시 도움 필요하면 말해 내가 빠질게
english: Let me know if you need a hand. I'm happy to help.
alt: If you need backup just holler.
note: need a hand = 도움이 필요하다. holler = 말하다

korean: 이번 분기 실적이 기대 이상이네요
english: This quarter's results exceeded expectations.
alt: We outperformed our targets this quarter.
note: exceed expectations = 기대를 넘다

korean: 신입이 적응을 잘하고 있어서 다행이다
english: I'm glad the new hire is adjusting well.
alt: It's great to see the newbie settling in nicely.
note: adjust / settle in = 적응하다

korean: 솔직히 그 방법은 좀 비효율적인 것 같아요
english: To be honest I think that approach is a bit inefficient.
alt: Honestly that doesn't seem like the most efficient way.
note: to be honest = 솔직히. inefficient = 비효율적

korean: 좋은 아이디어인데 실행이 쉽지 않을 것 같아
english: Great idea, but I think execution will be challenging.
alt: I love the concept, but implementation might be tricky.
note: execution / implementation = 실행

korean: 요즘 좀 지쳐 보여 괜찮아?
english: You look a bit worn out lately. Everything okay?
alt: You seem tired these days. Is everything alright?
note: worn out = 지친

korean: 점심 같이 먹을래? 새로 생긴 데 가보자
english: Wanna grab lunch? Let's try that new place.
alt: Up for lunch? There's a new spot I wanna check out.
note: grab lunch = 점심 먹다 (캐주얼)

korean: 주말 잘 보냈어? 뭐 했어?
english: How was your weekend? Do anything fun?
alt: Good weekend? What'd you get up to?
note: get up to = ~을 하다 (캐주얼)

korean: 이거 좀 봐줄 수 있어? 내가 잘 모르는 부분이라서
english: Could you take a look at this? It's outside my expertise.
alt: Mind helping me out? This isn't really my wheelhouse.
note: wheelhouse = 전문 분야. outside my expertise = 전문 밖

korean: 그때 도와줘서 고마워 덕분에 잘 끝냈어
english: Thanks for your help back then. I couldn't have done it without you.
alt: I really appreciate you stepping in. It made all the difference.
note: step in = 나서서 돕다. make all the difference = 큰 차이를 만들다

korean: 이번 프레젠테이션 정말 잘했어 준비 많이 했지?
english: That was an amazing presentation. You clearly put a lot of work into it.
alt: You killed it in the presentation. The prep really showed.
note: kill it = 잘해내다. prep = 준비

korean: 제가 실수한 부분이 있으면 말씀해주세요
english: Please let me know if I made any mistakes.
alt: I'm open to feedback if anything needs fixing.
note: open to feedback = 피드백에 열려있는

korean: 이번에 제가 맡아볼게요 경험 쌓고 싶어서요
english: I'd like to take this on. I want to build more experience.
alt: Let me handle this one. I'm looking to grow in this area.
note: take on = 맡다. build experience = 경험 쌓다

korean: 그 업무는 내 선에서 처리하기 어려워서 도움이 필요해요
english: That's beyond what I can handle alone, so I need some help.
alt: I'm in over my head on this one. Could use some support.
note: in over my head = 감당이 안 되는

korean: 오늘 발표 잘했어 긴장한 티 하나도 안 났어
english: Great presentation today. You didn't look nervous at all.
alt: You nailed that presentation. Couldn't even tell you were stressed.
note: nail it = 완벽하게 해내다

korean: 팀장님 오늘 기분이 안 좋아 보여 조심해
english: The boss seems to be in a bad mood today. Watch out.
alt: Heads up — the manager's not in the best mood.
note: in a bad mood = 기분이 안 좋은

korean: 새로 온 인턴 일 잘하더라
english: The new intern is doing a great job.
alt: The new intern's really pulling their weight.
note: pull one's weight = 제 몫을 하다

korean: 저 먼저 가봐도 될까요? 약속이 있어서요
english: Would it be okay if I head out? I have a prior commitment.
alt: Mind if I take off early? I've got something after work.
note: prior commitment = 선약. take off = 퇴근하다

korean: 이번 건은 제가 책임지겠습니다
english: I'll take full responsibility for this.
alt: This one's on me. I'll own it.
note: take responsibility = 책임지다. own it = 내 책임

korean: 자기소개서를 너무 길게 쓴 것 같은데 줄여야 할까?
english: I think my cover letter is too long. Should I shorten it?
alt: My cover letter might be too wordy. Should I trim it down?
note: cover letter = 자기소개서. trim down = 줄이다

korean: 면접 때 긴장해서 말을 못 했어
english: I was so nervous during the interview that I froze up.
alt: I blanked out in the interview because of nerves.
note: freeze up / blank out = 긴장으로 말이 안 나오다

korean: 이력서에 경력 위주로 정리해봐
english: Focus on your work experience when formatting your resume.
alt: Highlight your professional experience on your CV.
note: resume / CV = 이력서. highlight = 강조하다

korean: 합격 통보 받았어! 다음 주부터 출근이야
english: I got the offer! I start next week.
alt: They accepted me! My first day is next week.
note: get the offer = 합격하다. first day = 첫 출근

korean: 면접관이 압박 질문을 엄청 했어
english: The interviewer really grilled me.
alt: They threw some tough questions at me in the interview.
note: grill = 캐묻다. throw tough questions = 어려운 질문을 던지다

korean: 이 포지션에 지원하려고 하는데 조건이 어때?
english: I'm thinking of applying for this position. What are the terms?
alt: I want to apply for this role. What's the package like?
note: terms / package = 조건 (급여 포함)

korean: 불합격 통보 받았어 다음 기회를 노려야지
english: I didn't get the job. I'll try again next time.
alt: They passed on me. On to the next one.
note: pass on = 거절하다. on to the next one = 다음 기회로

korean: 최종 면접까지 갔는데 떨어졌어
english: I made it to the final round but didn't get picked.
alt: I got all the way to the last interview and they went with someone else.
note: final round = 최종 면접. go with = 선택하다

korean: 면접 때 뭐 입고 가야 해? 정장이야?
english: What should I wear to the interview? Business formal?
alt: Do I need to suit up for the interview?
note: business formal = 정장. suit up = 정장 입다

korean: 포트폴리오 준비했어? 면접 때 보여줄 거 있어야 해
english: Did you prepare your portfolio? You'll need something to show.
alt: Got your portfolio ready? They'll want to see examples of your work.
note: portfolio = 포트폴리오. examples of work = 작업 예시

korean: 추천서 부탁해도 될까요?
english: Would you be willing to write a reference letter for me?
alt: Could I ask you for a recommendation?
note: reference letter / recommendation = 추천서

korean: 면접에서 본인의 강점이 뭐냐고 물어보면 뭐라고 할 거야?
english: What are you going to say when they ask about your strengths?
alt: How will you answer the strengths question?
note: strengths = 강점. 면접 단골 질문

korean: 경력직으로 지원하는 건데 연봉 협상이 가능할까?
english: I'm applying as an experienced hire. Is the salary negotiable?
alt: Since I'm coming in mid-career can we negotiate the pay?
note: experienced hire = 경력직. negotiable = 협상 가능한

korean: 레퍼런스 체크 연락 올 수도 있어 미리 알려줄게
english: You might get a reference check call. Just giving you a heads up.
alt: They may reach out for a reference. Wanted to let you know in advance.
note: reference check = 평판 조회

korean: 채용 공고 봤는데 우리 회사에 딱 맞는 사람 있어
english: I saw the job posting and I know someone who'd be perfect for it.
alt: There's a great candidate I know who fits the job listing.
note: job posting / listing = 채용 공고

korean: 기술 면접이 있대 코딩 테스트도 있나 봐
english: There's a technical interview. Apparently there's a coding test too.
alt: They have a tech round with a coding challenge.
note: technical interview = 기술 면접. coding test = 코딩 테스트

korean: 면접 결과 언제 나와?
english: When will I hear back about the interview?
alt: When should I expect to get the results?
note: hear back = 연락 받다

korean: 지원 동기가 뭔지 정리해놔
english: Make sure you have your motivation for applying clearly prepared.
alt: Know your answer to 'Why do you want this job?' before you go in.
note: motivation for applying = 지원 동기

korean: 레주메에 공백기가 있는데 어떻게 설명하지?
english: There's a gap in my resume. How should I explain it?
alt: How do I address the employment gap on my CV?
note: employment gap = 경력 공백기

korean: 인턴 경험이라도 넣는 게 낫겠지?
english: I should at least include my internship experience right?
alt: It's worth putting the internship on there even if it was short.
note: internship = 인턴십. include = 포함하다

korean: 면접 때 역질문 뭐 하면 좋을까?
english: What questions should I ask the interviewer?
alt: Any good questions to ask at the end of the interview?
note: 역질문 = questions for the interviewer

korean: 연봉 인상률이 기대 이하야
english: The salary increase was below my expectations.
alt: The raise was disappointing to be honest.
note: raise / salary increase = 연봉 인상

korean: 연차가 몇 일 남았는지 확인해봐
english: Check how many PTO days you have left.
alt: See how much leave you've still got.
note: PTO days / leave = 연차

korean: 건강보험 혜택이 괜찮은 편이야?
english: Are the health insurance benefits decent?
alt: How's the health coverage at your company?
note: health insurance / coverage = 건강보험

korean: 승진 기준이 뭔지 아는 사람?
english: Does anyone know what the promotion criteria are?
alt: What does it take to get promoted here?
note: promotion criteria = 승진 기준

korean: 보너스 언제 나와? 이번 달이야?
english: When do bonuses get paid out? Is it this month?
alt: Are we getting bonuses this month?
note: bonus payout = 보너스 지급

korean: 퇴직금은 얼마나 받을 수 있어?
english: How much severance will I get?
alt: What's the severance package like?
note: severance / severance package = 퇴직금

korean: 복지 포인트로 뭐 살 수 있어?
english: What can I buy with the welfare points?
alt: What's covered under the benefits allowance?
note: welfare points / benefits allowance = 복지 포인트

korean: 육아휴직 신청하려면 어떻게 해야 해?
english: How do I apply for parental leave?
alt: What's the process for requesting parental leave?
note: parental leave = 육아휴직

korean: 야근 수당이 있긴 한 거야?
english: Do we even get overtime pay?
alt: Is there actually an overtime allowance?
note: overtime pay = 야근 수당

korean: 연봉 협상할 때 어떻게 말해야 해?
english: How should I approach salary negotiation?
alt: What's the best way to negotiate my pay?
note: salary negotiation = 연봉 협상

korean: 인사고과 결과가 좀 아쉽다
english: My performance review results were a bit disappointing.
alt: I was hoping for a better evaluation.
note: performance review / evaluation = 인사고과

korean: 4대 보험 가입되어 있는 거 맞죠?
english: I'm enrolled in the four major insurances right?
alt: The statutory insurances are all covered right?
note: four major insurances = 4대 보험 (한국 특유)

korean: 반차 쓸 수 있어? 오전만 쉬고 싶어
english: Can I take a half day? I just need the morning off.
alt: Is it okay to use a half-day leave for the morning?
note: half day = 반차

korean: 연말정산 서류 제출 기한이 언제야?
english: When's the deadline for year-end tax settlement documents?
alt: What's the due date for submitting tax adjustment paperwork?
note: year-end tax settlement = 연말정산

korean: 수습 기간이 끝나면 정규직 전환되는 거야?
english: Do I become a full-time employee after the probation period?
alt: Am I converted to permanent after probation ends?
note: probation period = 수습 기간. permanent = 정규직

korean: 사이닝 보너스 있어?
english: Is there a signing bonus?
alt: Do they offer a sign-on bonus?
note: signing bonus / sign-on bonus = 입사 축하금

korean: 스톡옵션 조건이 어떻게 돼?
english: What are the stock option terms?
alt: How does the equity package work?
note: stock options / equity = 주식 보상

korean: 인사팀에 문의해봐야 할 것 같아
english: I think I need to check with HR.
alt: You should reach out to the HR department about that.
note: HR = Human Resources = 인사팀

korean: 임금 체불이면 신고할 수 있어
english: If wages are overdue you can file a complaint.
alt: You can report unpaid wages to the labor board.
note: file a complaint = 신고하다. labor board = 노동위원회

korean: 출장 일정 잡혔어 다음 주 부산이야
english: The business trip is set. I'm heading to Busan next week.
alt: My trip is confirmed. I'll be in Busan next week.
note: business trip = 출장. confirmed = 확정

korean: 출장 경비 정산 언제까지 해야 해?
english: When do I need to submit my travel expense report?
alt: What's the deadline for filing my trip expenses?
note: expense report = 경비 정산서

korean: 출장 가서 클라이언트 미팅 잘 마쳤어
english: I wrapped up the client meeting on my business trip successfully.
alt: The client meeting on my trip went really well.
note: wrap up = 마무리하다

korean: 교통비 영수증 꼭 챙겨
english: Make sure to keep your transportation receipts.
alt: Don't forget to save your transit receipts for reimbursement.
note: receipt = 영수증. reimbursement = 환급

korean: 호텔 예약 했어? 출장 숙소 잡아야 해
english: Did you book the hotel? We need to arrange accommodation.
alt: Have you sorted out lodging for the trip?
note: book / arrange accommodation = 숙소 예약

korean: 클라이언트가 저녁 대접하겠대 거절하기 좀 그렇지?
english: The client wants to treat us to dinner. It'd be rude to decline right?
alt: The client offered dinner. We can't really say no can we?
note: treat to dinner = 저녁 대접하다. decline = 거절

korean: 출장 보고서 언제까지 내야 돼?
english: When is the trip report due?
alt: What's the deadline for the business trip write-up?
note: trip report / write-up = 출장 보고서

korean: 법인카드로 결제해도 돼?
english: Can I charge it to the company card?
alt: Is it okay to use the corporate card for this?
note: company card / corporate card = 법인카드

korean: 출장비 한도가 얼마야?
english: What's the travel budget limit?
alt: How much can I spend on the business trip?
note: travel budget / spending limit = 출장비 한도

korean: 비행기 표 예약해야 하는데 회사에서 해주나?
english: Do I need to book my own flight or does the company arrange it?
alt: Does the company handle flight bookings or do I do it myself?
note: book a flight = 항공권 예약하다

korean: 출장 중인데 긴급 건이 생겼어 전화해줘
english: I'm on a business trip and something urgent came up. Call me.
alt: Something's come up while I'm traveling. Give me a ring.
note: come up = 발생하다. give me a ring = 전화해줘

korean: 외근 나갈 건데 오후에 복귀할게
english: I'm heading out for a field visit. I'll be back in the afternoon.
alt: I've got an off-site meeting. I'll be back after lunch.
note: field visit / off-site = 외근

korean: 출장 다녀오니까 밀린 업무가 산더미야
english: I just got back from my trip and the work has piled up.
alt: Coming back from the business trip to a mountain of work.
note: pile up = 쌓이다. mountain of work = 산더미 같은 업무

korean: 현지 시간으로 몇 시에 미팅이야?
english: What time is the meeting in local time?
alt: What's the meeting time in their time zone?
note: local time / time zone = 현지 시간 / 시간대

korean: 출장지에서 와이파이가 안 돼서 연락이 늦었어
english: I didn't have Wi-Fi at the trip location so my reply was delayed.
alt: Sorry for the late response — couldn't get online during my trip.
note: couldn't get online = 인터넷 접속 불가

korean: 클라이언트 선물 뭐 가져가면 좋을까?
english: What would be a good gift to bring for the client?
alt: Any ideas for a client gift?
note: client gift = 고객 선물

korean: 오늘 재택이라 슬랙으로 연락해줘
english: I'm working from home today so reach me on Slack.
alt: I'm remote today. Hit me up on Slack.
note: WFH = Work From Home. hit me up = 연락해

korean: VPN 연결이 안 돼서 사내 시스템 접속이 안 돼
english: I can't connect to the VPN so I can't access the internal system.
alt: The VPN's down on my end. I'm locked out of the system.
note: VPN = Virtual Private Network. locked out = 접근 불가

korean: 재택하면 집중이 더 잘 되는 것 같아
english: I feel like I focus better when I work from home.
alt: I'm way more productive working remotely.
note: productive = 생산적인

korean: 화상회의 배경 좀 바꿔 뒤에 지저분해
english: You might want to change your virtual background. It's a bit messy behind you.
alt: Maybe blur your background or use a virtual one.
note: virtual background = 가상 배경. blur = 흐리게

korean: 재택할 때 카메라 켜야 하는 게 좀 부담스러워
english: Having to keep my camera on during WFH is a bit much.
alt: I find it stressful being on camera all day while remote.
note: on camera = 카메라 켜진 상태

korean: 공유 문서에 업데이트해놨으니까 확인해봐
english: I updated the shared doc. Take a look when you can.
alt: Check the shared document. I made some changes.
note: shared doc = 공유 문서

korean: 재택인데 급하게 출근해야 할 일이 생겼어
english: I'm WFH today but something came up and I need to go in.
alt: I was supposed to work from home but I have to head to the office.
note: go in / head to the office = 출근하다

korean: 원격으로 협업하는 게 생각보다 어렵네
english: Remote collaboration is harder than I thought.
alt: Working together virtually isn't as easy as it seems.
note: remote collaboration = 원격 협업

korean: 재택 장비 지원 받을 수 있어?
english: Can I get equipment support for my home office?
alt: Does the company provide WFH equipment?
note: equipment support = 장비 지원

korean: 시간대가 달라서 미팅 시간 맞추기 힘들어
english: It's hard to find a meeting time that works across time zones.
alt: Scheduling across different time zones is a pain.
note: time zone = 시간대. across time zones = 시차 간

korean: 재택근무 규정이 어떻게 돼? 주 몇 회 가능해?
english: What's the WFH policy? How many days a week can I work remotely?
alt: How often can we work from home per week?
note: WFH policy = 재택근무 규정

korean: 출근하는 날에는 핫데스크 써야 해
english: On office days we use hot desks.
alt: When we're in the office it's hot-desking.
note: hot desk = 고정석 없이 자유석 사용

korean: 재택하면서 운동도 하고 좋더라
english: Working from home lets me squeeze in exercise too.
alt: One perk of WFH is I can work out during breaks.
note: squeeze in = 시간을 내다. perk = 장점

korean: 오늘 하이브리드 근무라 오전은 집에서 오후는 회사에서 할게
english: I'm doing hybrid today. Morning from home, afternoon at the office.
alt: I'll work from home in the AM and come in for the PM.
note: hybrid = 재택 + 출근 혼합 근무

korean: 재택 중인데 택배가 와서 잠깐만요
english: I'm WFH and there's a delivery. Give me one sec.
alt: Hold on a sec — someone's at the door. I'm working from home.
note: delivery = 택배. hold on = 잠깐만

korean: 캘린더 공유해놔서 내 일정 확인할 수 있어
english: I shared my calendar so you can see my schedule.
alt: My calendar's open. Feel free to check my availability.
note: shared calendar = 공유 캘린더. availability = 가용 시간

korean: 재택인데 인터넷이 느려서 화상회의가 끊겨
english: My internet's slow at home so the video call keeps lagging.
alt: The connection at home is terrible. The call keeps buffering.
note: lag = 지연되다. buffer = 버퍼링

korean: 코워킹 스페이스 가서 일할까? 집에서 집중이 안 돼
english: Should I go to a coworking space? I can't focus at home.
alt: I'm thinking of hitting a coworking space. Home's too distracting.
note: coworking space = 공유 오피스

korean: 비동기 소통이라 답장이 좀 늦을 수 있어
english: Since we're working asynchronously replies might be delayed.
alt: We're async so don't expect instant responses.
note: asynchronous = 비동기적. async = 줄임말

korean: 올해 KPI 설정 다 했어?
english: Have you set your KPIs for this year?
alt: Are your annual KPIs finalized?
note: KPI = Key Performance Indicator = 핵심 성과 지표

korean: 상반기 성과 리뷰가 다음 주래
english: The mid-year performance review is next week.
alt: First-half evaluations are coming up next week.
note: mid-year review = 상반기 리뷰

korean: 자기평가서 작성하는 게 제일 어려워
english: Writing the self-assessment is the hardest part.
alt: Self-evaluations are always the toughest to write.
note: self-assessment / self-evaluation = 자기평가

korean: 목표 달성률이 어떻게 돼?
english: What's your goal achievement rate?
alt: How close are you to hitting your targets?
note: achievement rate = 달성률. hit targets = 목표 달성

korean: 피드백 면담 시간 잡아주세요
english: Could you schedule a feedback session?
alt: Can we set up a time for my performance discussion?
note: feedback session = 피드백 면담

korean: 개선점을 좀 더 구체적으로 알려주세요
english: Could you be more specific about the areas for improvement?
alt: Can you give me more detailed feedback on what I need to work on?
note: areas for improvement = 개선 영역

korean: 이번 분기 목표를 좀 더 도전적으로 잡아보자
english: Let's set more ambitious goals for this quarter.
alt: Let's push ourselves with more challenging targets this quarter.
note: ambitious / challenging = 도전적인

korean: 팀 전체 성과가 좋아서 인센티브 나올 수도 있대
english: The team performance was strong, so we might get incentives.
alt: Word is we could get a bonus since the team did well.
note: incentive = 인센티브/성과급

korean: 360도 피드백 작성해야 하는데 솔직하게 써도 되는 거야?
english: I have to do a 360 feedback. Can I be completely honest?
alt: Is the 360 review really anonymous? Can I write candidly?
note: 360 feedback = 동료 상호 평가. candidly = 솔직하게

korean: 성과가 기대에 못 미쳤다는 피드백을 받았어
english: I got feedback that my performance fell short of expectations.
alt: They told me I didn't meet the expected targets.
note: fall short of = ~에 미치지 못하다

korean: 다음 분기에는 리더십 역량을 키우고 싶어
english: I want to develop my leadership skills next quarter.
alt: I'm looking to strengthen my leadership capabilities going forward.
note: leadership skills / capabilities = 리더십 역량

korean: OKR 작성하는 거 도와줄래?
english: Can you help me write my OKRs?
alt: I need a hand drafting my OKRs.
note: OKR = Objectives and Key Results

korean: 평가 기준이 좀 모호한 것 같아
english: The evaluation criteria seem a bit vague to me.
alt: I'm not clear on how we're being measured.
note: evaluation criteria = 평가 기준. vague = 모호한

korean: 매출 목표는 달성했는데 고객 만족도가 좀 떨어졌어
english: We hit our sales target, but customer satisfaction dipped.
alt: Revenue goal was met but CSAT scores went down.
note: CSAT = Customer Satisfaction. dip = 하락하다

korean: 개인 성장 목표도 넣어야 해?
english: Do I need to include personal development goals too?
alt: Should I add professional growth objectives as well?
note: personal development / professional growth = 개인/전문 성장

korean: 피드백을 건설적으로 주는 게 참 어렵다
english: Giving constructive feedback is really tough.
alt: It's hard to deliver feedback without it sounding negative.
note: constructive feedback = 건설적 피드백

korean: 성과 평가에 불만이 있으면 이의 제기 가능해?
english: Can I dispute my performance review if I disagree?
alt: Is there an appeal process for performance evaluations?
note: dispute / appeal = 이의 제기하다

korean: 핵심 성과 지표가 너무 많아서 집중이 안 돼
english: There are too many KPIs to focus on.
alt: I can't prioritize when there are this many metrics.
note: metrics = 지표. prioritize = 우선순위를 정하다

korean: 이번 평가에서 승진 대상자에 포함됐어
english: I was included in the promotion list this evaluation cycle.
alt: I made the cut for promotion this round.
note: make the cut = 선발되다. promotion list = 승진 대상자 목록

korean: 사직서 제출하려고 하는데요
english: I'd like to submit my resignation.
alt: I'm planning to put in my two weeks.
note: resignation = 사직서. put in two weeks = 2주 전 통보

korean: 퇴사 사유가 뭐냐고 물어보면 뭐라고 해?
english: What do I say when they ask why I'm leaving?
alt: How should I answer the exit interview question about why?
note: exit interview = 퇴직 면담

korean: 이직 고민 중인데 아직 결정 못 했어
english: I'm thinking about switching jobs but haven't decided yet.
alt: I'm considering a move but I'm still on the fence.
note: on the fence = 결정을 못한

korean: 퇴직금 정산 언제 되는지 알아?
english: Do you know when the severance gets processed?
alt: When should I expect my severance payout?
note: severance payout = 퇴직금 지급

korean: 인수인계 기간이 한 달이래
english: The handover period is one month.
alt: They want a month for the transition.
note: handover / transition = 인수인계

korean: 이직하면 연봉이 올라갈까?
english: Will I get a salary bump if I switch companies?
alt: Do people usually get a raise when they move to a new job?
note: salary bump / raise = 연봉 인상

korean: 경력 관리 좀 해야 할 것 같아
english: I need to start being more strategic about my career.
alt: I should focus more on career development.
note: career development = 경력 개발

korean: 퇴사 통보를 언제 해야 적절할까?
english: When's the right time to give notice?
alt: How far in advance should I notify them about leaving?
note: give notice = 퇴사 통보하다

korean: 이 회사에서 더 성장할 수 있을까?
english: Do you think there's still room for growth at this company?
alt: Can I really advance my career here?
note: room for growth = 성장 여지

korean: 링크드인 프로필 업데이트해야겠다
english: I should update my LinkedIn profile.
alt: Time to refresh my LinkedIn.
note: LinkedIn = 비즈니스 SNS

korean: 직장 생활이 너무 지치는데 번아웃인가?
english: I'm so exhausted from work. Am I burning out?
alt: I'm completely drained. I think I might be burned out.
note: burn out / burned out = 번아웃

korean: 헤드헌터한테 연락 왔어
english: I got a call from a headhunter.
alt: A recruiter reached out to me.
note: headhunter / recruiter = 헤드헌터/리크루터

korean: 퇴사할 때 동료들한테 인사 메일 보내야지
english: When I leave, I should send a farewell email to my colleagues.
alt: I need to write a goodbye email to the team before I go.
note: farewell email = 퇴사 인사 메일

korean: 비경쟁 조항이 있어서 바로 경쟁사로 갈 수 없어
english: There's a non-compete clause so I can't join a competitor right away.
alt: I'm bound by a non-compete so going to a rival isn't an option yet.
note: non-compete clause = 경업 금지 조항

korean: 이직할 때 레퍼런스 체크 연락이 현 직장에 갈 수도 있어
english: A reference check for the new job might reach my current employer.
alt: They could contact my current company for a background check.
note: reference check = 평판 조회. background check = 신원 조사

korean: 커리어 전환을 고민하고 있어 완전 다른 분야로
english: I'm thinking about a career change to a completely different field.
alt: I'm considering pivoting to an entirely new industry.
note: career change / pivot = 전환

korean: 이 업계에서 10년이면 시니어급 아니야?
english: With 10 years in the industry shouldn't I be at a senior level?
alt: Ten years of experience should put me at senior level right?
note: senior level = 시니어급

korean: 회사 복지가 별로라 이직하려고
english: The benefits here are lacking so I'm looking to move on.
alt: I'm job hunting because the perks at this company aren't great.
note: benefits / perks = 복지. move on = 이직하다

korean: 퇴사 면담 때 솔직하게 말해도 되는 거야?
english: Should I be completely honest during the exit interview?
alt: Can I speak freely in the exit interview?
note: exit interview = 퇴직 면담. speak freely = 솔직히 말하다

korean: 이 메일 좀 급한데 바로 처리해줄 수 있어?
english: This email is kind of urgent. Can you handle it right away?
alt: This needs immediate attention. Can you get on it now?
note: immediate attention = 즉각적 관심

korean: 메일에 첨부파일이 안 열려 다시 보내줄래?
english: The attachment won't open. Can you resend it?
alt: I can't open the file you attached. Mind sending it again?
note: attachment = 첨부파일

korean: 다음 주 회의 참석자 명단 공유해줘
english: Can you share the attendee list for next week's meeting?
alt: Who's on the invite list for the meeting next week?
note: attendee list = 참석자 명단

korean: 회의 시간 30분 앞당길 수 있나요?
english: Can we move the meeting up by 30 minutes?
alt: Is it possible to bump the meeting up half an hour?
note: move up / bump up = 앞당기다

korean: 이 안건은 다수결로 결정하죠
english: Let's decide this by majority vote.
alt: Let's go with the majority on this one.
note: majority vote = 다수결

korean: 화상회의 중에 배경 소음이 너무 심해요
english: There's too much background noise during the video call.
alt: The background noise is really distracting on the call.
note: background noise = 배경 소음

korean: 회의 끝나고 액션 아이템 정리해서 보내줄게
english: After the meeting, I'll compile the action items and send them out.
alt: I'll wrap up the to-dos and share them after the call.
note: compile = 정리하다. action items = 실행 과제

korean: 프로젝트 킥오프 미팅 언제 하기로 했지?
english: When did we say the project kickoff meeting would be?
alt: What date was the kickoff set for?
note: kickoff meeting = 프로젝트 시작 회의

korean: 이 작업 ETA가 어떻게 돼?
english: What's the ETA on this task?
alt: When do you think you'll have this done?
note: ETA = Estimated Time of Arrival = 예상 완료 시간

korean: 리소스가 부족해서 외부 인력이 필요해
english: We're short on resources so we need to bring in outside help.
alt: We don't have enough bandwidth. We need external support.
note: bandwidth = 여력/리소스. external = 외부

korean: 마감 연장 요청해도 될까?
english: Can I request a deadline extension?
alt: Would it be possible to push back the due date?
note: deadline extension = 마감 연장. push back = 미루다

korean: 이거 어제까지였는데 왜 아직 안 됐어?
english: This was due yesterday. Why isn't it done yet?
alt: You missed the deadline on this. What happened?
note: due = 마감인. miss the deadline = 마감을 놓치다

korean: 위클리 리포트 금요일까지 제출이에요
english: The weekly report is due by Friday.
alt: Make sure the weekly report is submitted by end of day Friday.
note: weekly report = 주간 보고서. due by = ~까지

korean: 상사한테 안 좋은 소식 전하기가 어렵다
english: It's hard to deliver bad news to your boss.
alt: Breaking bad news to management is never easy.
note: deliver / break bad news = 나쁜 소식을 전하다

korean: 오늘 칼퇴하고 싶은데 눈치 보여
english: I want to leave on time today, but I feel like people are watching.
alt: I'd love to clock out at 6 but the office culture makes it awkward.
note: clock out = 퇴근하다. office culture = 사내 문화

korean: 커피 한잔 하면서 얘기할까?
english: Wanna grab coffee and chat?
alt: How about we talk over coffee?
note: grab coffee = 커피 마시러 가다

korean: 신입 교육 누가 담당해?
english: Who's in charge of onboarding the new hires?
alt: Who handles new employee training?
note: onboarding = 신입 교육/적응 과정

korean: 이 프로젝트 예산이 얼마나 돼?
english: What's the budget for this project?
alt: How much funding do we have for this project?
note: budget / funding = 예산

korean: 업무량 좀 줄여주실 수 있나요? 감당이 안 돼요
english: Could you lighten my workload a bit? I'm overwhelmed.
alt: I'm stretched too thin. Can we redistribute some of my tasks?
note: overwhelmed = 감당 안 되는. stretched thin = 한계

korean: 그 건은 위에 보고 드린 후에 진행하겠습니다
english: I'll proceed after reporting to management.
alt: Let me run it up the chain before moving forward.
note: run it up the chain = 상부에 보고하다

korean: 팀 회식 언제 할까? 다들 언제 돼?
english: When should we do the team dinner? When is everyone free?
alt: Let's plan a team outing. What dates work for everyone?
note: team dinner / outing = 회식/팀 모임

korean: 이번 달 야근이 너무 많아 워라밸이 엉망이야
english: I've been working way too much overtime this month. My work-life balance is shot.
alt: Too many late nights this month. My WLB is completely off.
note: work-life balance (WLB) = 일과 삶의 균형

korean: 면접에서 5년 후 계획을 물어보면 뭐라고 해?
english: What do you say when they ask where you see yourself in five years?
alt: How do you answer the 'five-year plan' question in an interview?
note: five-year plan = 5년 후 계획 (면접 단골)

korean: 이 포지션 경쟁률이 높다던데
english: I heard this position is really competitive.
alt: The competition for this role is apparently fierce.
note: competitive / fierce = 경쟁이 치열한

korean: 수습 기간 중에 해고될 수도 있어?
english: Can you get fired during the probation period?
alt: Is it possible to be let go during probation?
note: let go = 해고되다. probation = 수습 기간

korean: 연봉 테이블이 공개되어 있어?
english: Is the salary structure transparent?
alt: Are the pay bands publicly available?
note: salary structure / pay bands = 연봉 체계/급여 등급

korean: 경조사비 회사에서 지원해줘?
english: Does the company cover condolence and celebration expenses?
alt: Is there a company allowance for family events?
note: condolence = 조의. celebration = 축하

korean: 통근 시간이 너무 길어서 이사할까 고민 중이야
english: My commute is so long, I'm thinking about moving closer.
alt: The commute is killing me. I might need to relocate.
note: commute = 통근. relocate = 이사하다

korean: 그 프로젝트 진척이 좀 더디네
english: Progress on that project is a bit slow.
alt: That project isn't moving as fast as I'd like.
note: progress is slow = 진척이 느린

korean: 이 작업 자동화할 수 있을 것 같은데
english: I think we can automate this task.
alt: This seems like something we could automate.
note: automate = 자동화하다

korean: 예산 초과할 것 같은데 어떡하지?
english: We might go over budget. What should we do?
alt: It looks like we're going to exceed the budget.
note: go over budget / exceed budget = 예산 초과

korean: 외부 업체랑 계약 조건 조율 중이야
english: I'm negotiating the contract terms with the vendor.
alt: We're in talks with the vendor about contract details.
note: negotiate = 협상하다. vendor = 외부 업체

korean: 이번 릴리즈 날짜 확정됐어?
english: Has the release date been finalized?
alt: Is the launch date locked in?
note: release / launch date = 출시일. lock in = 확정

korean: QA 테스트 끝났어?
english: Is QA testing done?
alt: Have we finished quality assurance?
note: QA = Quality Assurance = 품질 보증

korean: 이 기능 MVP에 포함시킬까 말까?
english: Should we include this feature in the MVP?
alt: Is this feature in scope for the MVP?
note: MVP = Minimum Viable Product = 최소 기능 제품

korean: 회고에서 나온 개선점 잘 반영하고 있어?
english: Are we actually implementing the improvements from the retro?
alt: Have we followed through on the retrospective feedback?
note: retro = retrospective. follow through = 끝까지 이행

korean: 출장 때 렌터카 빌려야 하나?
english: Do I need to rent a car for the business trip?
alt: Should I get a rental for the trip?
note: rent a car / rental = 렌터카

korean: 해외 출장 시 환율 적용은 어떻게 해?
english: How do exchange rates work for international trip expenses?
alt: What exchange rate do we use for overseas travel claims?
note: exchange rate = 환율. travel claims = 출장비 청구

korean: 클라이언트 방문 전에 미리 연락했어?
english: Did you reach out to the client before the visit?
alt: Have you contacted the client ahead of the meeting?
note: reach out / contact = 연락하다. ahead of = 전에

korean: 출장 일비가 하루에 얼마야?
english: What's the daily per diem for business trips?
alt: How much is the daily allowance?
note: per diem / daily allowance = 일비

korean: 재택근무 신청은 몇 일 전에 해야 해?
english: How far in advance do I need to request WFH?
alt: What's the notice period for remote work requests?
note: notice period = 사전 통보 기간

korean: 슬랙 상태 좀 업데이트해줘 재택인지 출근인지
english: Update your Slack status so we know if you're remote or in-office.
alt: Set your Slack status, please. Are you WFH or in?
note: status update = 상태 업데이트

korean: 재택할 때 집중하려면 루틴이 중요해
english: Having a routine is key to staying focused when you WFH.
alt: Sticking to a schedule really helps with remote productivity.
note: routine = 루틴. productivity = 생산성

korean: 이번 분기 목표 중에 뭐가 제일 중요해?
english: Which of this quarter's goals is the top priority?
alt: What's the number one objective for Q this quarter?
note: top priority = 최우선. objective = 목표

korean: 정량 평가랑 정성 평가 비율이 어떻게 돼?
english: What's the ratio between quantitative and qualitative evaluation?
alt: How much weight is given to hard metrics vs. soft skills?
note: quantitative = 정량. qualitative = 정성

korean: 이직 시장이 요즘 안 좋다던데
english: I heard the job market isn't great right now.
alt: Apparently the hiring landscape is tough these days.
note: job market / hiring landscape = 채용 시장

korean: 경력 기술서 어떻게 써야 해?
english: How should I write my career description?
alt: What's the best way to outline my professional experience?
note: career description = 경력 기술서

korean: 네트워킹이 중요하다고 하는데 어떻게 시작해?
english: They say networking is important but how do I start?
alt: I know I should network more but where do I begin?
note: networking = 인맥 쌓기

korean: 멘토가 있으면 좋을 텐데
english: It'd be great to have a mentor.
alt: I wish I had someone to mentor me.
note: mentor = 멘토. mentorship = 멘토링

korean: 이 회사 기업문화가 어떤지 궁금해
english: I'm curious about the company culture here.
alt: What's the work culture like at this company?
note: company culture = 기업문화

korean: 복장 규정이 있어? 캐주얼 돼?
english: Is there a dress code? Can I dress casual?
alt: What's the dress policy? Is business casual okay?
note: dress code = 복장 규정. business casual = 비즈니스 캐주얼

korean: 이메일 말고 노션에 정리해서 공유해줄래?
english: Instead of email can you organize it in Notion and share?
alt: Can you put it in a Notion doc instead of emailing?
note: Notion = 협업 도구. organize = 정리하다

korean: 이 업무 매뉴얼 좀 만들어놓자 다음 사람도 쓸 수 있게
english: Let's create a manual for this so the next person can use it.
alt: We should document this process for future reference.
note: manual = 매뉴얼. document = 문서화하다

korean: 사수가 없어서 혼자 알아서 해야 해
english: I don't have a senior to guide me so I have to figure things out on my own.
alt: There's no one training me. I'm pretty much self-taught here.
note: figure out on my own = 혼자 알아내다

korean: 회의가 너무 많아서 일할 시간이 없어
english: I have so many meetings I barely have time to do actual work.
alt: Meeting overload is killing my productivity.
note: meeting overload = 회의 과다. productivity = 생산성

korean: 그거 내 R&R이 아닌데 왜 나한테 시키지?
english: That's not in my job description. Why am I being asked to do it?
alt: That's outside my role and responsibilities. Why me?
note: R&R = Roles & Responsibilities. job description = 직무기술서

korean: 이번 프로젝트 PM이 누구야?
english: Who's the PM for this project?
alt: Who's leading the project?
note: PM = Project Manager = 프로젝트 관리자

korean: 이해관계자 정리 좀 해줘 누가 관련되어 있는지
english: Can you map out the stakeholders? I need to know who's involved.
alt: Let's identify all the stakeholders on this.
note: stakeholder = 이해관계자

korean: 출장비 신청 양식 어디 있어?
english: Where can I find the travel expense claim form?
alt: Where's the template for submitting travel expenses?
note: expense claim form = 경비 청구서

korean: 클라이언트가 미팅을 갑자기 취소했어
english: The client cancelled the meeting last minute.
alt: The client pulled out of the meeting with no warning.
note: cancel last minute = 직전에 취소하다

korean: 재택 중에 슬랙 반응 좀 빨리 해줘
english: Try to respond faster on Slack when you're working remotely.
alt: Keep your Slack response time tight while WFH.
note: response time = 반응 시간

korean: 성과 평가 때 구체적인 수치로 보여주는 게 좋아
english: It's better to show your achievements with concrete numbers during reviews.
alt: Back up your performance with data and metrics at evaluation time.
note: concrete numbers = 구체적 수치. metrics = 지표

korean: 하반기 목표는 상반기 결과를 보고 조정하자
english: Let's adjust our second-half goals based on first-half results.
alt: We'll recalibrate H2 targets once we review H1 performance.
note: recalibrate = 재조정. H1/H2 = 상/하반기

korean: 퇴직 후에도 건강보험 유지할 수 있어?
english: Can I keep my health insurance after leaving the company?
alt: Is there a way to extend health coverage after resignation?
note: health coverage = 건강보험 혜택

korean: 경력직은 바로 실무 투입이라 적응이 빨라야 해
english: Experienced hires are expected to hit the ground running.
alt: Mid-career joiners need to get up to speed fast.
note: hit the ground running = 바로 성과를 내다

korean: 조직 개편이 있을 수도 있대
english: There might be a restructuring coming up.
alt: I heard there could be an organizational reshuffle.
note: restructuring / reshuffle = 조직 개편

korean: 이 프로젝트 ROI가 충분할까?
english: Will the ROI on this project be worth it?
alt: Is the return on investment strong enough to justify this?
note: ROI = Return on Investment = 투자 수익률

korean: 업무 효율을 높이려면 프로세스를 개선해야 해
english: We need to improve our processes to boost efficiency.
alt: Streamlining our workflow would really help productivity.
note: streamline = 간소화하다. boost = 높이다

korean: 이번 건은 크로스펑셔널 팀으로 진행하자
english: Let's handle this with a cross-functional team.
alt: We need people from different departments on this one.
note: cross-functional = 부서 간 협업

korean: 데일리 스탠드업 시간 10분만 하자
english: Let's keep the daily stand-up to 10 minutes.
alt: Daily sync should be 10 minutes max.
note: daily stand-up / sync = 매일 짧은 회의

korean: 고객 피드백 기반으로 개선점 정리해볼게
english: I'll compile improvement points based on customer feedback.
alt: Let me pull together a list of fixes from user feedback.
note: compile = 정리하다. user feedback = 사용자 피드백

korean: 그 업체 견적서 받았어?
english: Did you get the quote from that vendor?
alt: Has the vendor sent over their estimate yet?
note: quote / estimate = 견적서

korean: 내부 결재 받는 데 시간이 너무 오래 걸려
english: Internal approvals are taking way too long.
alt: The sign-off process is really dragging.
note: approval / sign-off = 결재. drag = 지체되다

korean: 이 건 법무팀 확인 받아야 돼
english: This needs to be reviewed by legal.
alt: We should run this by the legal department.
note: legal = 법무팀. run by = 확인받다

korean: 메일 수신 거부 링크 빠뜨리면 안 돼
english: Don't forget to include the unsubscribe link in the email.
alt: Make sure the opt-out link is in there.
note: unsubscribe / opt-out = 수신 거부

korean: 재택할 때 메신저 응답 시간 기준이 있어?
english: Is there a guideline for messenger response times when WFH?
alt: What's the expected response time on Slack during remote work?
note: response time guideline = 응답 시간 기준

korean: 퇴사할 때 회사 장비 반납해야 해
english: You need to return company equipment when you leave.
alt: Make sure to hand back all company property on your last day.
note: return / hand back = 반납하다

korean: 지식 공유 세션 한번 해볼까?
english: How about we do a knowledge-sharing session?
alt: Want to organize a lunch-and-learn?
note: knowledge-sharing / lunch-and-learn = 지식 공유 세션

korean: 이 회의 정말 이메일로 대체 가능했잖아
english: This meeting really could've been an email.
alt: Did we really need a meeting for this? An email would've sufficed.
note: could've been an email = 이메일로 충분했다 (유명 표현)

korean: 다음 라운드 면접 준비 뭐 하면 돼?
english: What should I prepare for the next round of interviews?
alt: How do I get ready for the second interview?
note: next round = 다음 라운드

korean: 레퍼럴 보너스 있어? 아는 사람 추천하려고
english: Is there a referral bonus? I want to recommend someone.
alt: Do we get anything for employee referrals?
note: referral bonus = 추천 보너스

korean: 유연근무제라서 출퇴근 시간이 자유로워
english: We have flex hours so I can set my own schedule.
alt: The flexible work policy lets me choose my start and end times.
note: flex hours / flexible work = 유연근무

korean: 안식년 제도가 있다는데 조건이 뭐야?
english: I heard there's a sabbatical program. What are the requirements?
alt: What do you need to qualify for a sabbatical?
note: sabbatical = 안식년/장기 휴가

korean: 이번에 팀 리더 맡게 됐어
english: I've been appointed as the team lead.
alt: They put me in charge of the team.
note: team lead = 팀 리더. put in charge = 책임을 맡기다

korean: 업무 자동화 툴 도입하자고 건의해볼까?
english: Should I propose adopting an automation tool?
alt: What if I suggest implementing workflow automation?
note: propose / suggest = 건의하다. automation = 자동화

korean: 이 프로젝트 포스트모템 해야지 같은 실수 반복하면 안 돼
english: We need a post-mortem on this project. We can't repeat the same mistakes.
alt: Let's do a lessons-learned session so we don't make the same errors.
note: post-mortem / lessons learned = 사후 분석/교훈

korean: 그 건은 NDA 걸려 있어서 말 못 해
english: That's under NDA so I can't discuss it.
alt: I'm bound by a non-disclosure agreement on that one.
note: NDA = Non-Disclosure Agreement = 비밀유지계약

korean: 오늘 워크숍 참석해야 하는 거 맞지?
english: I'm supposed to attend the workshop today right?
alt: The workshop is today isn't it? I need to be there?
note: workshop = 워크숍/실습형 세미나

korean: 분기별 올핸즈 미팅 준비해야 해
english: We need to prepare for the quarterly all-hands.
alt: The company-wide meeting is coming up. Let's get ready.
note: all-hands = 전사 미팅. quarterly = 분기별

korean: 이 타스크 지라에 등록해놨어?
english: Did you log this task in Jira?
alt: Is this ticket already in the Jira board?
note: Jira = 프로젝트 관리 도구. ticket = 작업 항목

korean: 야근 대신 내일 일찍 와서 하면 안 될까?
english: Instead of working late can I come in early tomorrow?
alt: Can I skip overtime tonight and start early in the morning instead?
note: work late = 야근. come in early = 일찍 출근

korean: 이번 달 매출 목표 달성 가능성이 어때?
english: What are the chances of hitting this month's sales target?
alt: Do you think we'll make our revenue goal this month?
note: hit / make a target = 목표 달성하다

korean: 연말 성과 발표 자료 만들어야 해
english: I need to put together the year-end performance presentation.
alt: The annual results deck needs to be ready.
note: year-end / annual = 연말. deck = 발표 자료

korean: 그거 결재 라인 타고 올라가야 해서 시간 좀 걸려
english: That needs to go through the approval chain so it'll take some time.
