alt: It has to work its way up the approval ladder. Give it some time.
note: approval chain / ladder = 결재 라인

korean: 하반기 채용 계획 나왔어?
english: Has the second-half hiring plan been released?
alt: Do we have the H2 recruitment roadmap yet?
note: hiring plan / recruitment roadmap = 채용 계획

korean: 이 건으로 야근하게 생겼네
english: Looks like I'm gonna be working late because of this.
alt: This is gonna keep me at the office tonight.
note: work late / stay late = 야근하다

korean: 내일 오전에 외부 미팅 있어서 좀 늦게 올게
english: I have an external meeting tomorrow morning, so I'll be in late.
alt: I've got an off-site in the AM so I'll get to the office a bit late.
note: external / off-site meeting = 외부 미팅

korean: 그 사람 업무 스타일이 나랑 안 맞아서 힘들어
english: Their work style doesn't mesh with mine and it's frustrating.
alt: We have totally different approaches and it's causing friction.
note: mesh = 맞다. friction = 마찰

korean: 결재 서류에 사인해주세요
english: Could you sign off on this document?
alt: I need your signature on the approval form.
note: sign off = 승인하다/서명하다

korean: 그 건 아직 검토 중이에요 조금만 기다려주세요
english: That's still under review. Could you hold on a bit?
alt: We're still looking into it. I'll get back to you shortly.
note: under review = 검토 중. hold on = 기다리다

korean: 다른 부서랑 협업이 잘 안 돼서 답답해
english: Cross-department collaboration isn't working and it's frustrating.
alt: Working with other teams has been a headache.
note: cross-department = 부서 간. headache = 골칫거리

korean: 세미나 참석비 회사에서 지원해줘?
english: Does the company cover seminar attendance fees?
alt: Can I expense the conference registration?
note: expense = 경비 처리하다. registration = 등록비

korean: 이번에 새로운 복지 제도가 생겼대
english: I heard there's a new benefit program being introduced.
alt: Apparently the company's rolling out a new perk.
note: roll out = 도입하다. perk = 복지 혜택

korean: 팀빌딩 활동 뭐 하면 좋을까?
english: What should we do for team building?
alt: Any ideas for a team-building activity?
note: team building = 팀빌딩/팀 결속 활동

korean: 그 파일 클라우드에 올려놨어?
english: Did you upload that file to the cloud?
alt: Is the file on the shared drive?
note: cloud / shared drive = 클라우드/공유 드라이브

korean: 오늘 미팅 노쇼가 두 명이나 있었어
english: Two people were no-shows at today's meeting.
alt: Two people didn't bother showing up to the meeting.
note: no-show = 예고 없이 불참한 사람

korean: 이 업무 SOP 좀 만들어놓자
english: Let's create an SOP for this process.
alt: We should document the standard procedure for this.
note: SOP = Standard Operating Procedure = 표준 운영 절차

korean: 사내 교육 프로그램 신청했어?
english: Did you sign up for the internal training program?
alt: Have you enrolled in the in-house course?
note: internal training / in-house = 사내

korean: 고객사에서 클레임이 들어왔어
english: We got a complaint from the client.
alt: The client filed a claim about the issue.
note: complaint / claim = 클레임/불만

korean: 그 건 컨펌 받았어? 진행해도 돼?
english: Did you get confirmation on that? Can we proceed?
alt: Has that been approved? Are we good to go?
note: confirmation = 확인/승인. good to go = 진행 OK

korean: 야근 식대 청구할 수 있어?
english: Can I claim dinner expenses for working overtime?
alt: Is overtime meal allowance a thing here?
note: meal allowance = 식대. claim = 청구하다

korean: 이번 주 금요일까지 시간 괜찮으세요?
english: Are you available by this Friday?
alt: Does Friday work for your schedule?
note: available = 시간이 되는

korean: 그 파일 버전 관리 좀 하자 헷갈려
english: Let's version-control that file. It's getting confusing.
alt: We need to keep track of file versions. This is messy.
note: version control = 버전 관리

korean: 내가 오늘 발표 대신 해줄까?
english: Want me to present on your behalf today?
alt: I can cover the presentation for you if you need.
note: on your behalf = 대신. cover = 대신하다

korean: 오늘 스프린트 리뷰 있는 거 맞지?
english: We have the sprint review today right?
alt: The sprint demo is today isn't it?
note: sprint review / demo = 스프린트 리뷰/데모

korean: 그 업체 미팅 잘 됐어? 계약할 만해?
english: How did the meeting with the vendor go? Worth signing?
alt: Did the vendor meeting go well? Are we moving forward?
note: vendor = 업체. move forward = 진행하다

korean: 휴가 복귀했는데 밀린 메일이 200통이야
english: I just got back from vacation and I have 200 unread emails.
alt: Coming back from PTO to 200 emails is brutal.
note: brutal = 힘든/가혹한 (캐주얼)

korean: 그 결정은 C레벨에서 내려야 해
english: That decision needs to come from the C-suite.
alt: That's a C-level call. Not ours to make.
note: C-suite / C-level = 최고 경영진 (CEO/CFO 등)

korean: 이번 주는 공휴일이 껴서 근무일이 짧아
english: This week's shorter because of the holiday.
alt: We have a short week thanks to the public holiday.
note: public holiday = 공휴일. short week = 짧은 근무 주

korean: 업무 관련 자격증 따면 수당 나와?
english: Do I get an allowance for getting work-related certifications?
alt: Is there a bonus for earning professional certificates?
note: certification = 자격증. allowance = 수당

korean: 그 문서 대외비라서 공유하면 안 돼
english: That document is confidential. You can't share it externally.
alt: That's classified as internal only. Don't circulate it.
note: confidential = 기밀. internal only = 대외비

korean: 새 시스템 도입에 적응하는 데 시간이 좀 걸리겠네
english: It'll take some time to adjust to the new system.
alt: Getting used to the new platform will need a transition period.
note: adjust / get used to = 적응하다. transition = 전환

korean: 프리랜서로 전환할까 고민 중이야
english: I'm thinking about going freelance.
alt: I'm considering becoming an independent contractor.
note: freelance / independent contractor = 프리랜서/독립 계약자

korean: 업무 인수인계서 작성 시작해야지
english: I need to start writing up the handover document.
alt: Time to begin drafting the transition notes.
note: handover document / transition notes = 인수인계서

korean: 그 프로젝트 최종 산출물 제출했어?
english: Did you submit the final deliverables for that project?
alt: Have the final project outputs been turned in?
note: deliverables = 산출물. turn in = 제출하다

korean: 이 건은 이메일보다 대면으로 얘기하는 게 나을 것 같아
english: I think it's better to discuss this face-to-face rather than over email.
alt: This is the kind of thing we should talk about in person.
note: face-to-face / in person = 대면으로

korean: 다음 주 월요일에 올핸즈 미팅 있어서 자료 준비해야 해
english: There's an all-hands on Monday so I need to prep my materials.
alt: I've got to get my presentation ready for the company meeting Monday.
note: all-hands = 전사 미팅. prep = 준비하다
category: 회사그 보고서 수치 다시 한번 확인해봐 틀린 것 같아
column7: Double-check the numbers in that report. I think there's an error.
column8: The figures look off. Can you verify them?

korean: 이메일 뉴스레터 발송 일정 확정됐어?
english: Has the email newsletter schedule been finalized?
alt: Is the send date for the newsletter locked in?
note: newsletter = 뉴스레터. lock in = 확정하다

korean: 이 메일에 답장할 때 전체 답장으로 해줘
english: When you reply to this use reply-all.
alt: Make sure to hit reply-all on this one.
note: reply-all = 전체 답장

korean: 회의 중에 질문은 마지막에 받겠습니다
english: I'll take questions at the end of the meeting.
alt: Let's save questions for the end, shall we?
note: save questions for the end = 질문은 마지막에

korean: 이번 미팅은 워킹 세션으로 진행하죠
english: Let's run this meeting as a working session.
alt: How about we make this a hands-on working meeting?
note: working session = 실무 작업 회의

korean: 프로젝트 간트 차트 업데이트 좀 해줘
english: Can you update the Gantt chart for the project?
alt: The project timeline chart needs refreshing.
note: Gantt chart = 간트 차트 (일정 관리 도구)

korean: 이 이슈 에스컬레이션 해야 할 것 같아
english: I think we need to escalate this issue.
alt: This needs to be bumped up to a higher level.
note: escalate = 상위로 올리다. bump up = 격상시키다

korean: 동료 평가 솔직히 쓰기 좀 부담스러워
english: Peer reviews are always awkward to write honestly.
alt: Being candid in peer evaluations feels uncomfortable.
note: peer review = 동료 평가. candid = 솔직한

korean: 그 건 품의서 올려야 진행 가능해
english: We need to submit a purchase request before we can proceed.
alt: That requires an internal approval form to move forward.
note: purchase request / approval form = 품의서

korean: 내일 아침 일찍 컨퍼런스 콜 있어서 좀 피곤하겠다
english: I have an early morning conference call tomorrow so I'll be beat.
alt: There's a 7 AM call tomorrow. I'm already dreading it.
note: conference call = 전화 회의. beat = 지친

korean: 이 작업 파이프라인에 넣어놨어?
english: Is this task in the pipeline?
alt: Has this been added to the workflow?
note: pipeline = 작업 흐름/파이프라인

korean: 클라이언트 요구사항이 자꾸 바뀌어서 힘들어
english: The client keeps changing requirements and it's exhausting.
alt: Constant scope changes from the client are draining.
note: requirements = 요구사항. draining = 지치게 하는

korean: 그 제안서 마감이 오늘이야 서둘러
english: The proposal deadline is today. Hurry up.
alt: The RFP response is due today. Let's hustle.
note: proposal = 제안서. RFP = Request for Proposal

korean: 재택근무할 때 업무 시작/종료 시간 공유해줘
english: Let me know your start and end times when you're WFH.
alt: Share your working hours when you're remote.
note: working hours = 근무 시간

korean: 이번 인사이동으로 부서가 바뀔 수도 있대
english: The upcoming personnel changes might move me to a different department.
alt: I could get transferred to another team with the reshuffle.
note: personnel changes = 인사이동. transfer = 이동

korean: 퇴사 전에 남은 연차 다 소진해야 해
english: I need to use up all my remaining PTO before I leave.
alt: Make sure to burn through your leftover vacation days before your last day.
note: use up / burn through = 소진하다

korean: 커리어 코칭 받아본 적 있어?
english: Have you ever done career coaching?
alt: Have you tried working with a career coach?
note: career coaching = 커리어 코칭

korean: 이직 제안을 받았는데 현 직장이랑 고민 중이야
english: I got a job offer, but I'm torn between that and staying.
alt: I'm weighing a new opportunity against my current position.
note: torn between = 사이에서 고민하는. weigh = 저울질하다

korean: 연봉 외에 복지 패키지도 비교해봐야 해
english: You should compare the benefits package too not just the salary.
alt: Don't just look at the base pay. Factor in the total comp.
note: benefits package = 복지 패키지. total comp = 총 보상

korean: 출장 중에 개인 일정 좀 넣어도 되나?
english: Is it okay to squeeze in some personal time during the business trip?
alt: Can I take a bit of personal time while I'm on the road?
note: on the road = 출장 중. squeeze in = 시간을 내다

korean: 이 건은 우리 팀 소관이 아니야 다른 팀에 넘겨
english: This isn't our team's jurisdiction. Pass it to another team.
alt: That falls under a different team's purview. Redirect it.
note: jurisdiction / purview = 소관/관할

korean: 수습 끝나면 연봉 재협상 가능한 거야?
english: Can I renegotiate my salary after probation ends?
alt: Is there room for a pay adjustment once I'm past probation?
note: renegotiate = 재협상. pay adjustment = 급여 조정

korean: 사내 동호회 가입하면 지원금 나와?
english: Do we get funding if we join a company club?
alt: Is there a subsidy for internal club activities?
note: company club = 사내 동호회. subsidy = 지원금

korean: 그 건 구두로만 합의했으니까 서면으로 남기자
english: We only agreed verbally, so let's put it in writing.
alt: That was just a verbal agreement. We should formalize it.
note: verbal = 구두의. put in writing = 서면으로 남기다

korean: 내 업무 범위가 점점 넓어지고 있어 직급은 그대로인데
english: My responsibilities keep growing but my title hasn't changed.
alt: I'm doing way more than my role requires without a title bump.
note: responsibilities = 업무 범위. title bump = 직급 상승

korean: 올해 워크숍 주제가 뭐야?
english: What's the theme for this year's workshop?
alt: Do we know the topic for the annual workshop?
note: theme / topic = 주제

korean: 타 부서 지원 요청하려면 어떻게 해야 해?
english: How do I request support from another department?
alt: What's the process for getting help from a different team?
note: request support = 지원 요청하다

korean: 이번 달 근태 정리 좀 해줘
english: Can you clean up this month's attendance records?
alt: Please finalize the attendance log for this month.
note: attendance records = 근태 기록

korean: 사무실 이전 얘기가 나오고 있어
english: There's talk about relocating the office.
alt: Rumor has it the office might move.
note: relocate = 이전하다. rumor has it = 소문에 의하면

korean: 그 미팅 아젠다에 내 발표도 넣어줘
english: Add my presentation to the meeting agenda.
alt: Can you put me on the agenda? I need to present something.
note: agenda = 안건 목록. put me on = 나를 넣어줘

korean: 지금 쓰고 있는 툴이 좀 불편한데 다른 걸로 바꾸자
english: The tool we're using isn't great. Let's switch to something better.
alt: This software is clunky. Can we explore alternatives?
note: clunky = 불편한. explore alternatives = 대안 찾다

korean: 보안 교육 이수했어? 이번 달까지야
english: Did you complete the security training? It's due this month.
alt: Have you finished the mandatory security course yet?
note: security training = 보안 교육. mandatory = 필수

korean: 그 프로젝트 예산 승인 나왔어?
english: Has the project budget been approved?
alt: Did we get the green light on the budget?
note: get the green light = 승인받다

korean: 이번 분기 핵심 이니셔티브가 뭐야?
english: What are the key initiatives for this quarter?
alt: What's the main focus area for Q this quarter?
note: initiative = 이니셔티브/주요 사업

korean: 회사에서 자기 개발비 지원해줘?
english: Does the company offer a professional development budget?
alt: Is there a learning and development allowance?
note: professional development = 자기 개발. allowance = 수당

korean: 그 클라이언트 미팅 회의록 공유해줘
english: Can you share the minutes from the client meeting?
alt: Send me the notes from the client call.
note: minutes / notes = 회의록

korean: 이메일 서명에 직함이랑 연락처 넣어줘
english: Include your title and contact info in your email signature.
alt: Make sure your sig has your job title and phone number.
note: title = 직함. contact info = 연락처

korean: 새 프로젝트 온보딩 자료 있어?
english: Are there onboarding materials for the new project?
alt: Is there a starter pack for getting up to speed on the project?
note: onboarding materials = 온보딩/적응 자료

korean: 이번 성과 리뷰에서 연봉 인상 요청해볼 거야
english: I'm going to ask for a raise during my performance review.
alt: I plan to negotiate a salary increase at my evaluation.
note: ask for a raise = 연봉 인상 요청

korean: 퇴사 후에도 연락하고 지내자
english: Let's keep in touch after I leave.
alt: Stay connected even after I'm gone okay?
note: keep in touch / stay connected = 연락하고 지내다

korean: 업무용 메신저에서 이모지로 답하면 실례야?
english: Is it rude to just react with an emoji on work messenger?
alt: Is an emoji reaction unprofessional in a work chat?
note: emoji reaction = 이모지 반응. unprofessional = 비전문적

korean: 이번 제안서 방향 잡아야 하는데 같이 브레인스토밍 할래?
english: I need to figure out the direction for this proposal. Want to brainstorm together?
alt: Let's put our heads together on the proposal direction.
note: put our heads together = 머리를 맞대다

korean: 기획안 초안 언제까지 나올 수 있어?
english: When can the first draft of the plan be ready?
alt: What's the timeline for the initial proposal draft?
note: first draft = 초안. timeline = 일정

korean: 이 기획서 시장 분석 부분이 좀 약한 것 같아
english: The market analysis section of this proposal feels a bit weak.
alt: I think we need to beef up the market research portion.
note: beef up = 강화하다. market analysis = 시장 분석

korean: 경쟁사 분석 자료 정리해놨어?
english: Have you put together the competitor analysis?
alt: Is the competitive landscape report ready?
note: competitor analysis = 경쟁사 분석. competitive landscape = 경쟁 환경

korean: 타겟 고객층을 좀 더 명확하게 정의하자
english: Let's define the target audience more clearly.
alt: We need to narrow down our target demographic.
note: target audience = 타겟 고객. demographic = 인구통계학적 대상

korean: 이 아이디어 사업성이 있을까?
english: Is this idea commercially viable?
alt: Does this concept have real business potential?
note: commercially viable = 사업성 있는. business potential = 사업 가능성

korean: 벤치마킹 자료 좀 모아줄래?
english: Can you gather some benchmarking data?
alt: Could you pull together some reference cases from competitors?
note: benchmarking = 벤치마킹. reference cases = 참고 사례

korean: 이 프로젝트 사업계획서 작성해야 해
english: I need to write a business plan for this project.
alt: We should draft a business proposal for this initiative.
note: business plan = 사업계획서. initiative = 사업/프로젝트

korean: SWOT 분석 해봤어?
english: Have you done a SWOT analysis?
alt: Did you run a strengths-weaknesses-opportunities-threats assessment?
note: SWOT = Strengths Weaknesses Opportunities Threats

korean: 이 기획은 실현 가능성이 낮아 보여
english: This plan doesn't seem very feasible.
alt: I'm not sure this is realistic given our constraints.
note: feasible = 실현 가능한. constraints = 제약 조건

korean: 발표 자료 몇 장 정도로 만들면 될까?
english: How many slides should the presentation be?
alt: What's the ideal slide count for the deck?
note: slide count = 슬라이드 수. deck = 발표 자료

korean: 발표 리허설 한 번 하고 가자
english: Let's do a dry run before the actual presentation.
alt: We should rehearse the presentation at least once.
note: dry run = 리허설/예행연습

korean: 슬라이드에 글자가 너무 많아 줄여야 해
english: There's too much text on the slides. We need to cut it down.
alt: The slides are text-heavy. Let's simplify them.
note: text-heavy = 글자가 많은. simplify = 간소화하다

korean: 발표 시간이 15분인데 초과하면 안 돼
english: The presentation is 15 minutes. We can't go over.
alt: Keep it within the 15-minute time limit.
note: go over = 초과하다. time limit = 시간 제한

korean: 차트랑 그래프를 좀 더 넣어서 시각화하자
english: Let's add more charts and graphs to make it more visual.
alt: We should visualize the data better with more graphics.
note: visualize = 시각화하다. graphics = 그래픽/도표

korean: 발표 도중에 질문 받을까 아니면 끝나고 받을까?
english: Should I take questions during or after the presentation?
alt: Do we want a Q&A during or at the end?
note: Q&A = Questions and Answers = 질의응답

korean: 핵심 메시지가 뭔지 명확하게 전달해야 해
english: You need to deliver the key message clearly.
alt: Make sure the main takeaway comes through loud and clear.
note: key message = 핵심 메시지. takeaway = 핵심 요점

korean: 이 슬라이드 순서를 좀 바꾸는 게 나을 것 같아
english: I think we should rearrange the order of these slides.
alt: The flow would be better if we shuffled the slides around.
note: rearrange = 재배열. flow = 흐름

korean: 발표할 때 너무 빨리 말하지 마 천천히 또박또박
english: Don't speak too fast during the presentation. Slow down and enunciate.
alt: Pace yourself and articulate clearly when presenting.
note: enunciate / articulate = 또박또박 말하다. pace yourself = 속도 조절하다

korean: 청중의 관심을 끌 수 있는 오프닝이 필요해
english: We need an opening that grabs the audience's attention.
alt: Start with a hook that pulls the audience in.
note: hook = 관심을 끄는 요소. grab attention = 관심을 끌다

korean: 프레젠테이션 마지막에 콜 투 액션을 넣자
english: Let's include a call to action at the end of the presentation.
alt: End with a clear CTA so they know the next step.
note: CTA = Call To Action = 행동 유도

korean: 발표 자료에 회사 로고 넣었어?
english: Did you include the company logo in the deck?
alt: Make sure the corporate branding is on the slides.
note: branding = 브랜딩. deck = 발표 자료

korean: 데모 시연 부분은 내가 할게
english: I'll handle the demo portion.
alt: Let me take over for the live demonstration.
note: demo = 시연. live demonstration = 실시간 시연

korean: 백업 슬라이드 준비해둬 혹시 질문 대비용으로
english: Prepare backup slides in case there are tough questions.
alt: Have some appendix slides ready for potential follow-up questions.
note: backup / appendix slides = 백업/부록 슬라이드

korean: 발표 후 피드백이 좋았어 반응이 괜찮았대
english: The feedback after the presentation was positive. People liked it.
alt: The response to the pitch was great. Good reception overall.
note: reception = 반응. pitch = 발표/프레젠테이션

korean: 이번 계약 조건 좀 더 유리하게 협상할 수 있을까?
english: Can we negotiate better terms on this contract?
alt: Is there room to push for more favorable conditions?
note: negotiate = 협상하다. favorable = 유리한

korean: 상대방이 가격을 너무 높게 불러서 조율이 필요해
english: The other party quoted too high. We need to negotiate down.
alt: Their asking price is steep. We need to bring them down.
note: quote = 견적을 내다. steep = 높은/가파른

korean: 협상 여지가 있어? 아니면 최종 제안이야?
english: Is there room for negotiation or is this the final offer?
alt: Is this their best and final or can we counter?
note: final offer = 최종 제안. counter = 역제안하다

korean: 윈윈할 수 있는 방향으로 가자
english: Let's find a win-win solution.
alt: Let's aim for a mutually beneficial outcome.
note: win-win = 상호 이익. mutually beneficial = 상호 유리한

korean: 이 조건은 우리한테 불리해서 수정 요청할게
english: These terms are disadvantageous for us. I'll request a revision.
alt: This clause doesn't work in our favor. I'll push back.
note: disadvantageous = 불리한. push back = 반대/수정 요청하다

korean: 양보할 수 있는 부분이 있으면 알려줘
english: Let me know what we can concede on.
alt: What are we willing to give ground on?
note: concede = 양보하다. give ground = 양보하다

korean: 대안이 없으면 협상력이 떨어져
english: Without alternatives we lose bargaining power.
alt: You need a Plan B or you have no leverage.
note: bargaining power = 협상력. leverage = 영향력/지렛대

korean: 상대방이 양보 안 하면 우리도 물러서지 말자
english: If they won't budge, we shouldn't either.
alt: If they hold firm we hold firm too.
note: budge = 양보하다. hold firm = 입장을 고수하다

korean: 가격 말고 다른 조건으로 협상해보자
english: Let's negotiate on other terms besides the price.
alt: Instead of haggling on price let's look at other levers.
note: haggle = 흥정하다. levers = 협상 수단

korean: 이번 딜은 꼭 성사시켜야 해
english: We absolutely need to close this deal.
alt: This deal has to go through no matter what.
note: close a deal = 거래를 성사시키다

korean: 납기일을 좀 늦출 수 있는지 물어봐줘
english: Can you ask if they can push the delivery date back?
alt: See if there's flexibility on the delivery timeline.
note: delivery date = 납기일. flexibility = 유연성

korean: MOU 먼저 체결하고 세부 조건은 나중에 정하자
english: Let's sign the MOU first and work out the details later.
alt: We can lock in an MOU and finalize specifics down the road.
note: MOU = Memorandum of Understanding = 양해각서

korean: 계약서 검토 법무팀한테 넘겼어?
english: Did you send the contract to legal for review?
alt: Has the legal team looked over the agreement yet?
note: legal = 법무팀. agreement = 계약서

korean: 독점 계약 조건이 좀 부담스러운데
english: The exclusivity clause feels a bit heavy.
alt: I'm not comfortable with the exclusive terms.
note: exclusivity clause = 독점 조항. exclusive = 독점의

korean: 위약금 조항 확인했어? 꼼꼼히 봐야 해
english: Did you check the penalty clause? You need to read it carefully.
alt: Make sure to scrutinize the breach of contract penalties.
note: penalty clause = 위약금 조항. scrutinize = 꼼꼼히 살피다

korean: 면접 준비 어떻게 하고 있어?
english: How are you preparing for the interview?
alt: What's your interview prep strategy?
note: interview prep = 면접 준비

korean: 면접에서 예상 질문 리스트 좀 만들어봐
english: Make a list of expected interview questions.
alt: Prep a list of likely questions they'll ask.
note: expected questions = 예상 질문

korean: 면접관한테 좋은 첫인상을 남기는 게 중요해
english: Making a good first impression on the interviewer is key.
alt: First impressions really matter in interviews.
note: first impression = 첫인상

korean: 화상 면접이라 배경이랑 조명 신경 써야 해
english: It's a video interview so pay attention to your background and lighting.
alt: Make sure your setup looks professional for the virtual interview.
note: virtual interview = 화상 면접. setup = 환경 설정

korean: 면접 후에 감사 이메일 보내는 게 좋아
english: It's a good idea to send a thank-you email after the interview.
alt: Following up with a thank-you note shows professionalism.
note: thank-you note = 감사 이메일. follow up = 후속 조치

korean: 그룹 면접이라 긴장이 더 돼
english: It's a group interview so I'm even more nervous.
alt: Panel interviews make me way more anxious.
note: group / panel interview = 그룹/패널 면접

korean: 케이스 면접 준비 많이 해야 할 것 같아
english: I think I need to do a lot of case interview prep.
alt: Case studies are a big part of their process so study up.
note: case interview = 케이스 면접 (컨설팅 등)

korean: 면접 때 연봉 얘기 먼저 꺼내도 돼?
english: Is it okay to bring up salary first in the interview?
alt: Should I mention compensation during the interview?
note: bring up = 꺼내다. compensation = 보상/급여

korean: 인성 면접이랑 실무 면접 따로 있대
english: There are separate behavioral and technical interviews.
alt: They do a cultural fit round and a skills-based round.
note: behavioral = 인성. technical = 실무. cultural fit = 문화 적합성

korean: 면접 복장은 비즈니스 캐주얼이면 될까?
english: Is business casual appropriate for the interview?
alt: Would smart casual be okay or should I go full formal?
note: business casual = 비즈니스 캐주얼. smart casual = 세미 캐주얼

korean: 연봉 인상 요청하려면 근거 자료가 있어야 해
english: You need supporting data if you want to ask for a raise.
alt: Come prepared with evidence when negotiating your salary.
note: supporting data = 근거 자료. evidence = 증거

korean: 시장 평균 연봉이랑 비교해봤어?
english: Have you compared it with the market average salary?
alt: Did you benchmark your pay against industry standards?
note: market average = 시장 평균. benchmark = 벤치마킹하다

korean: 기본급 외에 성과급 비율이 어떻게 돼?
english: What's the ratio of base pay to performance bonuses?
alt: How much of the total comp is variable pay?
note: base pay = 기본급. variable pay = 변동 급여/성과급

korean: 연봉 테이블이 투명하게 공개되면 좋겠어
english: I wish the salary bands were more transparent.
alt: It'd be nice if pay scales were publicly shared.
note: salary bands / pay scales = 급여 등급표

korean: 세전 연봉이랑 세후 실수령액 차이가 크네
english: The gap between gross and net salary is huge.
alt: There's a big difference between pre-tax and take-home pay.
note: gross = 세전. net / take-home = 세후 실수령

korean: 복리후생까지 포함하면 총 보상 패키지가 괜찮아
english: The total compensation package is decent when you include benefits.
alt: Factor in the perks and the total comp isn't bad.
note: total compensation = 총 보상. perks = 복리후생

korean: 연봉 동결이면 사실상 삭감이나 마찬가지야
english: A salary freeze is essentially a pay cut in real terms.
alt: No raise with inflation means you're earning less.
note: salary freeze = 연봉 동결. in real terms = 실질적으로

korean: 호봉제라서 매년 자동으로 올라가
english: It's a seniority-based pay system so it goes up automatically each year.
alt: Pay increases are automatic based on years of service here.
note: seniority-based = 호봉제. years of service = 근속 연수

korean: 스톡옵션 베스팅 기간이 4년이래
english: The stock option vesting period is four years.
alt: Equity vests over a four-year cliff.
note: vesting period = 베스팅 기간. cliff = 최소 근무 기간

korean: 올해 보너스 몇 퍼센트 나왔어?
english: What percentage was the bonus this year?
alt: How much did we get for the annual bonus?
note: annual bonus = 연간 보너스

korean: 승진 심사 기준이 뭔지 정확히 모르겠어
english: I'm not entirely sure what the promotion review criteria are.
alt: I don't have a clear picture of what it takes to get promoted.
note: promotion review = 승진 심사. criteria = 기준

korean: 승진하려면 성과만으로는 안 되고 리더십도 봐
english: Performance alone isn't enough for promotion. They also look at leadership.
alt: You need both results and leadership qualities to move up.
note: leadership qualities = 리더십 자질. move up = 승진하다

korean: 동기 중에 나만 아직 승진 못 했어
english: I'm the only one in my cohort who hasn't been promoted yet.
alt: Everyone who started with me has moved up except me.
note: cohort = 동기/같은 기수. move up = 승진하다

korean: 승진 누락되면 너무 속상하지 않아?
english: Doesn't it sting when you get passed over for promotion?
alt: Being overlooked for a promotion is really demoralizing.
note: get passed over = 누락되다. demoralizing = 사기를 꺾는

korean: 매니저급으로 올라가려면 뭘 더 해야 해?
english: What more do I need to do to get to the manager level?
alt: What does it take to move into a management role?
note: manager level = 매니저급. management role = 관리직

korean: 진급하면 업무 범위가 확 달라져
english: Once you get promoted your scope of work changes dramatically.
alt: Moving up means a completely different set of responsibilities.
note: scope of work = 업무 범위. dramatically = 크게

korean: 직급은 올랐는데 급여 인상은 미미해
english: I got promoted but the pay bump was minimal.
alt: The title change came with a barely noticeable raise.
note: pay bump = 급여 인상. minimal = 미미한

korean: 승진 면접 준비 어떻게 했어?
english: How did you prepare for the promotion interview?
alt: What was your approach for the promotion panel?
note: promotion interview / panel = 승진 면접/심사

korean: 올해 승진 TO가 몇 명이래?
english: How many promotion slots are there this year?
alt: What's the headcount for promotions this cycle?
note: promotion slots = 승진 TO(정원). headcount = 인원수

korean: 팀장으로 승진하면 관리 업무가 많아져서 실무를 못 해
english: If I get promoted to team lead I'll be doing more managing and less hands-on work.
alt: Moving to management means less time doing actual work.
note: hands-on = 실무의. management = 관리직

korean: 퇴사할 때 인수인계 잘 해놓고 가야 해
english: Make sure you do a thorough handover before you leave.
alt: Don't leave without a proper transition plan.
note: thorough handover = 철저한 인수인계

korean: 퇴사 사유를 어떻게 말하면 좋을까?
english: How should I phrase my reason for leaving?
alt: What's the best way to explain why I'm resigning?
note: reason for leaving = 퇴사 사유. resign = 사직하다

korean: 퇴사 후 공백기에 뭐 할 계획이야?
english: What are you planning to do during your career break?
alt: Got any plans for the gap between jobs?
note: career break = 경력 공백기. gap between jobs = 이직 공백

korean: 사직서 양식이 따로 있어?
english: Is there a specific resignation letter format?
alt: Do we have a template for the resignation letter?
note: resignation letter = 사직서

korean: 퇴사 면담에서 솔직하게 다 말할 거야?
english: Are you going to be completely honest in the exit interview?
alt: Will you hold back or speak your mind at the exit interview?
note: exit interview = 퇴직 면담. speak your mind = 솔직히 말하다

korean: 마지막 날 팀원들한테 간식이라도 사갈까?
english: Should I bring snacks for the team on my last day?
alt: I'm thinking of treating the team on my final day.
note: treat = 대접하다. final day = 마지막 날

korean: 잔여 연차 정산은 어떻게 돼?
english: How are unused PTO days settled upon resignation?
alt: What happens to my remaining leave balance when I quit?
note: unused PTO = 잔여 연차. settle = 정산하다

korean: 퇴사 후에도 비밀유지 의무가 있어
english: You still have confidentiality obligations after you leave.
alt: The NDA is still in effect even after resignation.
note: confidentiality obligations = 비밀유지 의무. NDA = 비밀유지계약

korean: 원만하게 퇴사하는 게 중요해 업계가 좁으니까
english: It's important to leave on good terms. The industry is small.
alt: Don't burn bridges. You never know when paths will cross again.
note: leave on good terms = 원만하게 떠나다. burn bridges = 관계를 끊다

korean: 퇴직금 산정 기준이 어떻게 되나요?
english: How is the severance pay calculated?
alt: What's the formula for calculating severance?
note: severance pay = 퇴직금. calculate = 산정하다

korean: 입사 첫날인데 긴장돼 뭘 준비하면 좋을까?
english: It's my first day and I'm nervous. What should I prepare?
alt: Any tips for my first day on the job?
note: first day = 첫 출근. tips = 조언

korean: 입사 서류 다 제출했어?
english: Did you submit all the onboarding documents?
alt: Have you turned in all the new hire paperwork?
note: onboarding documents = 입사 서류. paperwork = 서류

korean: 신입 오리엔테이션이 언제야?
english: When is the new employee orientation?
alt: What day is the onboarding session?
note: orientation = 오리엔테이션. onboarding session = 입사 교육

korean: 사수가 누군지 알아? 누구한테 물어봐야 해?
english: Do you know who your mentor is? Who should you ask?
alt: Have they assigned you a buddy or senior to show you the ropes?
note: mentor / buddy = 사수. show the ropes = 업무를 알려주다

korean: 사원증은 언제 나와?
english: When will I get my employee ID?
alt: How long does it take to get the badge?
note: employee ID / badge = 사원증

korean: 입사 동기들이랑 친해지면 좋아 도움 많이 돼
english: Try to get close with your cohort. It really helps.
alt: Building relationships with your fellow new hires pays off.
note: cohort = 동기. pays off = 보상이 되다

korean: 수습 기간 동안 평가 기준이 뭐야?
english: What are the evaluation criteria during the probation period?
alt: How will I be assessed during my trial period?
note: probation / trial period = 수습 기간

korean: 처음 3개월이 제일 중요해 인상을 잘 남겨
english: The first three months are crucial. Make a strong impression.
alt: Your first 90 days set the tone. Show them what you've got.
note: first 90 days = 첫 90일 (미국 기업 관행). set the tone = 분위기를 결정하다

korean: 시스템 계정 발급 신청했어?
english: Have you requested your system account setup?
alt: Did you apply for access to the internal systems?
note: system account = 시스템 계정. access = 접근 권한

korean: 입사 축하해! 적응 잘 하고 있어?
english: Congratulations on the new job! How are you settling in?
alt: Welcome aboard! Are you adjusting okay?
note: welcome aboard = 환영해. settling in = 적응하는

korean: 팀워크가 좋아야 프로젝트가 잘 돌아가
english: Good teamwork is what makes a project run smoothly.
alt: Projects only succeed when the team works well together.
note: teamwork = 팀워크. run smoothly = 순조롭게 돌아가다

korean: 혼자 다 하려고 하지 말고 팀원한테 분배해
english: Don't try to do everything yourself. Delegate to the team.
alt: Share the load with your teammates instead of shouldering it all.
note: delegate = 위임하다. share the load = 부담을 나누다

korean: 서로 의견이 다를 때 어떻게 조율해?
english: How do you reconcile different opinions within the team?
alt: What's the best way to align when teammates disagree?
note: reconcile = 조율하다. align = 맞추다/합의하다

korean: 팀 분위기가 좀 안 좋아 사기가 떨어진 것 같아
english: The team morale seems low. The vibe isn't great.
alt: Team spirit is down. I think people are a bit burnt out.
note: morale = 사기. team spirit = 팀 정신. burnt out = 지친

korean: 역할 분담이 불공평한 것 같아 조정이 필요해
english: The workload distribution feels unfair. We need to rebalance.
alt: Some people are doing way more than others. Let's even things out.
note: workload distribution = 업무 분배. even out = 균등하게 하다

korean: 팀 내 소통이 부족해서 실수가 생기는 거야
english: Miscommunication within the team is causing errors.
alt: The lack of internal communication is leading to mistakes.
note: miscommunication = 소통 부재. internal communication = 내부 소통

korean: 다른 팀이랑 협업할 때 창구를 하나로 통일하자
english: When collaborating with other teams, let's have a single point of contact.
alt: Let's designate one liaison for cross-team communication.
note: point of contact = 연락 창구. liaison = 연락 담당자

korean: 팀 회고 때 서로 칭찬도 하고 개선점도 나누자
english: During the team retro, let's share both praise and constructive feedback.
alt: Let's make the retrospective a mix of kudos and improvement areas.
note: retro = retrospective. kudos = 칭찬

korean: 신입한테 좀 더 관심 가져줘 적응이 어려워 보여
english: Pay more attention to the new hire. They seem to be struggling.
alt: The newbie looks like they could use some guidance.
note: pay attention = 관심을 기울이다. guidance = 안내/지도

korean: 우리 팀은 서로 백업이 잘 돼서 좋아
english: I like that our team backs each other up well.
alt: We've got great coverage on our team. Everyone supports each other.
note: back each other up = 서로 백업하다. coverage = 커버/지원

korean: 리더는 방향을 제시해야 해 시키는 것만 하면 안 돼
english: A leader needs to set the direction not just give orders.
alt: Leadership is about vision not just delegation.
note: set the direction = 방향을 제시하다. vision = 비전

korean: 팀원들의 의견을 경청하는 게 좋은 리더의 기본이야
english: Listening to your team members is the foundation of good leadership.
alt: Good leaders make people feel heard.
note: listen / feel heard = 경청하다/의견이 반영된다고 느끼다

korean: 마이크로매니징 좀 그만해줬으면 좋겠어
english: I wish they'd stop micromanaging.
alt: I'd appreciate a bit more autonomy. The micromanagement is stifling.
note: micromanaging = 마이크로매니징. autonomy = 자율성. stifling = 숨막히는

korean: 리더가 솔선수범해야 팀이 따라가지
english: When the leader leads by example, the team follows.
alt: Teams rally behind leaders who walk the talk.
note: lead by example = 솔선수범. walk the talk = 말한 대로 행동하다

korean: 피드백을 줄 때는 구체적으로 해야 효과가 있어
english: Feedback needs to be specific to be effective.
alt: Vague feedback isn't helpful. Be precise about what needs to change.
note: specific = 구체적인. precise = 정확한

korean: 팀원한테 권한을 위임하면 성장 기회가 돼
english: Delegating authority to team members gives them room to grow.
alt: Empowering your team creates development opportunities.
note: delegate authority = 권한 위임. empower = 권한 부여하다

korean: 좋은 리더는 공은 팀에게 돌리고 책임은 본인이 져
english: A good leader gives credit to the team and takes the blame.
alt: Great leaders share the glory and own the failures.
note: give credit = 공을 돌리다. take the blame = 책임지다

korean: 팀원 한 명 한 명의 강점을 파악해야 해
english: You need to identify each team member's strengths.
alt: Know what each person brings to the table.
note: strengths = 강점. brings to the table = 기여하는 바

korean: 갈등이 생겼을 때 빨리 중재해야 해
english: When conflict arises you need to mediate quickly.
alt: Don't let tensions fester. Address them head-on.
note: mediate = 중재하다. fester = 곪다. head-on = 정면으로

korean: 1대1 면담을 정기적으로 해야 팀원 상태를 알 수 있어
english: Regular one-on-ones help you stay in tune with your team.
alt: Consistent check-ins are essential for understanding your people.
note: one-on-one = 1대1 면담. stay in tune = 상태를 파악하다

korean: 피드백 받을 때 방어적으로 반응하지 마
english: Don't get defensive when receiving feedback.
alt: Try to take criticism constructively instead of getting defensive.
note: defensive = 방어적인. constructively = 건설적으로

korean: 긍정적인 피드백도 중요해 칭찬에 인색하지 마
english: Positive feedback matters too. Don't be stingy with praise.
alt: Recognition goes a long way. Make sure to acknowledge good work.
note: stingy = 인색한. recognition = 인정/칭찬

korean: 피드백은 행동에 초점을 맞춰야지 인격을 비판하면 안 돼
english: Focus feedback on behavior, not the person.
alt: Critique the work not the individual.
note: critique = 비평하다. behavior = 행동

korean: 샌드위치 피드백 기법 알아? 긍정-부정-긍정 순서로
english: Do you know the sandwich method? Positive-negative-positive.
alt: Try wrapping the criticism between two compliments.
note: sandwich method = 샌드위치 기법 (피드백 방식)

korean: 즉각적인 피드백이 효과적이야 나중에 하면 임팩트가 떨어져
english: Immediate feedback is more effective. Delayed feedback loses impact.
alt: Give feedback in the moment. Waiting too long dilutes the message.
note: immediate = 즉각적인. dilute = 희석시키다

korean: 솔직한 피드백 고마워 진짜 도움이 됐어
english: Thanks for the honest feedback. It really helped.
alt: I appreciate the candid input. It made a real difference.
note: candid = 솔직한. input = 의견/피드백

korean: 피드백을 요청하는 것도 능력이야
english: Asking for feedback is a skill in itself.
alt: Proactively seeking feedback shows maturity.
note: proactively = 능동적으로. maturity = 성숙함

korean: 이번 리뷰에서 구체적인 액션 플랜을 세우자
english: Let's create a concrete action plan from this review.
alt: Let's turn the feedback into specific next steps.
note: action plan = 행동 계획. concrete = 구체적인

korean: 부정적인 피드백 전달하기가 참 어렵다
english: Delivering negative feedback is really difficult.
alt: It's tough to give someone critical feedback without hurting them.
note: deliver feedback = 피드백을 전달하다. critical = 비판적인

korean: 피드백 반영한 수정본 내일까지 보내줄게
english: I'll send the revised version with your feedback incorporated by tomorrow.
alt: The updated draft reflecting your notes will be ready by tomorrow.
note: revised version = 수정본. incorporate = 반영하다

korean: 인사고과 등급이 S A B C D 중에 뭐 받았어?
english: What grade did you get on your performance review? S A B C or D?
alt: What was your performance rating this cycle?
note: performance rating = 성과 등급

korean: 상대평가라서 경쟁이 치열해
english: It's a relative grading system so the competition is fierce.
alt: Forced ranking makes it really competitive.
note: relative grading = 상대평가. forced ranking = 강제 배분

korean: 성과 평가에 정량적 지표만 반영하면 안 돼
english: Performance evaluations shouldn't rely solely on quantitative metrics.
alt: There's more to performance than just the numbers.
note: quantitative metrics = 정량적 지표. solely = 오로지

korean: 평가 결과에 이의가 있으면 어디에 말해야 해?
english: Where do I go if I want to dispute my evaluation results?
alt: What's the process for appealing my review score?
note: dispute = 이의 제기하다. appeal = 항소/이의 제기

korean: 팀 기여도를 어떻게 측정하지?
english: How do you measure individual contributions to the team?
alt: What metrics do we use to assess team contribution?
note: contribution = 기여. assess = 평가하다

korean: 동료 평가 비중이 몇 퍼센트야?
english: What percentage of the evaluation is based on peer reviews?
alt: How much weight do peer assessments carry?
note: peer review = 동료 평가. weight = 비중

korean: 성과가 좋으면 인센티브가 얼마나 차이 나?
english: How much of a difference in incentives is there based on performance?
alt: What's the bonus gap between top and average performers?
note: incentive = 인센티브. top performer = 최고 성과자

korean: 지난 분기 성과 리뷰 미팅 잡혀 있어?
english: Is the last quarter's performance review meeting scheduled?
alt: When is the quarterly performance check-in?
note: quarterly = 분기별. check-in = 점검 미팅

korean: 업적 평가서에 뭘 넣어야 할지 모르겠어
english: I'm not sure what to include in my achievement report.
alt: I'm drawing a blank on what to put in my performance writeup.
note: achievement report = 업적 평가서. draw a blank = 떠오르지 않다

korean: 평가 면담 때 커리어 방향도 같이 얘기하고 싶어
english: I'd like to discuss my career direction during the review meeting too.
alt: Can we also talk about career pathing in my evaluation?
note: career direction / pathing = 커리어 방향/경로

korean: 출장 갈 때 여권 유효기간 확인했어?
english: Did you check your passport expiration date before the trip?
alt: Make sure your passport is still valid for the business trip.
note: passport expiration = 여권 만료일

korean: 출장 중에 현지 음식이 안 맞아서 고생했어
english: I had a rough time with the local food during the trip.
alt: The food on the business trip didn't agree with me.
note: didn't agree with me = 안 맞았다 (음식). rough time = 힘든 시간

korean: 해외 출장이라 시차 적응이 힘들어
english: The jet lag from the international trip is killing me.
alt: I'm struggling with the time difference from the overseas trip.
note: jet lag = 시차 피로. time difference = 시차

korean: 출장 일정이 너무 빡빡해서 쉴 틈이 없었어
english: The trip schedule was so packed there was no downtime.
alt: I didn't have a moment to breathe on that business trip.
note: packed = 빡빡한. downtime = 쉬는 시간

korean: 공항 라운지 이용권 회사에서 지원해줘?
english: Does the company provide airport lounge access?
alt: Can I use the corporate account for lounge access?
note: lounge access = 라운지 이용. corporate account = 법인 계정

korean: 출장 복귀하면 바로 보고서 제출해야 해?
english: Do I have to submit the report right after I get back?
alt: Is the trip report due immediately upon return?
note: upon return = 복귀 즉시

korean: 출장 중에 노트북 분실했어 어떡하지?
english: I lost my laptop during the business trip. What do I do?
alt: My work laptop went missing on the trip. Who do I report this to?
note: lost / went missing = 분실한. report = 신고하다

korean: 현지에서 통역사가 필요한데 회사에서 지원 가능해?
english: I need an interpreter on-site. Can the company arrange one?
alt: Is there budget for a local translator during the trip?
note: interpreter / translator = 통역사/번역사. on-site = 현지

korean: 출장비 카드 결제 명세서 제출해야 해?
english: Do I need to submit the card statement for trip expenses?
alt: Should I turn in my credit card receipts for reimbursement?
note: card statement = 카드 명세서. reimbursement = 환급

korean: 다음 출장은 화상으로 대체할 수 있을까?
english: Can we replace the next trip with a virtual meeting?
alt: Could we do the next one remotely instead of traveling?
note: replace with = ~로 대체하다. remotely = 원격으로

korean: 거래처 담당자가 바뀌었다는데 인사 메일 보내야 하나?
english: The contact at our partner company changed. Should I send an introductory email?
alt: Our client rep changed. Do I need to reach out and introduce myself?
note: contact person = 담당자. introductory = 소개의

korean: 거래처에서 납기 연장을 요청했어
english: The vendor is asking for a delivery extension.
alt: Our supplier wants to push back the delivery date.
note: delivery extension = 납기 연장. supplier = 공급업체

korean: 거래처 관계 유지가 중요해 정기적으로 연락해
english: Maintaining vendor relationships is important. Stay in touch regularly.
alt: Keep up regular communication with our partners.
note: vendor relationship = 거래처 관계. stay in touch = 연락하다

korean: 거래처에서 클레임이 들어왔는데 어떻게 대응하지?
english: We received a complaint from a partner. How should we respond?
alt: The vendor filed a claim. What's our response strategy?
note: complaint / claim = 클레임. response strategy = 대응 전략

korean: 새로운 거래처 발굴이 필요해
english: We need to find new business partners.
alt: It's time to source new vendors.
note: source = 발굴하다. business partners / vendors = 거래처

korean: 거래처 미팅에 선물 챙겨가는 게 좋을까?
english: Should I bring a gift to the partner meeting?
alt: Is it appropriate to bring a small gift for the client?
note: appropriate = 적절한. gift = 선물

korean: 거래처 평가를 연 1회 해야 해
english: We need to do an annual vendor assessment.
alt: Supplier evaluations should be conducted once a year.
note: vendor assessment = 거래처 평가. conduct = 실시하다

korean: 거래처에서 가격 인상을 통보했어
english: The vendor notified us of a price increase.
alt: Our supplier just hit us with a rate hike.
note: price increase = 가격 인상. rate hike = 요금 인상

korean: 장기 거래처니까 좀 더 유연하게 대응하자
english: They're a long-term partner so let's be more flexible.
alt: Given our long relationship with them we should be accommodating.
note: long-term partner = 장기 거래처. accommodating = 유연한

korean: 거래처 실사 일정 잡았어?
english: Have you scheduled the vendor audit?
alt: When is the on-site inspection of the supplier?
note: vendor audit = 거래처 실사. on-site inspection = 현장 실사

korean: 마감이 내일인데 아직 반도 못 끝냈어
english: The deadline is tomorrow and I'm not even halfway done.
alt: It's due tomorrow and I've barely made a dent.
note: make a dent = 진전을 보이다. barely = 겨우

korean: 마감 전날에 항상 야근이야
english: I always end up working late the night before a deadline.
alt: Deadline eve is guaranteed overtime for me.
note: deadline eve = 마감 전날. overtime = 야근

korean: 마감 연장 가능한지 한번 물어볼게
english: Let me ask if we can get an extension on the deadline.
alt: I'll check if pushing the due date is an option.
note: extension = 연장. due date = 마감일

korean: 이 마감은 절대 못 미뤄 클라이언트 약속이야
english: This deadline is non-negotiable. We promised the client.
alt: There's zero flexibility on this one. The client is expecting it.
note: non-negotiable = 협상 불가. flexibility = 유연성

korean: 마감에 쫓기면 퀄리티가 떨어져
english: Rushing to meet deadlines compromises quality.
alt: The quality suffers when we're under time pressure.
note: compromise quality = 품질이 떨어지다. time pressure = 시간 압박

korean: 중간 마감이라도 잡자 최종 마감 전에
english: Let's set a mid-point deadline before the final due date.
alt: We should have an interim milestone to stay on track.
note: interim milestone = 중간 마감. on track = 순조롭게

korean: 마감 지키려면 밤새야 할 수도 있어
english: We might have to pull an all-nighter to meet the deadline.
alt: An all-nighter might be the only way to finish on time.
note: pull an all-nighter = 밤새다

korean: 마감일 변경되면 관련 부서에 바로 공유해줘
english: If the deadline changes notify all relevant departments immediately.
alt: Any shifts in the due date need to be communicated right away.
note: relevant departments = 관련 부서. communicate = 공유/전달하다

korean: 이번 마감은 무리 없이 맞출 수 있을 것 같아
english: I think we can comfortably meet this deadline.
alt: We should be able to hit this due date without any issues.
note: comfortably = 무리 없이. hit the deadline = 마감을 맞추다

korean: 마감 직전에 항상 문제가 터지더라
english: Issues always pop up right before the deadline.
alt: Something always goes wrong at the last minute.
note: pop up = 갑자기 나타나다. at the last minute = 마감 직전에

korean: 야근 수당이 안 나오면 무슨 의미가 있어
english: What's the point of overtime if there's no extra pay?
alt: Unpaid overtime is basically exploitation.
note: unpaid overtime = 무급 야근. exploitation = 착취

korean: 오늘도 야근이야 이번 주 벌써 세 번째야
english: I'm working late again. That's the third time this week.
alt: Another late night. This is getting ridiculous.
note: working late = 야근. ridiculous = 말도 안 되는

korean: 야근하면 택시비라도 나오나?
english: Do we at least get taxi fare for working late?
alt: Is there a transportation allowance for overtime?
note: taxi fare = 택시비. transportation allowance = 교통비 지원

korean: 야근 문화를 바꿔야 해 효율적으로 일하자
english: We need to change the overtime culture. Let's work smarter not harder.
alt: The late-night culture has to go. Efficiency should come first.
note: work smarter = 더 똑똑하게 일하다. efficiency = 효율성

korean: 야근 대신 내일 아침 일찍 오는 건 어때?
english: How about coming in early tomorrow instead of staying late?
alt: What if I start early in the morning rather than doing overtime?
note: come in early = 일찍 출근. rather than = ~대신

korean: 야근밥 뭐 먹을까? 배달 시킬까?
english: What should we eat for our overtime meal? Want to order delivery?
alt: Let's order food for the late shift. What are you in the mood for?
note: overtime meal = 야근 식사. late shift = 늦은 근무

korean: 매일 야근하면 건강에 안 좋아 쉬엄쉬엄해
english: Working overtime every day is bad for your health. Take it easy.
alt: Chronic overtime will catch up with you. Pace yourself.
note: chronic = 만성적인. pace yourself = 속도 조절하다

korean: 야근하고 나면 다음 날 컨디션이 최악이야
english: After working late my condition the next day is terrible.
alt: I'm always wrecked the morning after overtime.
note: wrecked = 망가진. condition = 컨디션

korean: 강제 야근은 아닌데 분위기상 안 갈 수가 없어
english: It's not mandatory overtime, but the atmosphere makes it hard to leave.
alt: Technically it's voluntary but peer pressure keeps you at your desk.
note: mandatory = 의무적인. peer pressure = 동료 압박

korean: 야근 줄이려면 업무 효율을 높여야 해
english: To reduce overtime we need to improve work efficiency.
alt: Cutting down on late nights starts with better time management.
note: work efficiency = 업무 효율. time management = 시간 관리

korean: 재택근무 시작할 때 출근 체크 어떻게 해?
english: How do I clock in when I start working from home?
alt: What's the check-in process for remote work days?
note: clock in = 출근 체크. check-in = 출석

korean: 재택할 때 집중하려면 전용 공간이 필요해
english: You need a dedicated workspace to focus when working from home.
alt: Having a separate home office space makes a huge difference.
note: dedicated workspace = 전용 공간. home office = 재택 사무실

korean: 재택인데 갑자기 출근하라고 하면 너무 불편해
english: Being called into the office suddenly when you're WFH is really inconvenient.
alt: Last-minute requests to come in during remote days are frustrating.
note: called in = 출근 요청. last-minute = 직전의

korean: 재택근무 일수가 줄어든다는 소문이 있어
english: Rumor has it remote work days are going to be cut.
alt: I heard they might reduce the number of WFH days.
note: cut / reduce = 줄이다. rumor has it = 소문에 의하면

korean: 집에서 일하면 퇴근 시간 구분이 안 돼
english: Working from home makes it hard to separate work and personal time.
alt: The line between work and life gets blurry when you WFH.
note: separate = 구분하다. blurry = 흐릿한

korean: 재택할 때 화상회의가 너무 많아서 카메라 피로가 심해
english: Too many video calls during WFH causes serious camera fatigue.
alt: Zoom fatigue is real when you're remote all day.
note: camera fatigue / Zoom fatigue = 화상회의 피로

korean: 하이브리드 근무가 가장 이상적인 것 같아
english: I think hybrid work is the most ideal setup.
alt: A mix of office and remote days seems like the best arrangement.
note: hybrid work = 하이브리드 근무. arrangement = 체계

korean: 재택할 때 운동 부족이 문제야 하루종일 앉아 있어
english: Lack of exercise is a problem when WFH. I sit all day.
alt: I barely move when I work from home. It's not healthy.
note: lack of exercise = 운동 부족. sedentary = 앉아서 하는

korean: 재택근무 정책이 회사마다 다르더라
english: Remote work policies differ from company to company.
alt: Every company has its own take on WFH rules.
note: remote work policy = 재택근무 정책

korean: 집에서 일하면 소통이 어렵다는 게 단점이야
english: The downside of WFH is that communication becomes harder.
alt: Remote work makes spontaneous collaboration difficult.
note: downside = 단점. spontaneous = 즉흥적인

korean: 점심시간이 짧아서 밖에 나가 먹기 힘들어
english: The lunch break is too short to eat out.
alt: There's barely enough time to grab lunch outside.
note: lunch break = 점심시간. eat out = 외식하다

korean: 점심 같이 먹을 사람 있어? 혼밥 싫은데
english: Anyone want to grab lunch together? I don't want to eat alone.
alt: Who's free for lunch? I'd rather not eat solo.
note: eat alone / solo = 혼밥. grab lunch = 점심 먹다

korean: 오늘 점심 뭐 먹을지 아직 못 정했어
english: I still haven't decided what to eat for lunch.
alt: I'm on the fence about what to have for lunch.
note: on the fence = 결정 못 한

korean: 점심시간에 산책하면 오후에 집중이 잘 돼
english: Taking a walk during lunch helps me focus in the afternoon.
alt: A lunchtime stroll really boosts my afternoon productivity.
note: stroll = 산책. boost = 높이다

korean: 구내식당 메뉴가 매일 똑같아서 질려
english: The cafeteria menu is the same every day. I'm sick of it.
alt: I'm so tired of the same old food at the company cafeteria.
note: cafeteria = 구내식당. sick of = 질린

korean: 점심값이 너무 올라서 도시락 싸올까 고민이야
english: Lunch prices have gone up so much I'm thinking about bringing my own.
alt: Food near the office is getting expensive. Might start packing lunch.
note: pack lunch = 도시락 싸다. bring your own = 직접 가져오다

korean: 점심시간에 잠깐 은행 다녀와도 돼?
english: Is it okay if I run to the bank during lunch?
alt: Mind if I step out to the bank on my lunch break?
note: run to = 잠깐 다녀오다. step out = 나가다

korean: 점심 먹고 나면 졸려서 커피가 필수야
english: I get so sleepy after lunch that coffee is a must.
alt: Post-lunch drowsiness is real. I need caffeine to survive.
note: drowsiness = 졸림. caffeine = 카페인

korean: 점심 미팅이라 샌드위치라도 사가야겠다
english: It's a lunch meeting so I should grab a sandwich at least.
alt: I'll pick up something quick since we're meeting over lunch.
note: lunch meeting = 점심 미팅. grab = 사다

korean: 점심시간에 업무 연락 오면 진짜 짜증나
english: Getting work messages during lunch is so annoying.
alt: I hate when people Slack me during my lunch break.
note: annoying = 짜증나는. lunch break = 점심시간

korean: 이 회사는 수평적 문화라 직급 호칭을 안 써
english: This company has a flat culture so we don't use titles.
alt: We go by first names here. It's a non-hierarchical environment.
note: flat culture = 수평적 문화. non-hierarchical = 비계층적

korean: 회식 자리에서 상사한테 술 따르는 문화가 좀 불편해
english: The culture of pouring drinks for your boss at company dinners is a bit uncomfortable.
alt: I find the hierarchical drinking etiquette at work events awkward.
note: hierarchical = 계층적인. etiquette = 예절

korean: 우리 회사는 자유로운 복장이라 편해
english: Our company has a casual dress code which is nice.
alt: The relaxed dress policy here is a huge plus.
note: casual dress code = 자유 복장. relaxed = 편한

korean: 사내 메신저 답장 속도가 곧 성실함으로 평가돼
english: How fast you reply on the work messenger is seen as a measure of dedication.
alt: Response time on Slack is basically your performance score here.
note: dedication = 성실함. response time = 답변 속도

korean: 이 회사는 야근을 미덕으로 여기는 분위기야
english: This company treats working late as a virtue.
alt: There's a culture of glorifying overtime here.
note: virtue = 미덕. glorify = 미화하다

korean: 신입이 커피 타는 문화가 아직 있어?
english: Does the tradition of new hires making coffee still exist?
alt: Do juniors still have to do the coffee runs?
note: tradition = 전통. coffee run = 커피 심부름

korean: 우리 팀은 매주 금요일에 팀 런치를 해
english: Our team does a team lunch every Friday.
alt: We have a weekly Friday lunch as a team.
note: team lunch = 팀 런치

korean: 사내 익명 게시판에 불만이 많이 올라오더라
english: There have been a lot of complaints on the anonymous company board.
alt: The internal anonymous forum is blowing up with grievances.
note: anonymous = 익명의. grievances = 불만

korean: 이 회사는 회의 시작 5분 전에 도착하는 게 예의야
english: At this company it's considered polite to arrive 5 minutes before a meeting.
alt: Showing up early to meetings is an unwritten rule here.
note: unwritten rule = 불문율. polite = 예의 바른

korean: 개방형 사무실이라 프라이버시가 없어
english: The open office layout means zero privacy.
alt: There's no personal space in an open-plan office.
note: open office / open-plan = 개방형 사무실. privacy = 프라이버시

korean: 주간 보고서 형식이 정해져 있어?
english: Is there a set format for the weekly report?
alt: Do we have a template for the weekly status update?
note: format / template = 형식/템플릿

korean: 보고 시 핵심 위주로 간결하게 정리해줘
english: Keep the report concise and focus on the key points.
alt: Stick to the highlights in your report. No fluff.
note: concise = 간결한. highlights = 핵심 사항. fluff = 불필요한 내용

korean: 경영진 보고라서 톤을 좀 격식 있게 맞춰야 해
english: It's a report for senior management so the tone needs to be more formal.
alt: This goes to the exec team so make it polished and professional.
note: senior management / exec team = 경영진. polished = 세련된

korean: 보고서에 그래프 넣으면 이해하기 쉬워
english: Adding graphs to the report makes it easier to understand.
alt: Visualizing the data with charts really helps convey the message.
note: graph / chart = 그래프/도표. convey = 전달하다

korean: 이번 보고에 리스크 항목도 포함시켜야 해
english: We need to include a risk section in this report.
alt: Make sure to flag potential risks in the update.
note: risk section = 리스크 항목. flag = 표시하다

korean: 보고 전에 팩트 체크 한번 더 해줘
english: Double-check the facts before you submit the report.
alt: Verify all the data points before the report goes out.
note: fact check = 팩트 체크. verify = 검증하다

korean: 일일 보고가 필요해? 아니면 주간 단위로?
english: Do we need daily reports or weekly ones?
alt: Is the reporting cadence daily or weekly?
note: reporting cadence = 보고 주기

korean: 보고서 양이 너무 많아 요약본도 같이 만들자
english: The report is too long. Let's create an executive summary too.
alt: This is a hefty report. We need a TL;DR version as well.
note: executive summary = 요약본. TL;DR = Too Long; Didn't Read

korean: 구두 보고 말고 서면으로 남기자
english: Let's document it in writing instead of just verbal reporting.
alt: Put it in a written report rather than just talking about it.
note: verbal = 구두의. in writing = 서면으로

korean: 보고서 마감이 오늘 5시야 잊지 마
english: The report is due at 5 PM today. Don't forget.
alt: Reminder: the report deadline is 5 o'clock sharp.
note: due = 마감인. sharp = 정각

korean: 인사팀에서 연락이 왔는데 서류 추가 제출하래
english: HR contacted me and they need additional documents.
alt: The HR department is asking for more paperwork.
note: HR = Human Resources. additional documents = 추가 서류

korean: 취업 규칙 어디서 볼 수 있어?
english: Where can I find the employment regulations?
alt: How do I access the company's work rules?
note: employment regulations = 취업 규칙

korean: 인사팀에 휴가 신청 시스템이 바뀌었대
english: HR says the leave request system has changed.
alt: Apparently there's a new process for requesting time off.
note: leave request = 휴가 신청. time off = 휴가

korean: 징계 절차가 어떻게 되는지 알아?
english: Do you know what the disciplinary process looks like?
alt: What happens if you go through a disciplinary action?
note: disciplinary process = 징계 절차

korean: 인사 발령이 났어 부서 이동이래
english: I got a personnel notice. I'm being transferred to another department.
alt: They issued a transfer order. I'm moving to a new team.
note: personnel notice = 인사 발령. transfer = 이동

korean: 복직 절차가 어떻게 돼? 육아휴직 끝나고
english: What's the process for returning to work after parental leave?
alt: How do I handle the reinstatement procedure post-maternity leave?
note: reinstatement = 복직. post-maternity leave = 출산휴가 후

korean: 인사팀에서 직원 만족도 조사를 하래
english: HR is conducting an employee satisfaction survey.
alt: They sent out a workplace engagement survey.
note: satisfaction survey = 만족도 조사. engagement = 참여도

korean: 급여 명세서가 안 나왔어 인사팀에 문의해야겠어
english: My pay stub hasn't been issued. I need to contact HR.
alt: The payslip is missing. I'll reach out to payroll.
note: pay stub / payslip = 급여 명세서. payroll = 급여팀

korean: 인사팀에서 보험 변경 안내가 왔어
english: HR sent a notice about insurance changes.
alt: There's an update from HR regarding benefits enrollment.
note: insurance changes = 보험 변경. benefits enrollment = 복지 등록

korean: 출퇴근 기록이 누락됐어 정정 신청해야 해
english: My attendance record is missing. I need to request a correction.
alt: There's a gap in my time log. I need to file a correction.
note: attendance record = 출퇴근 기록. correction = 정정

korean: 헬스장 지원 복지가 있어서 좋아
english: I love that the company subsidizes gym memberships.
alt: The fitness benefit is one of the best perks here.
note: subsidize = 지원하다. gym membership = 헬스장 회원권

korean: 자기 개발비로 온라인 강의 들어도 돼?
english: Can I use the professional development fund for online courses?
alt: Is the learning budget applicable to e-learning platforms?
note: professional development fund = 자기 개발비. e-learning = 온라인 학습

korean: 경조사 휴가가 며칠이야?
english: How many days off do we get for family events?
alt: What's the leave policy for weddings and funerals?
note: family events = 경조사. leave policy = 휴가 정책

korean: 사내 어린이집이 있어서 육아하기 편해
english: Having an on-site daycare makes it so much easier for parents.
alt: The company childcare center is a huge help for working parents.
note: on-site daycare = 사내 어린이집. childcare = 육아

korean: 통근 버스가 있어서 출퇴근이 편해
english: The company shuttle makes commuting really convenient.
alt: Having a commuter bus is a game changer for getting to work.
note: company shuttle = 통근 버스. commuter = 통근자

korean: 건강검진 지원이 포함되어 있어?
english: Is health screening covered by the company?
alt: Does the benefits package include an annual checkup?
note: health screening = 건강검진. annual checkup = 연례 검진

korean: 주택자금 대출 지원 제도가 있다던데
english: I heard there's a housing loan assistance program.
alt: Does the company offer housing subsidies or loans?
note: housing loan = 주택자금 대출. subsidy = 보조금

korean: 복지카드로 사용할 수 있는 곳이 한정적이야
english: The places where you can use the welfare card are limited.
alt: The benefits card has pretty restricted usage.
note: welfare card = 복지카드. restricted = 제한된

korean: 명절 선물 뭐 나와? 올해도 상품권이야?
english: What do we get for the holiday gift? Gift cards again?
alt: Are we getting gift certificates for the holiday bonus?
note: holiday gift = 명절 선물. gift card / certificate = 상품권

korean: 안식월 제도 써본 사람 있어? 어때?
english: Has anyone used the sabbatical month? How was it?
alt: What's the sabbatical leave like? Worth taking?
note: sabbatical month = 안식월. worth taking = 쓸 만한

korean: 신입사원 환영회 언제 하기로 했지?
english: When did we say we'd do the welcome party for the new hire?
alt: What date is the new employee welcome event?
note: welcome party = 환영회. new hire = 신입사원

korean: 배치 희망 부서가 있어?
english: Do you have a preferred department for your placement?
alt: Is there a team you'd like to be assigned to?
note: placement = 배치. assigned to = 배정된

korean: 인사 평가 피드백 면담 일정이 잡혔어?
english: Has the HR evaluation feedback meeting been scheduled?
alt: When's the appraisal discussion with our manager?
note: appraisal = 평가. feedback meeting = 피드백 면담

korean: 인사 제도 개편한다는데 뭐가 바뀌는 거야?
english: I heard they're revamping the HR system. What's changing?
alt: There's a personnel policy overhaul coming. Any details?
note: revamp = 개편하다. overhaul = 전면 개편

korean: 전직 지원 프로그램이 있어?
english: Is there an outplacement support program?
alt: Does the company offer career transition assistance?
note: outplacement = 전직 지원. career transition = 경력 전환

korean: 직무 순환 제도가 있다던데 신청할 수 있어?
english: I heard there's a job rotation program. Can I apply?
alt: Is the internal rotation program open for applications?
note: job rotation = 직무 순환. internal rotation = 내부 이동

korean: 직원 추천 제도로 입사한 사람이 많더라
english: A lot of people were hired through the employee referral program.
alt: Referral hires make up a big chunk of new employees.
note: employee referral = 직원 추천. referral hire = 추천 채용

korean: 연수 프로그램 일정이 어떻게 돼?
english: What's the schedule for the training program?
alt: When does the orientation training start?
note: training program = 연수 프로그램

korean: 인사 담당자한테 경력증명서 요청해야 해
english: I need to request a career certificate from HR.
alt: I have to get an employment verification letter from the HR team.
note: career certificate = 경력증명서. employment verification = 재직증명

korean: 조직도가 바뀌었대 확인해봐
english: The org chart has been updated. Check it out.
alt: There's a new organizational structure. Take a look.
note: org chart = 조직도. organizational structure = 조직 구조

korean: 회의실 예약 좀 해줄래? 10명 들어갈 수 있는 데로
english: Can you book a meeting room? One that fits ten people.
alt: Reserve a conference room for ten please.
note: book / reserve = 예약하다. conference room = 회의실

korean: 회의 시작 전에 노트북 연결 테스트 해봐
english: Test the laptop connection before the meeting starts.
alt: Make sure the projector setup works before we begin.
note: projector = 프로젝터. setup = 설정

korean: 이 안건은 오늘 결론 내야 해 더 미룰 수 없어
english: We need to reach a conclusion on this today. We can't delay any longer.
alt: This has been tabled enough times. Let's decide now.
note: reach a conclusion = 결론을 내다. tabled = 보류된

korean: 회의 참석률이 낮아서 정족수가 안 돼
english: Attendance is too low. We don't have a quorum.
alt: We can't proceed without enough people. We're short on quorum.
note: quorum = 정족수. attendance = 참석률

korean: 스탠딩 미팅으로 하자 짧게 끝내려고
english: Let's make it a standing meeting to keep it short.
alt: How about we do this one standing up so it doesn't drag?
note: standing meeting = 서서 하는 회의 (간결함 목적)

korean: 화상회의 접속 링크가 만료됐어 새로 보내줘
english: The video call link has expired. Can you send a new one?
alt: The meeting invite link isn't working. Resend please.
note: expired = 만료된. meeting invite = 회의 초대

korean: 회의 끝나고 5분 쉬고 다음 회의 가자
english: Let's take a five-minute break, then head to the next meeting.
alt: Quick breather between meetings. Five minutes.
note: breather = 잠깐의 쉬는 시간

korean: 회의 중에 딴짓하지 마 다 보여
english: Don't zone out during the meeting. Everyone can tell.
alt: Pay attention in the meeting. Multitasking is obvious on camera.
note: zone out = 딴짓하다/멍때리다. multitasking = 멀티태스킹

korean: 오늘 회의 패실리테이터가 누구야?
english: Who's facilitating today's meeting?
alt: Who's running the meeting today?
note: facilitator = 진행자. facilitate = 진행하다

korean: 이 토론이 산으로 가고 있어 주제로 돌아오자
english: This discussion is going off the rails. Let's get back on topic.
alt: We're veering off course. Let's refocus.
note: off the rails = 탈선한. veer off = 벗어나다

korean: 이메일 제목에 날짜를 꼭 넣어줘 나중에 찾기 편하게
english: Always include the date in the email subject for easy reference later.
alt: Put the date in the subject line so we can search for it easily.
note: subject line = 제목. easy reference = 쉬운 참조

korean: 외부 이메일 보낼 때는 경어를 써야 해
english: Use formal language when sending emails to external contacts.
alt: External emails should maintain a professional and polite tone.
note: formal language = 경어/격식체. external = 외부

korean: 이 이메일 영문 교정 좀 해줘
english: Can you proofread this English email for me?
alt: Mind checking my English email for any mistakes?
note: proofread = 교정하다. mistakes = 실수

korean: 메일 본문이 너무 길어 두 문단으로 줄여봐
english: The email body is too long. Try condensing it to two paragraphs.
alt: This is way too wordy. Cut it down to the essentials.
note: condense = 압축하다. wordy = 장황한

korean: 자동 서명에 SNS 링크도 넣을까?
english: Should I add social media links to my auto-signature?
alt: Is it appropriate to include LinkedIn in my email footer?
note: auto-signature = 자동 서명. email footer = 이메일 하단

korean: 이 이메일 발송 전에 최종 확인 부탁드립니다
english: Please do a final review before this email goes out.
alt: Give this one last look before I hit send.
note: final review = 최종 확인. goes out = 발송되다

korean: 메일링 리스트에서 나를 빼줘 관련 없는 메일이 너무 와
english: Remove me from the mailing list. I keep getting irrelevant emails.
alt: Take me off that distribution list. It's all noise.
note: mailing list / distribution list = 메일링 리스트. noise = 불필요한 정보

korean: 이 메일 보안 등급이 높으니까 암호화해서 보내
english: This email has a high security level so send it encrypted.
alt: Make sure to encrypt this one. It's sensitive material.
note: encrypted = 암호화된. sensitive = 민감한

korean: 고객한테 사과 메일 보내야 하는데 어떻게 써야 할까?
english: I need to send an apology email to the client. How should I word it?
alt: What's the best way to draft an apology email to the customer?
note: apology email = 사과 메일. word / draft = 작성하다

korean: 메일 수신자를 잘못 넣었어 빨리 취소할 수 있어?
english: I put the wrong recipient. Can I recall it quickly?
alt: I sent it to the wrong person. Is there a way to unsend?
note: recipient = 수신자. recall / unsend = 회수/취소

korean: 이번 프로젝트 일정이 타이트한데 리소스 추가 가능해?
english: The timeline is tight for this project. Can we get more resources?
alt: We need extra headcount to meet this aggressive schedule.
note: headcount = 인력. aggressive schedule = 빡빡한 일정

korean: 슬랙에서 중요한 결정 내리지 말고 회의에서 하자
english: Don't make important decisions on Slack. Let's discuss in a meeting.
alt: Critical decisions should be made in a meeting not on messenger.
note: critical decisions = 중요한 결정

korean: 이 문서 접근 권한 좀 줘 읽기만 할 수 있게
english: Can you give me access to this document? Read-only is fine.
alt: I need view access to that file. Can you share it?
note: access / view access = 접근 권한. read-only = 읽기 전용

korean: 업무 우선순위 정할 때 아이젠하워 매트릭스 써봐
english: Try using the Eisenhower matrix to prioritize your tasks.
alt: The urgent-important matrix really helps with task management.
note: Eisenhower matrix = 아이젠하워 매트릭스 (긴급/중요도 구분)

korean: 이 업무 자동화하면 시간 엄청 아낄 수 있을 텐데
english: Automating this task could save us a ton of time.
alt: Think of how much time we'd free up if this were automated.
note: automate = 자동화하다. free up = 확보하다

korean: 다음 주 일정이 빡빡해서 미리 준비해야 해
english: Next week's schedule is packed so I need to prepare in advance.
alt: I should get ahead of things since next week is going to be hectic.
note: packed = 빡빡한. hectic = 정신없는. get ahead = 미리 준비하다

korean: 이 작업 템플릿화해놓으면 나중에 편해
english: If we templatize this task it'll be much easier next time.
alt: Creating a template for this will streamline future work.
note: templatize = 템플릿화하다. streamline = 간소화하다

korean: 그거 내가 맡을게 네 업무량이 많아 보여서
english: I'll take that off your hands. You look like you have a lot on your plate.
alt: Let me handle that. You seem overloaded right now.
note: take off your hands = 대신 맡다. on your plate = 할 일이 많은

korean: 회사 시스템이 다운됐어 IT팀에 연락했어?
english: The company system is down. Did you contact IT?
alt: The servers crashed. Has anyone pinged the IT department?
note: system down = 시스템 다운. crash = 다운되다. ping = 연락하다

korean: 오늘 하루 종일 전화 받느라 일을 못 했어
english: I spent the whole day on calls and couldn't get any work done.
alt: Back-to-back calls all day. Zero productive time.
note: back-to-back = 연이은. productive time = 생산적인 시간

korean: 거래처에서 대금 결제가 늦어지고 있어
english: The vendor is late on payment.
alt: Our business partner is behind on their invoice.
note: late on payment = 결제 지연. invoice = 청구서

korean: 파트너사와 공동 마케팅 캠페인 할 수 있을까?
english: Could we run a joint marketing campaign with the partner?
alt: Would the partner be open to a co-marketing initiative?
note: joint / co-marketing = 공동 마케팅

korean: 거래처 미팅 의사록 작성해서 공유해줘
english: Write up the minutes from the vendor meeting and share them.
alt: Document what was discussed at the partner meeting.
note: minutes = 의사록/회의록. document = 기록하다

korean: 하청업체 관리가 점점 어려워지고 있어
english: Managing subcontractors is getting increasingly difficult.
alt: Subcontractor oversight is becoming a real challenge.
note: subcontractor = 하청업체. oversight = 관리/감독

korean: 거래처 선정 기준이 뭐야?
english: What are the criteria for selecting a vendor?
alt: How do we evaluate and choose our suppliers?
note: vendor selection = 거래처 선정. criteria = 기준

korean: 입찰 참여하려면 서류 준비가 많아
english: There's a lot of paperwork to prepare for the bid.
alt: Bidding requires a ton of documentation.
note: bid = 입찰. documentation = 서류/문서

korean: 거래 조건을 재검토해야 할 시점이야
english: It's time to revisit the terms of our agreement.
alt: We should renegotiate the deal terms.
note: revisit = 재검토하다. deal terms = 거래 조건

korean: 납품 품질에 문제가 있어서 항의해야 해
english: There's a quality issue with the delivery. We need to file a complaint.
alt: The shipment quality doesn't meet our standards. We need to escalate.
note: quality issue = 품질 문제. file a complaint = 항의하다

korean: 발주서 보냈어? 납기가 촉박해
english: Did you send the purchase order? The delivery deadline is tight.
alt: Has the PO been issued? We're running short on time.
note: purchase order (PO) = 발주서. running short = 시간이 촉박한

korean: 공급망이 불안정해서 대체 업체를 찾아봐야 해
english: The supply chain is unstable so we need to find alternative vendors.
alt: We should source backup suppliers given the supply chain disruptions.
note: supply chain = 공급망. backup supplier = 대체 공급업체

korean: 상반기 실적 보고서를 경영진에 제출해야 해
english: We need to submit the first-half performance report to the executive team.
alt: The H1 results deck needs to go to the C-suite.
note: H1 = first half. C-suite = 최고 경영진

korean: 이슈 사항 위주로 보고해줘 잘 되는 건 빼고
english: Focus your report on issues. Skip the things going well.
alt: Highlight the problem areas only. No need for the good news.
note: highlight = 강조하다. problem areas = 문제 영역

korean: 보고서에 해결 방안도 같이 제시해
english: Include proposed solutions in the report as well.
alt: Don't just flag the problem. Suggest fixes too.
note: proposed solutions = 제안된 해결방안. flag = 지적하다

korean: 월간 보고서 포맷이 바뀌었으니까 새 템플릿 써
english: The monthly report format changed so use the new template.
alt: There's an updated template for the monthly report. Switch to it.
note: monthly report = 월간 보고서. template = 템플릿

korean: 보고 자료에 출처를 명시해야 해
english: Make sure to cite your sources in the report.
alt: Include references for all the data in your report.
note: cite sources = 출처를 밝히다. references = 참고 자료

korean: 이번 회의에서 의결할 안건이 세 개야
english: There are three items to vote on in this meeting.
alt: We have three agenda items that need a decision today.
note: vote on = 의결하다. agenda items = 안건

korean: 회의 시간이 초과되고 있어 나머지는 다음에 하자
english: We're running over time. Let's table the rest.
alt: The meeting's gone overtime. Let's carry the rest over.
note: running over time = 시간 초과. carry over = 다음으로 넘기다

korean: 이번 회의 목표를 먼저 명확히 하고 시작하자
english: Let's clarify the meeting objectives before we begin.
alt: Set the goals first so we stay focused during the discussion.
note: objectives = 목표. clarify = 명확히 하다

korean: 화상회의 레코딩 파일 공유해줄 수 있어?
english: Can you share the recording of the video call?
alt: Is the meeting recording available somewhere?
note: recording = 녹화 파일. available = 이용 가능한

korean: 전원 참석이 어려우면 비동기로 의견 수렴하자
english: If not everyone can attend let's collect opinions asynchronously.
alt: We can do an async vote if people can't make the meeting.
note: asynchronous = 비동기적. collect opinions = 의견 수렴

korean: 프리랜서 계약서 검토 좀 해줄래?
english: Can you review the freelancer contract for me?
alt: Mind looking over the independent contractor agreement?
note: freelancer contract = 프리랜서 계약서. independent contractor = 독립 계약자

korean: 이번 계약 갱신 시 조건을 좀 더 좋게 받아내자
english: Let's push for better terms when we renew the contract.
alt: We should leverage the renewal to negotiate improved conditions.
note: renew = 갱신하다. leverage = 활용하다

korean: 상대방이 최후통첩을 보내왔어
english: The other side sent us an ultimatum.
alt: They gave us a take-it-or-leave-it offer.
note: ultimatum = 최후통첩. take-it-or-leave-it = 받아들이든 말든

korean: 이번 입찰에서 가격 경쟁력이 핵심이야
english: Price competitiveness is key in this bid.
alt: We need to come in with an aggressive price to win the tender.
note: price competitiveness = 가격 경쟁력. tender = 입찰

korean: 중재 없이는 합의가 어려울 것 같아
english: I don't think we can reach an agreement without mediation.
alt: We might need a third party to help settle this.
note: mediation = 중재. third party = 제3자. settle = 해결하다

korean: 클라이언트가 할인을 요구하고 있어
english: The client is asking for a discount.
alt: They want us to come down on the price.
note: discount = 할인. come down on price = 가격을 낮추다

korean: 계약 기간을 2년에서 3년으로 늘리면 단가를 낮춰줄 수 있어
english: If we extend the contract from 2 to 3 years, we can lower the unit price.
alt: A longer commitment gets you a better per-unit rate.
note: unit price = 단가. commitment = 약정

korean: 지적 재산권 조항은 꼭 확인해야 해
english: Make sure to check the intellectual property clause.
alt: The IP clause needs careful review.
note: intellectual property (IP) = 지적 재산권. clause = 조항

korean: 수의 계약으로 진행 가능한지 알아봐줘
english: Can you check if we can proceed with a sole-source contract?
alt: See if this qualifies for a direct award without competitive bidding.
note: sole-source contract = 수의 계약. direct award = 수의 계약(미국식)

korean: 결제 조건을 선불에서 후불로 바꿀 수 있을까?
english: Can we change the payment terms from upfront to net payment?
alt: Is it possible to switch from prepayment to deferred billing?
note: upfront = 선불. net payment / deferred billing = 후불

korean: 팀빌딩으로 방탈출 어때? 협동심 기를 수 있잖아
english: How about an escape room for team building? It builds cooperation.
alt: An escape room would be a fun way to strengthen team dynamics.
note: escape room = 방탈출. team dynamics = 팀 역학

korean: 팀 내에서 갈등이 생기면 빨리 해결해야 해
english: Conflicts within the team should be resolved quickly.
alt: Don't let internal tensions simmer. Address them early.
note: conflicts = 갈등. simmer = 속으로 끓다. address = 다루다

korean: 서로 업무를 크로스 트레이닝하면 백업이 쉬워져
english: Cross-training each other's tasks makes backup coverage easier.
alt: If we learn each other's roles we'll have better redundancy.
note: cross-training = 교차 교육. redundancy = 대체 인력/여유

korean: 팀 목표와 개인 목표를 연결시켜야 동기부여가 돼
english: Aligning team goals with individual goals boosts motivation.
alt: People are more motivated when their personal objectives tie into the team's.
note: align = 연결시키다. motivation = 동기부여

korean: 리모트 팀이라 팀 본딩이 어려워 온라인 활동 해볼까?
english: It's hard to bond as a remote team. Should we try online activities?
alt: Being a distributed team makes bonding tough. Virtual events might help.
note: remote / distributed team = 원격팀. bonding = 유대감 형성

korean: 승진 경쟁 때문에 팀 분위기가 살벌해
english: The promotion race is making the team atmosphere tense.
alt: Competition for promotions is creating tension within the team.
note: promotion race = 승진 경쟁. tense = 긴장된

korean: 이번 프로젝트는 애자일 방식으로 진행하자
english: Let's run this project using an agile approach.
alt: We're going agile for this one.
note: agile = 애자일 (반복적/유연한 개발 방식)

korean: 사용자 페르소나를 먼저 정의하고 기획하자
english: Let's define the user personas first before planning.
alt: Start with the target user profiles then build the plan around them.
note: user persona = 사용자 페르소나. user profile = 사용자 프로필

korean: POC 먼저 해보고 본격적으로 진행할지 결정하자
english: Let's do a POC first and then decide whether to go full scale.
alt: Run a proof of concept before committing to the full project.
note: POC = Proof of Concept = 개념 증명

korean: 시장 진입 전략을 좀 더 구체화해야 해
english: We need to flesh out the go-to-market strategy.
alt: The market entry plan needs more detail.
note: go-to-market (GTM) strategy = 시장 진입 전략. flesh out = 구체화하다

korean: 업무 스트레스로 잠을 못 자겠어
english: Work stress is keeping me up at night.
alt: I can't sleep because of work-related anxiety.
note: work stress = 업무 스트레스. anxiety = 불안

korean: 동료가 자꾸 내 공을 가로채
english: My coworker keeps taking credit for my work.
alt: Someone on the team keeps stealing the spotlight for things I did.
note: take credit = 공을 가로채다. steal the spotlight = 주목을 가로채다

korean: 상사한테 건의하고 싶은 게 있는데 어떻게 말하지?
english: I want to suggest something to my boss but I'm not sure how to bring it up.
alt: How do I tactfully bring up a suggestion to management?
note: suggest = 건의하다. tactfully = 요령 있게

korean: 업무 메일 보낼 때 Dear로 시작해야 해?
english: Do I have to start work emails with Dear?
alt: Is it necessary to use Dear in professional emails?
note: Dear = 이메일/편지 인사말. 격식 표현

korean: Best regards랑 Kind regards 차이가 뭐야?
english: What's the difference between Best regards and Kind regards?
alt: Is there a nuance between Best regards and Kind regards?
note: Best/Kind regards = 이메일 맺음말. 격식 정도 차이

korean: 이 일 처리하느라 다른 일 손을 못 대고 있어
english: I've been tied up with this task and haven't been able to touch anything else.
alt: This project has consumed all my bandwidth. Everything else is on hold.
note: tied up = 묶여있는. bandwidth = 여력

korean: 이 업무 흐름 좀 정리해줘 누가 뭘 하는지 모르겠어
english: Can you map out the workflow? I'm confused about who does what.
alt: We need a clear process flow. Roles and responsibilities are unclear.
note: workflow = 업무 흐름. process flow = 프로세스 흐름

korean: 사내 정치에 휘말리고 싶지 않아
english: I don't want to get caught up in office politics.
alt: I'd rather stay out of the workplace drama.
note: office politics = 사내 정치. workplace drama = 직장 내 갈등

korean: 이 일은 긴급하지만 중요하지는 않아 구분해서 해야 해
english: This is urgent but not important. We need to distinguish between the two.
alt: Don't confuse urgency with importance. They're different.
note: urgent = 긴급한. important = 중요한. distinguish = 구분하다

korean: 업무 인수받았는데 전임자가 기록을 안 남겨놔서 막막해
english: I took over the work but the previous person left no documentation. I'm lost.
alt: The predecessor didn't leave any notes. I'm starting from scratch.
note: predecessor = 전임자. from scratch = 처음부터

korean: OT 없이 정시 퇴근하는 게 목표야
english: My goal is to leave on time without any overtime.
alt: I'm aiming for a clean exit at 6 every day.
note: on time = 정시에. clean exit = 깔끔한 퇴근

korean: 메일 한 통에 안건은 하나만 넣어
english: Keep each email to one topic.
alt: One email per subject keeps things organized.
note: one topic per email = 안건 하나씩 (비즈니스 매너)

korean: 회신 기한을 명시해주면 좋겠어
english: It would help if you specified a reply deadline.
alt: Please include a response deadline in your emails.
note: reply deadline = 회신 기한. specify = 명시하다

korean: To whom it may concern은 언제 써?
english: When do you use To whom it may concern?
alt: Is To whom it may concern still appropriate in modern emails?
note: To whom it may concern = 관계자께 (수신자 불명 시)

korean: 이메일로 감사 표현할 때 Thank you for your prompt reply가 좋아
english: When thanking via email use Thank you for your prompt reply.
alt: A quick Thank you for your quick response goes a long way.
note: prompt reply = 빠른 답변. goes a long way = 효과적이다

korean: 새 프로젝트 시작할 때 킥오프 문서 만들어야 해
english: We need to create a kickoff document when starting a new project.
alt: A project charter should be the first thing we put together.
note: kickoff document / project charter = 프로젝트 착수 문서

korean: 이해관계자 분석 먼저 하고 기획 들어가자
english: Let's do a stakeholder analysis before we start planning.
alt: Map out the stakeholders first then move into the planning phase.
note: stakeholder analysis = 이해관계자 분석

korean: 유저 리서치 결과가 나왔어 공유할게
english: The user research results are in. I'll share them.
alt: We've got the UX research findings. Let me distribute them.
note: user research = 유저 리서치. UX = User Experience

korean: 로드맵을 분기별로 나눠서 관리하자
english: Let's break the roadmap down by quarter.
alt: Organize the roadmap into quarterly milestones.
