note: roadmap = 로드맵. quarterly milestones = 분기별 마일스톤

korean: 이 기능은 다음 버전에서 반영하자 우선순위가 낮아
english: This feature goes in the next release. It's low priority.
alt: Let's backlog this feature for a future iteration.
note: backlog = 보류하다. iteration = 반복/버전

korean: 발표 자료 디자인이 좀 밋밋해 비주얼을 살려야 해
english: The presentation design is a bit bland. We need to make it more visual.
alt: The deck needs more visual punch. It looks too plain.
note: bland = 밋밋한. visual punch = 시각적 임팩트

korean: 데이터 기반으로 발표해야 설득력이 있어
english: Present with data backing your points. It's more convincing.
alt: Data-driven presentations carry more weight.
note: data-driven = 데이터 기반. carry weight = 설득력이 있다

korean: 발표 중간에 동영상 틀어도 돼?
english: Is it okay to play a video in the middle of the presentation?
alt: Can I embed a video clip in the deck?
note: embed = 삽입하다. video clip = 동영상 클립

korean: 발표할 때 아이 컨택을 유지하는 게 중요해
english: Maintaining eye contact during your presentation is important.
alt: Make sure to look at your audience not just the slides.
note: eye contact = 아이 컨택/눈 맞춤

korean: Q&A 세션에서 답 모르면 솔직하게 모른다고 해
english: If you don't know the answer during Q&A just say so honestly.
alt: It's okay to say you'll follow up later if you're unsure.
note: Q&A = 질의응답. follow up = 나중에 답변하다

korean: 인사고과 면담 스크립트 좀 준비해야겠어
english: I should prepare a script for my performance review meeting.
alt: Let me draft some talking points for my evaluation discussion.
note: script = 스크립트. talking points = 요점

korean: 자기 평가를 너무 겸손하게 쓰면 손해야
english: Being too modest in your self-assessment works against you.
alt: Don't undersell yourself on the self-evaluation.
note: modest = 겸손한. undersell = 과소평가하다

korean: 핵심 성과를 수치로 증명해야 해
english: You need to prove your key achievements with numbers.
alt: Back up your accomplishments with quantifiable results.
note: quantifiable = 수치화 가능한. accomplishments = 성과

korean: 성과 목표를 SMART하게 설정해야 한다잖아
english: Performance goals should be set using the SMART framework.
alt: Make your goals Specific Measurable Achievable Relevant and Time-bound.
note: SMART = 구체적/측정가능/달성가능/관련성/기한 (목표 설정 기법)

korean: 직속 상관의 평가가 가장 비중이 크지?
english: The direct supervisor's evaluation carries the most weight right?
alt: Isn't the manager's assessment the biggest factor in the review?
note: direct supervisor = 직속 상관. carries weight = 비중이 크다

korean: 이번 워크숍에서 팀 비전을 재정립하자
english: Let's redefine the team vision at this workshop.
alt: This workshop should be about realigning our team's direction.
note: redefine = 재정립하다. realign = 재조정하다

korean: 서번트 리더십이 요즘 트렌드야
english: Servant leadership is the current trend.
alt: The leadership style that's in right now is servant leadership.
note: servant leadership = 서번트 리더십 (섬기는 리더십)

korean: 리더로서 약점을 인정하는 것도 용기야
english: Admitting your weaknesses as a leader takes courage.
alt: Showing vulnerability in leadership actually builds trust.
note: vulnerability = 취약성. builds trust = 신뢰를 쌓다

korean: 코칭 스타일로 팀원을 이끌어봐
english: Try coaching your team members instead of directing them.
alt: Lead through coaching rather than command and control.
note: coaching = 코칭. command and control = 지시와 통제

korean: 후임 리더를 키우는 것도 리더의 역할이야
english: Developing future leaders is also part of a leader's job.
alt: Succession planning should be on every leader's mind.
note: succession planning = 후계자 양성 계획

korean: 복장 자유인데 고객 미팅 있는 날은 좀 차려입어
english: We have a casual dress code but dress up on client meeting days.
alt: Feel free to dress down except when clients are in.
note: dress up = 차려입다. dress down = 편하게 입다

korean: 회사에서 정치적으로 올바른 표현을 써야 해
english: You should use politically correct language at work.
alt: Be mindful of inclusive language in the workplace.
note: politically correct = 정치적으로 올바른. inclusive language = 포용적 언어

korean: 우리 회사는 실패를 용인하는 문화야
english: Our company has a culture that tolerates failure.
alt: We embrace a fail-fast mentality here.
note: tolerate failure = 실패를 용인하다. fail-fast = 빨리 실패하고 배우는

korean: 사내 동아리 활동이 활발해서 좋아
english: I like that the company clubs are really active.
alt: The internal hobby groups are a great way to connect with colleagues.
note: company clubs = 사내 동아리. connect = 교류하다

korean: 다양성과 포용성을 중시하는 회사 문화가 좋아
english: I appreciate the company's focus on diversity and inclusion.
alt: The D&I initiatives here are genuinely meaningful.
note: diversity and inclusion (D&I) = 다양성과 포용성

korean: 재택근무 모니터 듀얼로 쓰니까 효율이 올라가더라
english: Using dual monitors for WFH really boosted my efficiency.
alt: A second screen makes a huge difference when working from home.
note: dual monitors = 듀얼 모니터. boost efficiency = 효율을 높이다

korean: 재택근무할 때 출퇴근 시간을 루틴으로 정해놔
english: Set fixed start and end times for your remote work routine.
alt: Having a structured schedule helps maintain work-life boundaries at home.
note: fixed times = 고정 시간. boundaries = 경계

korean: 코워킹 스페이스 비용 회사에서 지원해줘?
english: Does the company cover coworking space costs?
alt: Is there a reimbursement for using a shared office space?
note: coworking space = 공유 오피스. reimbursement = 환급

korean: 재택근무 환경 점검 체크리스트가 있어?
english: Is there a checklist for setting up a proper WFH environment?
alt: Does HR have a home office setup guide?
note: checklist = 점검표. home office setup = 재택 환경 구성

korean: 재택근무 날에도 회의가 줄지 않아서 힘들어
english: Even on WFH days the meeting load doesn't decrease.
alt: Remote days are just as meeting-heavy as office days.
note: meeting load = 회의 부담. meeting-heavy = 회의가 많은

korean: 야근할 때 효율적으로 하려면 목표를 정해놓고 해
english: To be efficient during overtime set clear goals beforehand.
alt: Don't just stay late aimlessly. Know exactly what you need to finish.
note: efficient = 효율적인. aimlessly = 목적 없이

korean: 야근 신청서 제출해야 수당 받을 수 있어
english: You need to submit an overtime request form to get paid for it.
alt: File the OT request or you won't get compensated.
note: overtime request = 야근 신청. compensated = 보상받는

korean: 주말 근무까지 하면 진짜 번아웃 온다
english: Working weekends on top of everything will definitely lead to burnout.
alt: If we add weekend work to the mix burnout is inevitable.
note: weekend work = 주말 근무. burnout = 번아웃

korean: 야근하는데 에어컨이 꺼져서 너무 덥다
english: The AC turned off during overtime and it's so hot.
alt: They shut down the climate control after hours. It's unbearable.
note: AC = air conditioning. climate control = 냉난방. unbearable = 견딜 수 없는

korean: 야근해서 끝내봤자 또 새 일이 들어와
english: Even if I finish by working late new tasks just pile on.
alt: Overtime is pointless when there's always more work waiting.
note: pile on = 쌓이다. pointless = 무의미한

korean: 점심 먹고 낮잠 잘 수 있는 공간이 있으면 좋겠어
english: I wish there were a nap room for post-lunch rest.
alt: Having a quiet space for a power nap after lunch would be amazing.
note: nap room = 수면실. power nap = 짧은 낮잠

korean: 점심시간에 온라인 강의 들어도 될까?
english: Is it okay to take an online course during lunch?
alt: Can I use my lunch break for personal learning?
note: online course = 온라인 강의. personal learning = 개인 학습

korean: 구내식당이 없어서 매일 밖에서 사 먹어야 해
english: There's no cafeteria so I have to eat out every day.
alt: Without an on-site canteen eating out daily gets expensive.
note: cafeteria / canteen = 구내식당. eat out = 외식하다

korean: 점심시간에 회의 잡지 마 밥 먹을 시간은 줘야지
english: Don't schedule meetings during lunch. People need to eat.
alt: Lunch hour should be sacred. No meetings during that time.
note: sacred = 신성한 (여기서는 지켜야 할)

korean: 오늘 점심 밀키트로 해결할까?
english: Should we just do a meal kit for lunch today?
alt: Let's grab a quick meal kit and eat at our desks.
note: meal kit = 밀키트/간편식

korean: 복지 포인트 사용처가 제한적이라 아쉬워
english: The welfare points have limited use, which is disappointing.
alt: I wish the benefits credit could be used at more places.
note: welfare points = 복지 포인트. limited use = 사용처 제한

korean: 사내 심리 상담 서비스가 있어 이용해봐
english: The company offers an employee counseling service. Try it out.
alt: There's an EAP available if you need to talk to someone.
note: EAP = Employee Assistance Program = 직원 지원 프로그램

korean: 통신비 지원 받고 있어?
english: Are you getting the communication expense benefit?
alt: Does your plan include a phone or internet allowance?
note: communication expense = 통신비. allowance = 지원금

korean: 유연근무제 덕분에 아이 등원시키고 출근할 수 있어
english: Thanks to flexible hours I can drop my kid off at school before work.
alt: Flex time lets me handle the school run before heading to the office.
note: flexible hours = 유연근무. school run = 등하교 도우미

korean: 사내 카페에서 커피가 무료라서 좋아
english: I love that the in-house cafe offers free coffee.
alt: Free coffee at the office cafe is such a nice perk.
note: in-house cafe = 사내 카페. perk = 특전

korean: 인사 담당자가 직원 고충 처리를 해줘야 해
english: HR should handle employee grievance management.
alt: It's HR's job to address workplace complaints.
note: grievance management = 고충 처리. complaints = 불만

korean: 근로계약서 내용 꼼꼼히 읽어봤어?
english: Did you read the employment contract thoroughly?
alt: Make sure to go through every clause of your work contract.
note: employment contract = 근로계약서. clause = 조항

korean: 산업재해 보험 적용 범위가 궁금해
english: I'm curious about what the industrial accident insurance covers.
alt: What does workers' compensation cover exactly?
note: workers' compensation = 산재보험. coverage = 적용 범위

korean: 퇴직연금 DB형이야 DC형이야?
english: Is the retirement pension a defined benefit or defined contribution plan?
alt: Are we on a DB or DC retirement plan?
note: DB = Defined Benefit. DC = Defined Contribution = 확정급여/확정기여

korean: 직장 내 괴롭힘 신고 절차가 어떻게 돼?
english: What's the process for reporting workplace harassment?
alt: How do I file a workplace bullying complaint?
note: workplace harassment = 직장 내 괴롭힘. bullying = 괴롭힘

korean: 마감 전에 버퍼 시간을 두는 게 중요해
english: It's important to build in buffer time before deadlines.
alt: Always pad the schedule with some slack before the due date.
note: buffer time = 여유 시간. pad = 여유를 두다. slack = 여유

korean: 이번 마감 못 맞추면 페널티가 있어?
english: Are there penalties for missing this deadline?
alt: What happens if we don't deliver on time?
note: penalties = 페널티. deliver on time = 제때 납품하다

korean: 마감 스프린트 들어갔어 다른 일 하지 마
english: We're in a deadline sprint. Drop everything else.
alt: Focus mode is on. Nothing else until this is shipped.
note: sprint = 스프린트/전력질주. shipped = 완료/출시된

korean: 데드라인을 역산해서 중간 마일스톤을 잡자
english: Let's work backwards from the deadline to set interim milestones.
alt: Reverse-engineer the timeline starting from the due date.
note: work backwards = 역산하다. interim milestones = 중간 마일스톤

korean: 마감 당일에 수정 요청 들어오면 정말 난감해
english: Getting revision requests on deadline day is the worst.
alt: Last-minute change requests on the due date are a nightmare.
note: revision requests = 수정 요청. nightmare = 악몽

korean: 발표 때 긴장되면 청중 한 명한 명 눈을 맞춰봐
english: If you're nervous during the presentation try making eye contact one person at a time.
alt: Focus on individual faces in the audience to calm your nerves.
note: audience = 청중. calm nerves = 긴장을 풀다

korean: 발표 자료는 3초 안에 메시지가 전달되어야 해
english: Each slide should convey its message within three seconds.
alt: The audience should get the point of each slide at a glance.
note: convey = 전달하다. at a glance = 한눈에

korean: 클리커 가져왔어? 발표할 때 필요해
english: Did you bring the clicker? You'll need it for the presentation.
alt: Make sure you have a presenter remote for the talk.
note: clicker / presenter remote = 발표용 리모컨

korean: 이 그래프 출처를 슬라이드에 명시해야 해
english: You need to cite the source of this graph on the slide.
alt: Include the data source on the chart for credibility.
note: cite = 인용하다. credibility = 신뢰성

korean: 발표 마무리를 임팩트 있게 해야 기억에 남아
english: End the presentation with impact so it sticks with people.
alt: A strong closing leaves a lasting impression.
note: impact = 임팩트. lasting impression = 오래 남는 인상

korean: 연봉 외에 사이닝 보너스도 협상할 수 있어?
english: Can I negotiate a signing bonus on top of the salary?
alt: Is a sign-on bonus negotiable along with the base pay?
note: signing bonus = 사이닝 보너스. on top of = ~에 더해서

korean: 업계 평균보다 낮으면 협상 카드로 쓸 수 있어
english: If it's below industry average you can use that as leverage.
alt: Being underpaid relative to the market gives you negotiating power.
note: industry average = 업계 평균. leverage = 협상력

korean: 연봉 인상 요청 시 타이밍이 중요해
english: Timing is crucial when asking for a raise.
alt: Pick the right moment to bring up your salary expectations.
note: timing = 타이밍. bring up = 꺼내다

korean: 경쟁사 오퍼를 카운터 오퍼 용도로 쓸 수 있을까?
english: Can I use a competing offer as leverage for a counteroffer?
alt: Would showing another company's offer help me negotiate here?
note: competing offer = 경쟁사 제안. counteroffer = 역제안

korean: 총 보상 패키지를 기본급만으로 판단하면 안 돼
english: Don't judge the total compensation package by base salary alone.
alt: Look at the full picture — base plus bonuses plus benefits.
note: total compensation = 총 보상. base salary = 기본급

korean: 업무 매뉴얼이 있으면 신입이 적응하기 쉬워
english: Having a work manual makes it easier for new hires to adjust.
alt: An onboarding playbook really accelerates the learning curve.
note: work manual = 업무 매뉴얼. playbook = 지침서. learning curve = 학습 곡선

korean: 사내 위키에 정보가 잘 정리되어 있어?
english: Is the information well-organized on the internal wiki?
alt: How up-to-date is the company knowledge base?
note: internal wiki = 사내 위키. knowledge base = 지식 베이스

korean: 이 업무 매뉴얼 최신 버전이 맞아?
english: Is this the latest version of the work manual?
alt: Make sure you're looking at the most current documentation.
note: latest version = 최신 버전. documentation = 문서

korean: 회의 노트 공유 문서에 실시간으로 적어줘
english: Take meeting notes in the shared doc in real time.
alt: Let's do live note-taking in the collaborative document.
note: real time = 실시간. collaborative = 협업용

korean: 화상회의 전에 오디오 테스트 해봐
english: Test your audio before the video call.
alt: Do a quick sound check before the meeting starts.
note: audio test / sound check = 오디오 테스트

korean: 이번 분기 회고에서 개선 사항 세 가지를 뽑자
english: Let's identify three improvement areas from this quarter's retrospective.
alt: Pick the top three takeaways from the quarterly retro for action items.
note: improvement areas = 개선 사항. takeaways = 핵심 요점

korean: 회의 초대에 목적과 안건을 명시해줘
english: Include the purpose and agenda in the meeting invite.
alt: Always state the meeting objective in the calendar invitation.
note: meeting invite = 회의 초대. objective = 목적

korean: 우리 팀 업무 가시성을 높여야 해
english: We need to increase the visibility of our team's work.
alt: Our team needs to be better at showcasing what we do.
note: visibility = 가시성. showcase = 보여주다

korean: 팀 규칙을 정해놓으면 협업이 수월해져
english: Setting team norms makes collaboration smoother.
alt: Having ground rules established upfront prevents a lot of friction.
note: team norms / ground rules = 팀 규칙. friction = 마찰

korean: 번아웃 방지하려면 업무 경계를 확실히 해야 해
english: To prevent burnout you need to set clear work boundaries.
alt: Protecting your personal time is essential to avoiding burnout.
note: boundaries = 경계. burnout = 번아웃

korean: 상사가 무리한 요구를 할 때 어떻게 거절해야 하지?
english: How do I push back on unreasonable requests from my boss?
alt: What's the best way to say no to your manager diplomatically?
note: push back = 거절하다. diplomatically = 외교적으로

korean: 이직 면접 볼 때 현 직장 욕하면 안 돼
english: Don't badmouth your current employer during a job interview.
alt: Speaking negatively about your present company is a major red flag.
note: badmouth = 욕하다/험담하다. red flag = 경고 신호

korean: 면접에서 팀워크 경험 예시를 준비해둬
english: Prepare an example of teamwork experience for the interview.
alt: Have a STAR-format story ready about collaboration.
note: STAR = Situation Task Action Result (면접 답변 기법)

korean: 면접 대기실에서 너무 긴장하지 마 자연스럽게 대화해
english: Don't be too tense in the waiting room. Just chat naturally.
alt: Stay relaxed before you go in. Natural confidence is key.
note: tense = 긴장한. natural confidence = 자연스러운 자신감

korean: 면접 후 감사 이메일에 면접에서 논의한 내용을 언급해
english: Reference what you discussed in the interview in your thank-you email.
alt: Mention specific topics from the conversation in your follow-up note.
note: reference = 언급하다. follow-up note = 후속 이메일

korean: 직급별 급여 밴드가 공개되면 공정성이 높아져
english: Transparency in salary bands by level improves fairness.
alt: Pay transparency fosters a sense of equity among employees.
note: salary bands = 급여 구간. pay transparency = 급여 투명성

korean: 성과급은 개인 성과랑 팀 성과 둘 다 반영돼?
english: Are performance bonuses based on both individual and team results?
alt: Does the variable pay factor in personal and team metrics?
note: variable pay = 변동 급여. factor in = 반영하다

korean: RSU 베스팅 스케줄이 어떻게 돼?
english: What's the RSU vesting schedule?
alt: How are the restricted stock units distributed over time?
note: RSU = Restricted Stock Unit = 양도제한부 주식. vesting schedule = 베스팅 일정

korean: 계약직에서 정규직 전환 시 연봉이 바뀌나?
english: Does the salary change when converting from contract to permanent?
alt: Is there a pay adjustment when I transition to full-time?
note: contract = 계약직. permanent / full-time = 정규직

korean: 퇴직금 중간정산 가능한 거 알고 있었어?
english: Did you know you can request an interim settlement of severance?
alt: You can get a partial severance payout before you leave under certain conditions.
note: interim settlement = 중간정산. partial payout = 부분 지급

korean: 승진 후 첫 리더 경험이라 부담스러워
english: This is my first leadership role after the promotion and it's daunting.
alt: I just got promoted to a lead position and the pressure is real.
note: daunting = 부담스러운. lead position = 리더 직위

korean: 동기 중에 가장 먼저 승진해서 어색해
english: I was the first in my cohort to get promoted so it's a bit awkward.
alt: Being promoted before my peers makes things a little weird.
note: cohort = 동기. peers = 동료

korean: 승진 심사에 영어 발표가 포함돼 있어
english: The promotion review includes an English presentation.
alt: There's an English presentation component in the promotion assessment.
note: promotion review = 승진 심사. component = 구성 요소

korean: 관리직 승진보다 전문직 트랙이 더 맞는 것 같아
english: I think the specialist track suits me better than management.
alt: I'd rather go the individual contributor route than management.
note: specialist track = 전문직 트랙. individual contributor (IC) = 개인 기여자

korean: 승진하면 팀을 직접 꾸려야 하는데 준비가 됐을까?
english: If I get promoted I'll have to build my own team. Am I ready?
alt: Leading a team from scratch is a big step. I'm not sure I'm prepared.
note: build a team = 팀을 꾸리다. from scratch = 처음부터

korean: 퇴사 결심했어 더 이상 미루지 않을 거야
english: I've made up my mind to resign. No more procrastinating.
alt: I've decided to leave. I'm done going back and forth.
note: make up my mind = 결심하다. procrastinate = 미루다

korean: 마지막 근무일에 짐 정리하려면 시간이 좀 필요해
english: I need some time on my last day to pack up my things.
alt: Give me a bit of time to clear out my desk on my final day.
note: pack up = 짐을 싸다. clear out = 정리하다

korean: 퇴사 후에도 전 동료들과 네트워크를 유지해
english: Keep in touch with former colleagues even after you leave.
alt: Maintain your professional network with ex-coworkers. It pays off.
note: network = 네트워크/인맥. ex-coworkers = 전 동료

korean: 퇴사 사유를 개인 사정이라고만 해도 돼?
english: Can I just say it's for personal reasons?
alt: Is citing personal reasons enough or do they need specifics?
note: personal reasons = 개인 사정. specifics = 구체적 사유

korean: 권고사직이랑 자발적 퇴사는 차이가 커
english: There's a big difference between being asked to resign and quitting voluntarily.
alt: Voluntary resignation and involuntary separation have very different implications.
note: voluntary = 자발적. involuntary separation = 권고사직/비자발적 퇴직

korean: 입사할 때 받는 웰컴 키트에 뭐가 들어 있어?
english: What's in the welcome kit you get when you join?
alt: What does the new hire swag bag include?
note: welcome kit = 웰컴 키트. swag bag = 기념품 가방

korean: 첫 주에는 주로 시스템 세팅이랑 교육이야
english: The first week is mostly system setup and training.
alt: Expect your first week to be all about onboarding and getting set up.
note: system setup = 시스템 세팅. onboarding = 입사 교육

korean: 입사 전에 미리 읽어볼 자료가 있어?
english: Are there any materials I should read before starting?
alt: Is there a pre-boarding reading list or anything to review?
note: pre-boarding = 입사 전 준비. reading list = 읽어볼 목록

korean: 입사 후 멘토링 프로그램에 배정돼
english: After joining you'll be assigned to a mentoring program.
alt: New hires are paired with a mentor during onboarding.
note: mentoring program = 멘토링 프로그램. paired with = 짝지어진

korean: 첫 달은 많이 물어보는 게 당연하니까 부담 갖지 마
english: It's totally normal to ask lots of questions in your first month. Don't stress.
alt: Asking questions early on is expected. Don't feel bad about it.
note: don't stress = 부담 갖지 마. expected = 당연한

korean: 피드백 줄 때 감정을 배제하고 사실만 말해
english: When giving feedback stick to facts and leave emotions out of it.
alt: Keep feedback objective and data-driven. Emotions cloud the message.
note: objective = 객관적인. cloud = 흐리게 하다

korean: 동료한테 칭찬 피드백을 공개적으로 해주면 사기가 올라가
english: Publicly praising a colleague boosts team morale.
alt: Public recognition for good work goes a long way for motivation.
note: public recognition = 공개 칭찬. morale = 사기

korean: 지속적인 피드백이 연 1회 평가보다 효과적이야
english: Continuous feedback is more effective than an annual review.
alt: Ongoing feedback loops outperform yearly evaluations.
note: continuous = 지속적인. feedback loop = 피드백 순환

korean: 피드백을 메일로 받으면 기록이 남아서 좋아
english: Getting feedback in writing via email is nice because it creates a record.
alt: Written feedback gives you something to refer back to.
note: in writing = 서면으로. refer back to = 나중에 참고하다

korean: 익명 피드백이라도 솔직하게 쓰는 게 좋아
english: Even anonymous feedback should be written honestly.
alt: Take anonymous surveys seriously and be truthful.
note: anonymous = 익명의. truthful = 솔직한

korean: 출장 후에 명함 정리해놓는 게 좋아
english: Organize the business cards you collected after the trip.
alt: Sort through your contacts from the trip while they're fresh.
note: business cards = 명함. sort through = 정리하다

korean: 출장 시 회사 폰이랑 개인 폰 분리해서 써
english: Use separate phones for work and personal during business trips.
alt: Keep your work and personal devices separate on trips.
note: separate = 분리된. devices = 기기

korean: 출장 간 동안 대리 업무자를 지정해놔
english: Designate someone to cover your duties while you're on the trip.
alt: Assign a backup before you leave for the business trip.
note: designate = 지정하다. backup = 대리인

korean: 해외 거래처 방문 시 현지 비즈니스 매너를 숙지해
english: Familiarize yourself with local business etiquette when visiting overseas clients.
alt: Know the cultural norms before meeting international partners.
note: business etiquette = 비즈니스 매너. cultural norms = 문화적 관행

korean: 거래처에서 연말 인사 선물 보내왔어
english: The business partner sent us a year-end gift.
alt: We received a holiday present from the vendor.
note: year-end gift = 연말 선물. vendor = 거래처

korean: 프로젝트 예산안을 경영진에게 프레젠테이션해야 해
english: I need to present the project budget proposal to the executive team.
alt: The budget pitch to leadership is coming up.
note: budget proposal = 예산안. pitch = 제안 발표

korean: 발표 시 laser pointer 대신 화면 내 하이라이트를 써
english: Use on-screen highlights instead of a laser pointer.
alt: Try the digital pointer feature instead of a physical laser.
note: laser pointer = 레이저 포인터. on-screen = 화면 내

korean: 이 슬라이드 애니메이션이 과해 심플하게 바꿔
english: The animation on this slide is too much. Keep it simple.
alt: Tone down the slide transitions. Less is more.
note: animation = 애니메이션. less is more = 심플한 게 더 좋다

korean: 인사팀에서 다음 달에 리더십 교육이 있다고 공지했어
english: HR announced a leadership training program next month.
alt: There's a mandatory leadership workshop coming up per HR.
note: leadership training = 리더십 교육. mandatory = 필수

korean: 위기 상황에서 리더의 의사결정이 중요해
english: A leader's decision-making is critical during a crisis.
alt: In a crisis the leader's judgment sets the tone for the team.
note: crisis = 위기. judgment = 판단력. sets the tone = 분위기를 결정하다

korean: 우리 회사 유연근무 종류가 시차출퇴근이랑 재택 두 가지야
english: Our company offers two types of flex work: staggered hours and remote.
alt: The flexible options here are shifted schedules and WFH.
note: staggered hours = 시차 출퇴근. shifted schedules = 교대 일정

korean: 복지 혜택 안내서 어디서 받을 수 있어?
english: Where can I get the benefits guide?
alt: Is the employee benefits handbook available online?
note: benefits guide / handbook = 복지 안내서

korean: 경조금 신청 절차가 어떻게 돼?
english: What's the process for applying for congratulatory or condolence funds?
alt: How do I request the family event allowance?
note: congratulatory / condolence funds = 경조금

korean: 사내 도서 지원금으로 뭐 사면 좋을까?
english: What should I buy with the company book allowance?
alt: Any good recommendations for the reading benefit?
note: book allowance = 도서 지원금. reading benefit = 독서 복지

korean: 올해 워케이션 프로그램에 신청할 수 있어?
english: Can I apply for the workation program this year?
alt: Is the work-from-anywhere program still accepting applicants?
note: workation = 워케이션 (일+휴가). work-from-anywhere = 어디서나 근무

korean: 직장 내 성희롱 예방 교육 이수했어?
english: Have you completed the workplace sexual harassment prevention training?
alt: Did you finish the mandatory anti-harassment course?
note: sexual harassment prevention = 성희롱 예방. mandatory = 필수

korean: 개인정보 보호 교육도 연 1회 필수야
english: Data privacy training is also mandatory once a year.
alt: Annual GDPR/privacy compliance training is required.
note: data privacy = 개인정보 보호. GDPR = 일반 데이터 보호 규정

korean: 인사팀에서 직무 기술서 업데이트하라고 했어
english: HR asked us to update our job descriptions.
alt: They want everyone to refresh their role definitions.
note: job description = 직무 기술서. role definition = 역할 정의

korean: 채용 프로세스가 너무 길어 좋은 인재를 놓쳐
english: The hiring process is too long and we're losing good candidates.
alt: Our slow recruitment pipeline is costing us top talent.
note: recruitment pipeline = 채용 프로세스. top talent = 우수 인재

korean: 온보딩 프로세스를 개선해야 신입 이탈률이 줄어
english: Improving the onboarding process will reduce new hire turnover.
alt: Better onboarding directly impacts early-stage retention.
note: onboarding = 온보딩. turnover = 이직률. retention = 유지율

korean: AI 도구를 업무에 도입하면 생산성이 올라갈까?
english: Would adopting AI tools at work boost productivity?
alt: Do you think AI integration would improve our workflow?
note: AI tools = AI 도구. productivity = 생산성

korean: 업무용 GPT 사용 가이드라인이 있어?
english: Is there a guideline for using GPT at work?
alt: Does the company have a policy on AI-assisted work?
note: guideline = 가이드라인. AI-assisted = AI 보조

korean: 이 데이터를 대시보드로 시각화해줄 수 있어?
english: Can you turn this data into a dashboard?
alt: Could you visualize these metrics in a dashboard format?
note: dashboard = 대시보드. visualize = 시각화하다

korean: 슬랙 알림을 너무 많이 받아서 집중이 안 돼
english: I'm getting too many Slack notifications to focus.
alt: The constant pings on Slack are killing my concentration.
note: notifications = 알림. concentration = 집중력

korean: 포커스 타임을 캘린더에 블록해놔 방해받지 않게
english: Block focus time on your calendar so you won't be interrupted.
alt: Reserve deep work slots on your schedule to protect your concentration.
note: focus time = 집중 시간. deep work = 딥 워크 (방해 없는 집중 업무)

korean: 분기 말에 항상 업무가 몰려서 힘들어
english: Work always piles up at the end of the quarter.
alt: Quarter-end crunch is brutal every single time.
note: quarter-end = 분기 말. crunch = 집중 부하기

korean: 클라이언트가 스코프를 계속 추가하는데 비용은 안 올려줘
english: The client keeps adding scope without increasing the budget.
alt: We're dealing with scope creep and no additional funding.
note: scope creep = 범위 확대. additional funding = 추가 비용

korean: 이거 내 권한 밖이라 결재 올려야 해
english: This is above my pay grade. I need to escalate for approval.
alt: I don't have the authority to approve this. It needs to go up.
note: above my pay grade = 내 권한 밖. escalate = 상위로 올리다

korean: 업무 관련 커뮤니티에 가입해두면 도움이 많이 돼
english: Joining work-related communities can be really helpful.
alt: Professional communities are a great source of knowledge and connections.
note: professional community = 전문 커뮤니티

korean: 이 프로젝트 사후 분석에서 무엇을 배웠어?
english: What did we learn from the post-mortem on this project?
alt: What were the key lessons from the project debrief?
note: post-mortem = 사후 분석. debrief = 사후 보고

korean: 업무 집중도를 높이려면 뽀모도로 기법을 써봐
english: Try the Pomodoro technique to improve your focus.
alt: The 25-minute focused work blocks really help with concentration.
note: Pomodoro technique = 뽀모도로 기법 (25분 집중/5분 휴식)

korean: 컨퍼런스 참가비 회사에서 지원해줘?
english: Does the company cover conference attendance fees?
alt: Can I get my conference ticket expensed?
note: conference = 컨퍼런스. expensed = 경비 처리된

korean: 이 업무 프로세스 좀 간소화할 수 없을까?
english: Can we simplify this work process?
alt: There must be a way to streamline this procedure.
note: simplify / streamline = 간소화하다. procedure = 절차

korean: 업무 이관 문서를 꼼꼼히 작성해줘
english: Write the handover documentation thoroughly.
alt: Make the transition document as detailed as possible.
note: handover documentation = 업무 이관 문서. detailed = 상세한

korean: 사내 해커톤에 참가하면 혁신 아이디어를 낼 수 있어
english: Participating in the company hackathon is a great way to generate innovative ideas.
alt: The internal hackathon is a good chance to showcase creative solutions.
note: hackathon = 해커톤. innovative = 혁신적인

korean: 업무 이메일은 24시간 내로 회신하는 게 매너야
english: It's good etiquette to reply to work emails within 24 hours.
alt: Responding to professional emails within a day is standard business practice.
note: etiquette = 매너/예절. standard practice = 표준 관행

korean: 감사 메일에 구체적으로 뭐가 도움이 됐는지 적어줘
english: In your thank-you email specify exactly what was helpful.
alt: When expressing gratitude via email mention the specific contribution.
note: specify = 명시하다. contribution = 기여

korean: 메일 서두에 바로 용건을 밝히는 게 좋아
english: It's best to state the purpose right at the beginning of the email.
alt: Lead with your ask or main point in the first sentence.
note: state the purpose = 용건을 밝히다. lead with = ~로 시작하다

korean: 장문 이메일 보낼 때는 요약을 맨 위에 넣어
english: When sending a long email put a summary at the top.
alt: For lengthy emails start with a TL;DR at the beginning.
note: summary = 요약. TL;DR = 요약본 (Too Long; Didn't Read)

korean: 부재중 자동 응답에 대리 연락처를 꼭 넣어
english: Always include a backup contact in your out-of-office auto-reply.
alt: Your OOO message should have an alternate point of contact.
note: out-of-office (OOO) = 부재중. backup contact = 대리 연락처

korean: 이번 프로젝트에서 얻은 교훈을 문서화하자
english: Let's document the lessons learned from this project.
alt: We should write up a retrospective report with key takeaways.
note: lessons learned = 교훈. retrospective report = 회고 보고서

korean: 사내 멘토링 프로그램에 멘티로 신청하고 싶어
english: I'd like to sign up as a mentee for the company mentoring program.
alt: Can I apply to be mentored through the internal program?
note: mentee = 멘티. mentoring program = 멘토링 프로그램

korean: 타 부서 업무를 이해해야 협업이 수월해져
english: Understanding other departments' work makes collaboration easier.
alt: Cross-functional awareness improves teamwork across the board.
note: cross-functional awareness = 부서 간 이해. across the board = 전반적으로

korean: 팀 내 심리적 안전감이 성과에 영향을 줘
english: Psychological safety within the team impacts performance.
alt: Teams with high psychological safety tend to perform better.
note: psychological safety = 심리적 안전감. perform = 성과를 내다

korean: 업무 외 자리에서 팀원을 알아가는 것도 중요해
english: Getting to know team members outside of work is also important.
alt: Informal bonding outside office hours strengthens team dynamics.
note: informal bonding = 비공식 교류. team dynamics = 팀 역학

korean: 이 보고서 수정본 보내드릴 테니까 다시 확인 부탁드려요
english: I'll send the revised report so please review it again.
alt: The updated version is coming your way. Another round of review please.
note: revised = 수정된. round of review = 검토 차수

korean: 데이터 분석 결과를 인사이트 위주로 정리해줘
english: Summarize the data analysis results, focusing on insights.
alt: Pull out the key insights from the analytics report.
note: insights = 인사이트/통찰. analytics = 분석

korean: 보고서에 다음 분기 전망도 포함시켜
english: Include the outlook for next quarter in the report.
alt: Add a forward-looking section with Q-next projections.
note: outlook = 전망. projections = 예측

korean: 비즈니스 영어 이메일에서 Please find attached는 좀 구식이야
english: In business emails, Please find attached is a bit outdated.
alt: Modern email style uses I've attached or Here's the file instead.
note: Please find attached = (구식) 첨부 드립니다. outdated = 구식의

korean: 회의에서 내 의견이 무시당하는 느낌이야
english: I feel like my opinions are being ignored in meetings.
alt: It seems like no one values my input during discussions.
note: ignored = 무시당하는. values my input = 내 의견을 존중하다

korean: 회의 전에 사전 자료를 읽어와야 생산적인 토론이 가능해
english: If everyone reads the pre-read materials, the discussion will be more productive.
alt: Showing up unprepared wastes everyone's time. Do the pre-work.
note: pre-read materials = 사전 자료. productive = 생산적인

korean: 이번 상반기 핵심 성과 세 가지를 정리해봐
english: Summarize your top three achievements from the first half.
alt: List your three biggest wins for the H1 review.
note: achievements / wins = 성과. H1 = 상반기

korean: 리더십 역량 개발 계획서를 작성해야 해
english: I need to write a leadership development plan.
alt: Draft a personal leadership growth roadmap.
note: leadership development plan = 리더십 역량 개발 계획

korean: 변화 관리는 리더의 핵심 역량 중 하나야
english: Change management is one of the core competencies of a leader.
alt: Leaders need to be able to guide their teams through transitions.
note: change management = 변화 관리. core competencies = 핵심 역량

korean: 우리 회사는 플랫 조직이라 의사결정이 빨라
english: Our company has a flat structure so decisions are made quickly.
alt: The flat hierarchy here speeds up the decision-making process.
note: flat structure = 수평 조직. hierarchy = 위계 구조

korean: 사무실 리모델링한다는데 좌석 배치가 어떻게 될까?
english: The office is being remodeled. How will the seating be arranged?
alt: With the office renovation I wonder how the floor plan will change.
note: remodel / renovation = 리모델링. floor plan = 평면도/좌석 배치

korean: 스탠딩 데스크 신청할 수 있어?
english: Can I request a standing desk?
alt: Is it possible to get a sit-stand desk?
note: standing desk / sit-stand desk = 스탠딩 데스크

korean: 우리 회사는 브리핑이 매일 아침 있어
english: Our company has a daily morning briefing.
alt: We kick off each day with a morning standup.
note: briefing = 브리핑. standup = 스탠드업 미팅

korean: 인턴 피드백 세션 준비했어?
english: Have you prepared for the intern feedback session?
alt: Is the mid-internship review ready?
note: intern feedback = 인턴 피드백. mid-internship review = 인턴 중간 평가

korean: 업무량 대비 보상이 적절한지 평가해봐야 해
english: We should assess whether the compensation matches the workload.
alt: It's worth evaluating if the pay reflects the actual work demand.
note: compensation = 보상. workload = 업무량. evaluate = 평가하다

korean: 고과 등급에 따라 인센티브 차등이 커
english: The incentive gap between performance grades is significant.
alt: Bonus payouts vary widely depending on your rating.
note: incentive gap = 인센티브 차이. rating = 등급

korean: 사내 공모전에 참가해볼까?
english: Should I enter the internal competition?
alt: The company contest sounds interesting. Want to join?
note: internal competition = 사내 공모전

korean: 오피스 간식이 항상 있어서 소소한 행복이야
english: Having office snacks always available is such a small joy.
alt: The free snacks at the office are an underrated perk.
note: office snacks = 사무실 간식. underrated = 과소평가된

korean: 요즘 업계 동향이 어때? 트렌드 리포트 본 적 있어?
english: What are the latest industry trends? Have you seen any trend reports?
alt: Have you caught up on the latest market intelligence?
note: industry trends = 업계 동향. market intelligence = 시장 정보

korean: 이직할 때 비경쟁 조항 기간이 얼마야?
english: How long is the non-compete period when you leave?
alt: What's the duration of the non-compete clause after resignation?
note: non-compete period = 비경쟁 기간. duration = 기간

korean: 경력 단절 후 복귀하려면 어떻게 준비해야 해?
english: How should I prepare to return to work after a career break?
alt: What's the best way to get back into the workforce after time off?
note: career break = 경력 단절. workforce = 노동 시장

korean: 신입 때 실수해도 괜찮아 그게 배우는 거야
english: It's okay to make mistakes when you're new. That's how you learn.
alt: Rookies are expected to stumble. It's all part of the learning process.
note: rookies = 신입. stumble = 실수하다

korean: 수시 채용이라 빈자리가 나면 바로 공고가 올라가
english: It's rolling recruitment so postings go up as soon as there's an opening.
alt: They hire on a rolling basis whenever a position opens up.
note: rolling recruitment = 수시 채용. posting = 공고

korean: 마감 후에 회고 미팅 꼭 하자 같은 실수 반복 안 하게
english: Let's definitely do a retro after the deadline so we don't repeat mistakes.
alt: A post-deadline review is a must to learn from what went wrong.
note: retro = 회고. post-deadline review = 마감 후 리뷰

korean: 마감에 쫓기다 보면 체크 과정을 건너뛰게 돼 주의해
english: When rushing to meet deadlines, people tend to skip QC steps. Be careful.
alt: Deadline pressure makes us cut corners on quality. Watch out.
note: QC = Quality Check. cut corners = 대충하다

korean: 업무 자동화 스크립트 하나 만들어놓으면 반복 작업이 줄어
english: Building one automation script can eliminate a lot of repetitive work.
alt: A single script can save you hours of manual tasks every week.
note: automation script = 자동화 스크립트. repetitive = 반복적인
